Pak Study MCQs · pages 51-100

Sharpen your Pak Study recall

Tap a card to reveal the explanation. Take it one seal at a time.

500 questions in this set
1

Hazrat _______ was first who learnt to write.

Correct Answer: Idrees (A.S)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Idris (A.S) is considered the first person who learned writing.
  • He is also known for knowledge, wisdom, and early civilization development.
  • He is also associated with astronomy and mathematics.

Additional information:

  • Total major Prophets (Ulul Azm): 5 (Nuh, Ibrahim, Musa, Isa, Muhammad)

Last verified on 27-04-2026

2

Nation of Hazrat Noah (AS) worshiped ___ idols.

Correct Answer: 5
Show explanation
Explanation

The nation of Noah worshiped five idols:

  1. Wadh
  2. Suwa
  3. Yaghuth
  4. Yauq
  5. Nas

Additional information:

  • The duration of the Toofan-e-Noah (Flood of Noah) was around 6 months.
  • The event occurred 2,242 years after Hazrat Adam (AS).
  • About 80 people were with him in the boat.
  • Hazrat Noah (AS) lived for 950 years.
  • He was sent to Iraq.
  • He is called the predecessor, Naji Ullah, and Shaikh ul Ambiya.
  • His title is Abu ul Bashr Sani.
  • After the flood, the city of Khasran was established.

Last verified on 27-04-2026

3

How many verses were revealed in the first Wahi?

Correct Answer: Five
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Number of verses: 5
  • Location of the first wahi: Cave Hira
  • In: 610 AD
  • At the Age of 40 years 6 months
  • Prescriber of wahi in Makkah: Sharjeel-ibn-Hasana Kandi
  • First writer of Wahi in Quraish: Hazrat Zaid bin Sabit (RA)

Last verified on 27-04-2026

4

What was written on seal of the Holy Prophet (PBUH)?

Correct Answer: Allah, Rasool, Muhammad
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The seal of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) had three words.
  • These words were arranged as Allah – Rasool – Muhammad.
  • It was engraved on a silver ring.
  • The seal was used for official letters to kings and rulers.
  • The seal affixed on important letters by the Prophet (SAW) was in the Custody of Hazrat Huzaifa (RA).
  • It started being used around 7 A.H.

Last verified on 27-04-2026

5

What are the third belief in Iman-e-Mufassal?

Correct Answer: The Books of Allah (The Divine Scriptures)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The third belief in Iman-e-Mufassal is faith in the Books of Allah.
  • Muslims believe Allah revealed divine books for guidance.

7 Beliefs of Iman-e-Mufassal)

  1. Faith in Allah
  2. Faith in Angels
  3. Faith in Books
  4. Faith in Prophets
  5. Faith in Day of Judgment
  6. Faith in Taqdir (destiny)
  7. Faith in Life after death

Last verified on 27-04-2026

6

Imam-e-Dar-ul-Hijrat was a title of ______?

Correct Answer: Imam Malik
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The title Imam-e-Dar-ul-Hijrat was given to Imam Malik.
  • Birth → 711 AD (Madinah)
  • Death → 795 AD (Madinah)
  • Book → Al-Muwatta
  • Hadith Count → ~1,720 Hadith
  • Field → Fiqh (Maliki School of Thought)

Last verified on 21-04-2026

7

Who among the following was a Historian, justice, philosopher as well as Politician?

Correct Answer: Abdur Rehman bin Khaldun
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Abdur Rehman bin Khaldun was an Arab sociologist, philosopher, and historian. 
  • Born: May 27, 1332, in Tunis
  • Died: March 17, 1406, in Egypt
  • He made major contributions to historiography, sociology, economics, and demography.
  • His best-known book is the "Muqaddimah" or "Prolegomena" ("Introduction").

Last verified on 27-04-2026

8

The Seal of Holy Prophet (P.B.U.H) was in the custody of?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Huzaifa (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The seal of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) was made of silver.
  • Inscription: “Allah – Rasool – Muhammad” (in three lines).
  • It started being used around 7 A.H for official letters.
  • The seal was kept in custody of Hazrat Huzaifa ibn al-Yaman (R.A).

Last verified on 31-03-2026

9

Which one of the following is included amongst the Ashra Mubashra?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Saad Bin Abi Waqas
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Ashra Mubashra are those Companions of Prophet Hazrat Muhammad (P.B.U.H), who were given Good News in their life that they will be awarded Heaven.

Following is the List with Names of Ashra Mubashra:

  1. Hazrat Abu Bakar (R.A)
  2. Hazrat Umer Farooq (R.A)
  3. Hazrat Usman (R.A)
  4. Hazrat Ali (R.A)
  5. Hazrat Zubair ibn-e-Awam (R.A)
  6. Hazrat Talha (R.A)
  7. Hazrat Abdul Rehman bin-e-Auf (R.A)
  8. Hazrat Abu Obaida bin-al-Jarah (R.A)
  9. Hazrat Saad bin-e-Abi Waqas (R.A)
  10. Hazrat Saeed bin-e-Zaid (R.A)

Last verified on 02-04-2026

10

Who declared Nile River as Sayed Ul Anhar??

Correct Answer: Hazrat Umar (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA) declared the Nile River as Sayed ul Anhar (Chief of Rivers).
  • This happened during his caliphate (634–644 AD).
  • The Nile River was highly valued for its importance to Egypt.
  • Length of Nile River: ~6650 km
  • Origin: Lake Victoria (East-Central Africa)

Last verified on 27-04-2026

11

First census of Islamic world was held in _____ period.

Correct Answer: Hazrat Umar (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The first census in the Islamic world was conducted during the period of Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA).
  • It was done to manage the growing Islamic state efficiently.
  • The census helped in organizing tax (Zakat & Jizya) and military records.
  • It took place during his caliphate (634–644 AD).

Last verified on 27-04-2026
12

The Holy Prophet Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) performed first Friday Prayer (Namaz-e-Juma) in the year?

Correct Answer: 1 A.H
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The first Jumuah prayer was performed by Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) after Hijrah.
  • It took place in the year 1 A.H during the migration to Madinah.
  • The Prophet (PBUH) led the prayer at Banu Salim ibn Awf area.
  • This was the first official Friday congregation in Islam.

Last verified on 25-04-2026

13

What companion of Prophet (PBUH) was awarded with the title of “The Sword of Allah”?

Correct Answer: Khalid bin Waleed (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Khalid ibn al-Walid (R.A) was given the title Saifullah (Sword of Allah).
  • This title was awarded after the Battle of Mu'tah.
  • He showed extraordinary military leadership and saved the Muslim army.
  • Born in 592 AD in Mecca.
  • Died in 642 AD in Homs, Syria.
  • Major Caliphs served: Abu Bakr (RA), Umar (RA)

Last verified on 27-04-2026

14

منافق کی تعریف کیا ہے؟

Correct Answer: جو صرف زبان سے کلمہ پڑھے مگر دل سے ایمان نہ رکھے
Show explanation
Explanation

منافق اس شخص کو کہتے ہیں جو بظاہر اسلام کا دعویٰ کرے (زبان سے کلمہ پڑھے) لیکن دل میں کفر یا دشمنی چھپائے رکھے

منافق کی تین نشانیاں نبی اکرم ﷺ نے فرمایا

إِذَا حَدَّثَ كَذَبَ

جب وہ بات کرے تو جھوٹ بولے۔

وَإِذَا وَعَدَ أَخْلَفَ

جب وہ وعدہ کرے تو وعدہ خلافی کرے۔

وَإِذَا اؤْتُمِنَ خَانَ

جب وہ امانت دی جائے تو خیانت کرے۔


Last updated by NA on 26 April 2026

15

What is charged from Non-Muslims in an Islamic country instead of Ushr?

Correct Answer: Jizya
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Jizya is a tax taken from non-Muslims in an Islamic state.
  • It is charged instead of Ushr/Zakat which Muslims pay.
  • In return, non-Muslims receive protection and state services.
  • It is part of the Islamic financial system.

Last verified on 26-04-2026

16

Who translated the Holy Quran in Urdu for the first time?

Correct Answer: Shah Abdul Qadir and Shah Rafiuddin
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The first Urdu translation of the Holy Quran was done by Shah Abdul Qadir and Shah Rafiuddin.
  • Both were sons of Shah Waliullah.
  • Shah Rafiuddin translated more literally, word-to-word.
  • Shah Abdul Qadir’s translation was simpler and easier to understand.

Additional information

  • First time translated into Punjabi:Tauqir Amaan Khan
  • First time translated into Sindhi:Aakhund Azzizullah
  • First time translated into English:George Sale
  • First time in IndiaSindhi
  • First time in WorldPersian

Last updated by NA on 25- April-2026

17

Who was the first commander of the Islamic Army?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Hamza (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Hazrat Hamza (RA) is regarded as the first commander of the Islamic army.
  • He was the uncle of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
  • He was also known as “Sayyid-ush-Shuhada” (Leader of Martyrs).
  • He led the first Islamic expedition (Sariyyah) in early Madinah period.
  • He was martyred in the Battle of Uhud in 3 AH.

Last verified on 26-04-2026

18

نماز با جماعت کتنے لوگوں سے ہوتی ہے؟

Correct Answer: 2 یا زیادہ افراد
Show explanation
Explanation

نمازِ باجماعت کے لیے کم از کم دو افراد (ایک امام اور ایک مقتدی/پیروکار) کا ہونا ضروری ہے۔ 

اگر دو لوگ ہوں، تو ایک امام بنے گا اور دوسرا مقتدی اس کے دائیں جانب تھوڑا پیچھے کھڑا ہوگا۔
Last updated by NA on 26 April 2026
19

Identify the last ghazwa in which Holy prophet (PBUH) participated?

Correct Answer: Ghazwa Tabuk
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The Expedition of Tabuk, also known as the Expedition of Usra.
  • Muslims forces was 30,000.
  • Start date: October 630 AD
  • Date: October 630 CE (Rajab AH 9)
  • Result: Bloodless battle
  • Location: north to Tabuk, near the Gulf of Aqaba, in present-day northwestern Saudi Arabia
  •  Ghazwa means that war in which our PROPHET (PBUH) took part while in which PROPHET (PBUH) did not took part is known as war.
  • The first ghazaw was ghazawa Al abawa.

Last verified on 16-03-2026

20

To which Sahabi the keys of Jerusalem were handed over first of all?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Umer Farooq (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The keys of Jerusalem were handed over to Hazrat Umar (RA).
  • This event happened after the peaceful conquest of the city in 16 AH (637 AD).
  • The city surrendered without major bloodshed.
  • Hazrat Umar (RA) personally traveled to accept the keys.

Last verified on 26-04-2026

21

Who conquered Persia for the first time?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Saad bin Abi Waqas (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Hazrat Saad bin Abi Waqqas (RA) led the Muslim conquest of Persia.
  • He was the commander in the famous Battle of Qadisiyyah.
  • This battle opened the path to the fall of the Sassanid Empire.
  • The conquest took place during the Caliphate of Hazrat Umar (RA).
  • Battle of Qadisiyyah was the decisive battle against the Persian Empire.
  • The Persian Empire was one of the two superpowers of that era (Byzantine and Persian).

Last verified on 26-04-2026

22

In which night Quran was revealed?

Correct Answer: Lailat-ul-Qadr
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The Qur’an was first revealed on Lailat-ul-Qadr.
  • It is known as the Night of Power or Decree.
  • It is usually sought in the odd nights (21st, 23rd, 25th, 27th, 29th) of Ramadan.
  • It is better than a thousand months in reward.
  • It marks the beginning of revelation to Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

Last verified on 26-04-2026

23

Religious terminology of “Nirwan” is related to?

Correct Answer: Hindu-mat
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Nirwana (Nirvana) is a key concept in Buddhism.
  • It means liberation from suffering and the cycle of rebirth.
  • It is the ultimate spiritual goal in Buddhist teachings.
  • Buddhism was founded by Siddhartha Gautama (Buddha).

Last verified on 26-04-2026

24

The largest Muslim army was in the battle of?

Correct Answer: Tabuk
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The largest Muslim army was gathered in the Battle of Tabuk.
  • It took place in 9 AH (630 AD).
  • Around 30,000 Muslims participated in this expedition.
  • It was against the Byzantine (Roman) forces.
  • It showed strong unity and preparation of the Muslim community.

Last verified on 26-04-2026

25

The Fallen wealth is called?

Correct Answer: Luqta
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Luqta refers to lost or found property in Islamic law.
  • It includes items that are found but belong to someone else.
  • The finder must announce it and try to return it to the owner.

Additional information:

  • Rikaz = buried treasure or hidden wealth found underground.
  • Nafaqa = financial maintenance/support (e.g., for family).

Last verified on 26-04-2026

26

The inheritants that are determined from the deceased’s inheritance?

Correct Answer: Zavil-fra-id
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Zavil-fra-id are the heirs whose shares are fixed in Islamic inheritance.
  • Their portions are clearly mentioned in the Qur’an.
  • They receive inheritance first before other heirs.
  • This system ensures fair and structured distribution of wealth.
  • It is an important part of Islamic law (Faraid).

Last verified on 26-04-2026

27

Which Companion did the Prophet allow speculation while appointing him as Governor of Yemen with the responsibility of collecting Zakat?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Ma’az bin Jabal (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) appointed Hazrat Muadh bin Jabal (RA) as Governor of Yemen.
  • He was given the responsibility of collecting Zakat and administering justice.
  • The Prophet (PBUH) allowed him to use ijtihad (independent reasoning).
  • He was one of the most knowledgeable companions in Islamic law.
  • This shows trust in his understanding of Qur’an and Sunnah.

Last verified on 26-04-2026

28

The Hadith which has two narrators in each class is called?

Correct Answer: Khabr-e-Aziz
Show explanation
Explanation
  • A Hadith narrated by at least two narrators in every level is called Khabr-e-Aziz.
  • It is more reliable than Khabr-e-Gharib (single narrator).
  • It is less widespread than Khabr-e-Mashhoor.
  • Islamic scholars classify Hadith based on number of narrators.
  • This classification helps determine authenticity and strength of Hadith.

Last verified on 26-04-2026
29

In which Surah of Quran are the injunctions regarding Qisas?

Correct Answer: Al-Baqara
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The injunctions of Qisas are mainly mentioned in Surah Al-Baqarah.
  • It is discussed in verses 178–179.
  • Qisas means “law of equal retaliation” in Islamic law.
  • It ensures justice in cases of murder and injury.

Last verified on 26-04-2026

30

Who is called the conqueror of Egypt?

Correct Answer: Amr ibn al-Aas
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Amr ibn al-Aas (RA) is called the conqueror of Egypt.
  • He led the Muslim conquest of Egypt during 640–646 AD.
  • The conquest was carried out under the Rashidun Caliphate.
  • Later, he also served as the governor of Egypt.
  • He was appointed commander by Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) and served under Hazrat Umar (RA).

Last verified on 26-04-2026

31

The plague of Amwas and the severe famine in Arabia occurred in which Caliph’s era?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Umer Farooq (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The Plague of Amwas and severe famine occurred during the caliphate of Hazrat Umar (RA).
  • The plague spread in Syria around 18 AH (639 AD).
  • It caused the death of many صحابہ (Companions).
  • A severe famine also struck Arabia during the same period.
  • The plague is also called “Year of Ashes” due to drought and hardship.

Last verified on 26-04-2026

32

What happened 50 days before the birth of Holy Prophet (PBUH)?

Correct Answer: Incident of Phiel
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The event known as the Incident of the Elephant occurred around 570 CE, just before the Prophet’s (PBUH) birth.
  • It happened when Abraha attacked Makkah with elephants to destroy the Kaaba.
  • Allah destroyed the army by sending birds with stones (Ababil).
  • This event is mentioned in Surah Al-Fil.
  • It is considered a sign of protection for the Kaaba.

Last verified on 26-04-2026

33

Which Christian monk did the Prophet meet on the way to Syria?

Correct Answer: Bahira
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) met a Christian monk named Bahira.
  • This meeting happened during a trade journey to Syria in his youth.
  • Bahira recognized signs of prophethood in him (Muhammad ﷺ).
  • He advised Abu Talib to take care of the young Muhammad (PBUH).

Last verified on 26-04-2026
34

The Prophet (PBUH) started a position with the title of sahib Al Suq. What were its duties?

Correct Answer: Inspecting the army
Show explanation
Explanation
  • “Sahib al-Suq” was responsible for market supervision in Madinah.
  • The role ensured honesty in trade and fair pricing.
  • It prevented cheating, fraud, and exploitation in business.
  • It maintained Islamic economic justice in society.
  • The Prophet (PBUH) personally monitored market ethics.

Last verified on 26-04-2026

35

In the beginning Prophet Muhammad ﷺ worked as a shepherd for?

Correct Answer: Banu Saad
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Prophet Muhammad ﷺ worked as a shepherd at the age of 12 years in his early life.
  • He used to graze goats for Banu Saad tribe.
  • This work taught him patience and responsibility.
  • Later, he became a successful merchant (businessman).
  • His honest nature earned him the title Al-Amin (The Trustworthy).
  • Banu Saad is also known for fostering the Prophet ﷺ in his childhood through Hazrat Halimah Saadia (RA).

Last verified on 26-04-2026

36

Which companion asked the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) for permission to fight against his infidel father in the battle Uhud?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Hanzila (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Hazrat Abu Hanzila (RA) participated in the Battle of Uhud.
  • His father (Abu ‘Amir) was on the side of the disbelievers.
  • He sought permission from the Prophet (PBUH) to fight against him.
  • This shows his strong faith and commitment to Islam.
  • Battle of Uhud (Muslims and Quraysh) was fought in 3 A.H (625 AD).

Last verified on 26-04-2026

37

Who added second Azan for Friday prayers?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Usman (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The second Azan for Jumuah was started by Hazrat Usman (RA).
  • It was introduced during his caliphate.
  • He was the third Caliph of Islam (644–656 CE).
  • The purpose was to inform more الناس as the population increased.
  • The practice is mentioned in Sahih al-Bukhari (Friday Prayer section).

Last verified on 26-04-2026

38

Which two essential prayers were obligatory on Muslims before Mairaj-un-Nabi (SAW) or early days of Islam?

Correct Answer: Fajar & Asar
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Before Mairaj (Ascension), five daily prayers were not yet obligatory.
  • Early Muslims used to offer two prayers regularly.
  • These were Fajar (morning) and Asar (evening) prayers.
  • After Mairaj, five daily prayers became compulsory.
  • This happened around 10th year of Prophethood.
  • Salah became a fundamental pillar of Islam afterward.

Last verified on 26-04-2026

39

What was the old name of Ethiopia?

Correct Answer: Abbasinya
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The old name of Ethiopia was Abyssinia
  • Landlocked country on the Horn of Africa.
  • Capital is Addis Ababa.
  • Currency is the Birr.
  • The official language is Amharic.
  • Located in the Continent of Africa.
  • Primary Religion is Islam and Christianity.
  • Head of Govt is Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed.
  • Parliament is called the Federal Council and the House of Representatives.


Last updated by NS on 20-April-2026

40

The wine was prohibited (haram) in ______ A.H.

Correct Answer: 4th
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Wine (alcohol) was prohibited in stages in Islam.
  • The final and complete prohibition came in 4 A.H.
  • This ruling is mentioned in the Quran in Surah Al-Ma’idah.

Last verified on 26-04-2026

41

In which Hijri year was Riba (usury/interest) declared haram (forbidden)?

Correct Answer: 9 A.H
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Riba (interest/usury) was declared completely haram in 9 A.H.

  • The ruling was revealed in the final period of Islam.
  • It is mentioned in Surah Al-Baqarah (2:278–279).

Last verified on 26-04-2026

42

Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) gave the key of Bait Ullah permanently to Hazrat _____?

Correct Answer: Usman bin Talha
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The key of Baitullah (Kaaba) was entrusted by the Holy Prophet Muhammad (SAW).
  • This event took place after the Conquest of Makkah (8 AH / 630 AD).
  • The key was given to Hazrat Usman bin Talha (RA) from the Banu Abd al-Dar family.
  • The Prophet (SAW) declared that this trust will remain in his family forever.

Last verified on 21-04-2026

43

Hazrat Abu Bakar (R.A) entrusted the work of compilation of Holy Quran to?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Zaid bin Sabit (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • After many Huffaz were martyred in battles, Quran compilation was needed.
  • At Hazrat Umar (RA) request, Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) assigned this task to Hazrat Zaid bin Thabit (RA).
  • He carefully collected Quranic verses from written and memorized sources.
  • This compilation was done after the Battle of Yamama.
  • It ensured the preservation of the Holy Quran.

Last verified on 26-04-2026

44

In which surah is the ruling not to offer prayers while intoxicated?

Correct Answer: Surah Nisa
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The ruling about not praying while intoxicated is mentioned in the Quran.
  • It is found in Surah An-Nisa (4:43).
  • Muslims are instructed not to approach prayer while under the influence of alcohol.
  • The verse was part of gradual prohibition of alcohol in Islam.
  • Surah An-Nisa gives guidelines for purity before prayer.

Last verified on 26-04-2026

45

Jumma Prayer was offered and led first time in Islam by?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Musab bin Umair (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The first Jumuah (Friday) prayer in Islam was led by Hazrat Mus’ab bin Umair (RA).
  • It was performed in Madinah before the Hijrah of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
  • He was appointed as the first Islamic ambassador to Madinah.
  • He taught Islam and led the first Friday congregation there.

Last verified on 26-04-2026

46

To which Prophet were the Ten Commandments revealed?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Musa (A.S)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The Ten Commandments were given to Hazrat Musa (A.S).
  • They are divine moral and legal instructions from Allah.
  • These commandments are part of the teachings given on Mount Sinai.
  • They emphasize worship of One God and ethical behavior.
  • They are also mentioned in Islamic and Biblical traditions.

Last verified on 26-04-2026

47

Who is entitled to have both Shariat and Kitab?

Correct Answer: Rasool
Show explanation
Explanation
  • A Rasool (Messenger) is given both Shariat (law) and Kitab (book).
  • A Nabi may follow previous Shariat but does not necessarily bring a new book.
  • Rasools are sent with a new divine message and scripture.
  • They guide people with updated laws from Allah.
  • This distinguishes Rasool from Nabi in Islamic teachings.

Last verified on 26-04-2026

48

Which is known as “Saloos-ul-Quran” (One-third of the Quran)?

Correct Answer: Surah Ikhlas
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Surah Ikhlas is known as “one-third of the Quran” (Saloos-ul-Quran).
  • It emphasizes pure monotheism (Tawheed).
  • It has 4 short verses.
  • It is a Makki Surah.
  • It explains the oneness and uniqueness of Allah.

Last verified on 26-04-2026
49

Umme Aiman was the mother of?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Zaid bin Haris (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Umme Aiman (RA) was the caretaker and servant of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
  • She was the mother of Hazrat Zaid bin Haris (RA).
  • Hazrat Zaid bin Haris (RA) was the adopted son of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

Last verified on 26-04-2026

50

Who was the first preacher of Islam appointed/sent by/to Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ for the people of Madina (before his migration)?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Musab ibn Umair (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Musab ibn Umayr was appointed the first ambassador of Islam.
  • He embraced Islam in 614 CE.
  • He was sent to Yathrib (Medina) to prepare the city for the forthcoming Hijra after the first pledge with the ansar.
  • A man of Medina named Asad ibn Zurara assisted him.
  • He died in the Battle of Uhud in 625 CE.

Last verified on 21-04-2026

51

He was the cousin of the Prophet (PBUH) and also one of the Ashra Mubashras?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Zubair bin Awam (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Ashra Mubashra are those Companions of Prophet Hazrat Muhammad (P.B.U.H), who were given Good News in their life that they will be awarded Heaven.

Following is the List with Names of Ashra Mubashra.

  1. Hazrat Abu Bakar (R.A)
  2. Hazrat Umer Farooq (R.A)
  3. Hazrat Usman (R.A)
  4. Hazrat Ali (R.A)
  5. Hazrat Zubair ibn-e-Awam (R.A)
  6. Hazrat Talha (R.A)
  7. Hazrat Abdul Rehman bin-e-Auf (R.A)
  8. Hazrat Abu Obaida bin-al-Jarah (R.A)
  9. Hazrat Saad bin-e-Abi Waqas (R.A)
  10. Hazrat Saeed bin-e-Zaid (R.A)

Last verified on 02-04-2026

52

Hazrat Ali (R.A) was appointed as caretaker of Madina when the Prophet (PBUH) left for Ghazwa?

Correct Answer: Tabuk
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Hazrat Ali (RA) was appointed caretaker of Madinah during the expedition of Tabuk.
  • The Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) left him to manage the city.
  • He was responsible for the families and affairs of Muslims in Madinah.
  • Battle of Tabuk took place in 9 AH (630 AD).
  • This shows the trust of the Prophet (PBUH) in Hazrat Ali (RA).

Last verified on 26-04-2026

53

Who is called Malik-ut-Tajjar?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Usman Ghani (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Hazrat Usman Ghani (RA) is known as Malik-ut-Tajjar (King of Traders).
  • He was a very successful and wealthy merchant.
  • 3rd Caliph: Hazrat Usman (RA) (644–656)
  • Purchased Well of Ruma for المسلمين
  • Funded Army of Tabuk
  • Among the Ashra Mubashra (10 promised Jannah)

Last verified on 26-04-2026

54

The "Zakhraf" is a Surah of the Holy Quran. It means?

Correct Answer: Gold
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Surah Az-Zukhruf is the 43rd Surah of the Holy Quran.
  • The word “Zukhruf” means gold or ornaments.
  • It refers to worldly decorations and luxuries.
  • The Surah warns against being deceived by worldly wealth.
  • It emphasizes the importance of faith over materialism.

Last verified on 26-04-2026

55

Etikaf is ____ in the month of Ramzan.

Correct Answer: Sunnah
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Etikaf (I‘tikaf) is a Sunnah practice in Ramadan.
  • It is performed in the last 10 days of Ramadan.
  • Muslims stay in the mosque for worship and devotion.
  • It helps in seeking Laylat-ul-Qadr (Night of Power).
  • The Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) regularly observed it.

Last verified on 26-04-2026
56

After the fall of Yarmuk the Muslims laid siege to?

Correct Answer: Jerusalem
Show explanation
Explanation
  • After the victory in the Battle of Yarmuk (636 AD), Muslims advanced further.
  • They laid siege to Jerusalem (Bait-ul-Maqdis).
  • The siege led to the peaceful surrender of the city.
  • It occurred during the caliphate of Hazrat Umar (RA).
  • This marked a major success against the Byzantine Empire.

Last verified on 26-05-2026

57

The important part of Hajj is ?

Correct Answer: Waqoof e Arafat
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The most important part of Hajj is the Day of 'Arafah
  • This takes place on the 9th day of Dhul-Hijjah
  • It is often described as the heart of Hajj,
  • Pilgrims travel from Mina to the plains of Arafat


Last updated by NA on 25- April-2026


58

Talmood is a book of which religion

Correct Answer: Jews
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The Talmud is the central text of Rabbinic Judaism
  • It is a primary source of Jewish law (Halakha)
  • The Talmud is the "Oral Law" (Mishnah and Gemara)
  • That explains, debates, and applies the Torah to daily life.

Last updated by NA on 25- April-2026

59

Which is the third month of Islamic Calendar?

Correct Answer: Rabi-ul-Awwal
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Rabi' al-Awwal is the third month of the Islamic (Hijri) calendar
  • Translates to "The First Spring" 

List of Islamic Months

  1. Muḥarram, محرم الحرام
  2. Ṣafar, صفر المظفر
  3. Rabi al Awwal ربیع الاول
  4. Rabi al Sani ربیع الثانی
  5. Jumada al Awwal جمادی الاول
  6. Jumada al Thani جمادی الثانی
  7. Rajab, رجب المرجب
  8. Shabanشعبان المعظم
  9. Ramadan(fasting), رمضان المبارک
  10. Shawwal شوال المکرم
  11. Dhu al Qadah ذوالقعدۃ الحرام
  12. Dhu al Hijjah ذوالحجۃ الحرام:

**** 

Each month begins approximately at the time of the new moon.

**

RK220123

60

The last wife of Prophet Muhammad (SAAW)?

Correct Answer: Maimuna
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Maymunah bint al-Harith al-Hilaliyyah was the eleventh of Muhammad.
  • Her original name was Barrah 
  • Muhammad changed to Maymunah, meaning "good tidings", 
  • Born: 594 CE, Mecca, Hejaz, Arabia (present-day KSA)
  • Died: Dhu al-Hijjah, 51 A.H. age was 80 or 81.
  • Resting place: Sarif, Hejaz
  • Title: Ummul-Muʾmineen


    "Hazrat Muhammad PBUH Had 11th Wives.

    1.       HAZRAT KHADEJA,

    2.       HAZRAT SAUDA

    3.       HAZRAT AYESHA

    4.       HAZRAT HAFSA

    5.       HAZRAT ZAINAB KHUZEMA

    6.       HAZRAT UMM E SALMA

    7.       HAZRAT ZAINAB BINT E JAHESH

    8.       HAZRAT JAVERIA

    9.       HAZRAT UMM E HABIBA

    10.   HAZRAT SAFIA

    11.   HAZRAT MEMONA RA "


    Last updated by NA on 25- April-2026


61

What is the main message of Surah Hujurat?

Correct Answer: Ethic
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Surah Al-Hujurat focuses on establishing the ethical guidelines for a healthy Muslim community 
  • Al-hujurat meaning: The Chambers
  • 49th chapter (surah) of the Quran 
  • Classification: Medinan
  • Position: Juzʼ 26
  • No. of verses: 18
  • No. of Rukus: 2
  • No. of words: 353
  • No. of letters 1533

62

Abdullah bin Ubay (Head of Munafiqeen) died in?

Correct Answer: 9 AH
Show explanation
Explanation
  •  Abdullah bin Ubayy passed away in the month of Dhul-Qada in the 9 AH
  • Labelled him a munafiq or hypocrite  and "leader of the munafiqun."
  • He was the chief of the Arab tribe Banu Khazraj 
  • Leading men of Medina 
  • Other name: Ibn Salul

Last updated by NA on 25- April-2026

63

The analysis of the text of Hadith is called in terminology?

Correct Answer: Dirayat
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Dirayat al-Hadith: This branch of Hadith science focuses on the analysis of the text (Matn).
  • Ensures the meaning and context are correctly understood

Additional information

  • Mustalahat: This refers generally to the classification and terminology used in Hadith studies
  • Asma al-Rijal: This is the science of the biographies of the narrators.
  • Ahwal-al-Rawi: This term refers to the condition or state of the narrator. 

Last updated by NA on 25- April-2026

64

For how many years Bani Israel wandered in Desert of Sinai?

Correct Answer: 40
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Bani Israel wandered in the desert for 40 years.
  • Due to the refusal to enter the Promised Land (Canaan) 
  • Bani Israel refers to the descendants of Hazrat Yaqoob (A.S)

Last updated by NA on 25- April-2026

65

The tax levied by the Islamic Govt. in the days of famine is called?

Correct Answer: Daraib
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Daraib: temporary, taxes imposed by Islamic governments in cases of emergency, such as famine, war
  • Ushoor: A 10% tax on agricultural produce (or 5% on irrigated land) paid by Muslims.
  • Khiraj: A land tax imposed on agricultural land, particularly on non-Muslim subjects.
  • Khumus: A one-fifth (20%) tax on war spoils, mineral wealth, or treasure troves.

Last updated by NA on 25- April-2026

66

Marital relation resulting in another relation is called?

Correct Answer: Musaharat
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Musaharat (Affinity): Relations established through marriage (e.g., mother-in-law, son-in-law, stepchildren).
  • Hadanat (Custody): The right and duty to look after a child, especially after a divorce.
  • Qurabat (Kinship): Relatives, generally by blood.
  • Silah Rehmi (Kindness to Relatives): The Islamic duty of maintaining good ties with kin

Last updated by NA on 25- April-2026

67

First Hajj in Islam was performed in?

Correct Answer: 9 Hijri
Show explanation
Explanation
  •  First Hajj offered in 9 A.H.
  • Hajj means to intend.
  • There are 3 types of Hajj.
  • Hajj was made compulsory in 9 A.H
  • Tawaf begins from  Black Stone (Hajar al-Aswad).

  • The holy prophet performed only 1 Hajj in 10th A.H.

    Last updated by NA on 25- April-2026
    68

    Shah Abdul Qadir translated Quran in ______ language?

    Correct Answer: Urdu
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Shah Abdul Qadir translated the Quran into Urdu language
    • Completed around 1790 (or published around 1828)
    • He was the son of Shah Abdul Aziz Dehlawi
    • The grandson of Shah Waliullah.

    Additional information

    • First time translated into Punjabi:Tauqir Amaan Khan
    • First time translated into Sindhi:Aakhund Azzizullah
    • First time translated into English:George Sale
    • First time in IndiaSindhi
    • First time in WorldPersian

    Last updated by NA on 25- April-2026

    69

    The divorce in which the husband loses the right to “Ruju"?

    Correct Answer: Ba’in
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • An irrevocable divorce (Talaq-e-Bain) is the divorce in which a husband loses the right to ruju (reconcile/take back)
    • His wife during the iddah (waiting) period

    Additional information

    • Raj’i (Talaq-i-Rajaee): This is a revocable divorce, where the husband has the right to Ruju during the iddah period.
    • Ahsan: This is the most laudable form of Rajaee (revocable) divorce.
    • Bid’at (Talaq-e-Biddat): Often, this refers to triple talaq, which makes the divorce immediately irrevocable

    Last updated by NA on 25- April-2026

    70

    Who is the author of the book Seerat-al-Mustafa?

    Correct Answer: Idrees Kandhalvi
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The author of the book “Seerat-al-Mustafa” is Idris Kandhlawi
    • Language: Urdu.
    • Published in three volumes
    • Published: 1941 (As per the İslam Ansiklopedisi)
    • Published in English: 2011


    Last updated by NA on 25- April-2026

    71

    Year of grief is?

    Correct Answer: 10th Nabvi
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    •  The "Year of Grief" (Aam-ul-Huzn) is the 10th Nabvi 
    • It is known by Death of Hazrat Khadija (RA) and Abu Talib
    • Period of extreme hardship following the boycott of Banu Hashim

    72

    When did the Holy Prophet (PBUH) migrate to Madina after declaration of Prophethood?

    Correct Answer: 13th Year
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The Holy Prophet (PBUH) migrated from Mecca to Medina in 622 CE,
    • It was in the 13th year of his Prophethood. 
    • This marks the beginning of the Islamic calendar.
    • This event, known as the Hijra.

    Last updated by NA on 25- April-2026


    73

    What is meant by Nakhl?

    Correct Answer: Palm Tree
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Nakhl refers to the Palm Tree 
    • The word comes from Arabic (نخل) 
    • It is mentioned frequently in the Quran

    Last updated by NA on 25- April-2026

    74

    Which Surah of the Holy Quran was recited in the court of Najashi the King of Abyssinia?

    Correct Answer: Surah Maryam
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Surah Maryam was recited in the court of King Najashi.
    • It was recited by the Muslims who migrated to Abyssinia.
    • The surah explains the story of Hazrat Maryam (AS) and Hazrat Isa (AS).
    • King Najashi (Negus) was impressed and protected the Muslims.
    • He later accepted Islam and supported Muslims.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    75

    Garden of Fidaq was in the possession of?

    Correct Answer: Jews
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The Garden of Fidak was located near Madinah.
    • It was originally under the control of the Jewish community.
    • It was acquired by Muslims after the Khaybar expedition.
    • It became state property during the time of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    76

    What do you understand by "Bait ul Ateeq"?

    Correct Answer: The Holy Kaaba
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • “Bait ul Ateeq” refers to the Holy Kaaba.
    • It means the “Ancient House of Allah”.
    • It is the first house built for worship on Earth.
    • Muslims face it during prayers (Qiblah).
    • It is located in Makkah, Saudi Arabia.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    77

    The cover of the Kaaba is called _____?

    Correct Answer: Kiswah
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The cover of the Holy Kaaba is called Kiswah.
    • It is also known as Ghilaf-e-Kaaba in Urdu.
    • It is made of black silk with golden embroidery.
    • The Kiswah is replaced every year in Makkah.
    • It is prepared with great care and religious respect.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    78

    Which of the following Surah of the Holy Quran carries the injunction for utilization of Zakat?

    Correct Answer: Surah-e-Tauba
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The injunction for utilization of Zakat is given in Surah At-Tawbah (9:60).

    It clearly defines eight categories of people eligible for Zakat.

    1. The poor (Al-Fuqara).
    2. The needy (Al-Masakin).
    3. Zakat administrators.
    4. New Muslims and friends of the Muslim community.
    5. Slaves and captives.
    6. The debt-ridden.
    7. In the cause of God.
    8. The wayfarer (travelling with few resources).

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    79

    Taloot was the father-in-law of?

    Correct Answer: Hazart Dawood (AS)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Taloot (Saul) was a righteous king of بني إسرائيل.
    • He was the father-in-law of Hazrat Dawood (AS).
    • Hazrat Dawood (AS) married Taloot’s daughter after victory over Jalut (Goliath).
    • Taloot was chosen by Allah to lead the Israelites in battle.
    • This event is mentioned in the Holy Quran (Surah Al-Baqarah).

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    80

    How many encounters took place between the Muslims and non-Muslims during the life of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)?

    Correct Answer: 82
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • During the life of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH), Muslims faced many battles and encounters.
    • The total number of encounters is commonly recorded as 82.
    • These include Ghazwat (battles led by the Prophet) and Sariyyah (expeditions).
    • These struggles helped establish Islam in Arabia.
    • They took place mainly against the Quraysh and other opposing tribes.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    81

    A verse of the Holy Quran indicates the name of _______?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Zaid (RA)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Hazrat Zaid bin Harithah (RA) is the only companion mentioned by name in the Quran.
    • His name appears in Surah Al-Ahzab (33:37).
    • He was the adopted son of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
    • The verse refers to his marriage situation after divorce.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    82

    By "Isma-ur-Rijaal" is meant?

    Correct Answer: The Seerat of the describers of Traditions
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • “Isma-ur-Rijaal / Ilm al-Rijal” is the science of Hadith narrators.
    • It studies the life, character, and reliability of narrators.
    • It helps to verify authentic and weak Hadiths.
    • It ensures the correctness of Islamic traditions (Ahadith).

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    83

    Zaboor means?

    Correct Answer: Melodious/Pieces
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The Zabur was revealed to Hazrat Dawood (A.S).
    • Its literal meaning is Melodious songs or pieces.
    • The word Zabur is the Arabic name for the Psalms.
    • It contains praises and hymns of Allah.
    • It was sent for guidance and spiritual reflection.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026
    84

    In which year of Hijri was the order to demolish Masjid Zarar?

    Correct Answer: 9 AH after the Battle of Tabuk
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Masjid al-Dirar was built in Medina by Abu Amir and 12 hypocrites near Masjid Quba.
    • It is mentioned in the Holy Quran in Surah At-Tawbah.
    • The mosque was used for anti-Islamic activities and conspiracy against Muslims.
    • In 630 CE (9 AH), Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) ordered its destruction.
    • This happened after the Expedition of Tabuk, due to its harmful purpose.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    85

    The following Prophet suffered from blindness in his old age?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Yaqoob (AS)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Hazrat Yaqoob (AS) became blind in his old age.
    • His blindness was caused due to deep sorrow for Hazrat Yusuf (AS).
    • Later, his eyesight was restored by Allah after reunion.
    • This event is mentioned in the Holy Quran (Surah Yusuf).
    • He is also known as Prophet Jacob in English.
    • His story teaches patience (Sabr) and trust in Allah (Tawakkul).

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    86

    The real name of Abdul Muttalib was?

    Correct Answer: Shaybah
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The real name of Abdul Muttalib was Shaybah.
    • He was the grandfather of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
    • He belonged to the respected Quraysh tribe of Makkah.
    • He became the guardian of Zamzam well and Kaaba affairs.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    87

    What is the verbal meaning of "Zakat"?

    Correct Answer: Purification
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The verbal meaning of “Zakat” is Purification.
    • It comes from the Arabic root meaning “to purify” and “to grow”.
    • In Islam, Zakat purifies wealth and increases blessings.
    • It is the 3rd fundamental pillar of Islam.
    • It was made compulsory in 2 AH.
    • It is mentioned 32 times in the Holy Quran.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    88

    In which prayer (Namaz) there is no Sajda?

    Correct Answer: Namaz-e-Janaza
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Namaz-e-Janaza is the only prayer without Sajda (prostration).
    • It is a funeral prayer offered for a deceased Muslim.
    • It consists of 4 Takbeers and standing (Qiyam) only.
    • No Ruku and no Sajda are performed in this prayer.
    • It is a collective obligation (Farz-e-Kifayah).

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    89

    Bibi Fizza was the maid-servant of?

    Correct Answer: Bibi Fatima Zehra (R.A)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Bibi Fizza was the loyal maid-servant of Hazrat Fatima Zehra (R.A).
    • She served in the household of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
    • She is known for her devotion and respect in Islamic history.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    90

    Hazrat Ali (RA) did not participate in the battle of _____?

    Correct Answer: Tabook
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Hazrat Ali (RA) did not participate in the Battle of Tabuk.
    • He was ordered by the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) to stay in Madinah.
    • He acted as a caretaker of the Muslim community and Ahl-e-Bait.
    • The expedition of Tabuk took place in 9 AH (630 AD).
    • It was a major military campaign against the Byzantine forces.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    91

    In which Ghazwa Hazrat Ali (RA) was awarded Zulfiqar?

    Correct Answer: Uhad
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The famous sword Zulfiqar was awarded to Hazrat Ali (R.A) in the Battle of Uhud.
    • It was given by Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) after his bravery in battle.
    • Zulfiqar became a symbol of courage and Islamic strength.
    • Hazrat Ali (R.A) is known as “Lion of Allah (Asadullah)” for his bravery.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    92

    Who was appointed as deputy in Medina on the occasion of Ghazwah Badr Sanni by Rasool Allah (P.B.U.H)?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Abdullah bin Umm Maktum (R.A)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Deputy of Madinah → Hazrat Abdullah ibn Umm Maktum (R.A)
    • Event → Ghazwah Badr Sani (Badr al-Maw’id)
    • Role → Administrative responsibility of Madinah
    • Status → Blind companion (Sahabi)
    • Importance → Trusted early Muslim leader

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    93

    Who built Bait al Hikmat?

    Correct Answer: Caliph Mamoon Rashid
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Caliph Al-Ma'mun played the key role in developing Bait al-Hikmat (House of Wisdom) in Baghdad.
    • It became a major center of learning, research, and translation during the Islamic Golden Age.
    • Scholars from different fields worked there, especially in science, philosophy, and medicine.
    • It was established during the Abbasid Caliphate period (9th century CE).

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    94

    Prophet was staying at the house of ______ on the night of Miraj.

    Correct Answer: Umme-e-Hani
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The Miraj event took place on the 27th of Rajab in the 10th (621 A.D) year of Prophethood.
    • Prophet Muhammad (SAW) was staying at the house of  Umm-e-Hani bint Abi Talib (Prophet's Cousin's Sister) on the night of Miraj.
    • That night, Angel Jibreel came to the Prophet (SAW) and took him on the journey of Israa and Miraj.
    • During Miraj, Hazrat Adam and Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) met at the first heaven.
    • The event is mentioned in Surah Bani Israel.

    It is also known as 'Isra and Mi'raj' or 'Shab-e-Meraj'. 

    • Isra: Night journey from Makkah to Masjid Al-Aqsa.
    • Mi’raj: Journey from Masjid Al-Aqsa to the heavens.

    Gifts of the 27th of Rajab:

    • The five prescribed daily prayers.
    • The last part of the chapter of Al-Baqara (Amanar-Rasulu).

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    95

    By which name, Holy Quran calls believers of Hazrat Issa (A.S)?

    Correct Answer: Hawari
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The Holy Quran refers to the followers of Hazrat Isa (A.S) as “Al-Hawariyyun” (The Disciples).
    • These were the close companions and helpers of Prophet Isa (A.S).
    • They believed in him and supported his mission of preaching monotheism.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    96

    The nation of Samood was preached by?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Salih (AS)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The nation of Samood was preached by Hazrat Saleh (AS).
    • A miracle of the pregnant female camel was sent to Samood.

    Last verified on 26-04-2026

    97

    Which Ayat of the Holy Quran is called Sardar-ul-Ayat?

    Correct Answer: Ayat-ul-Kursi
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Ayatul Kursi is the 255th verse of the 2nd surah of the Quran, Al-Baqarah.
    • It is located in the third para named "تِلْكَ ٱلْرُّسُلُ."
    • It is also known as Sardar ul Ayat or The Throne Verse.
    • Surah Al-Baqarah is the longest Surah of the Qur'an.
    • It revealed after the Prophet ﷺ migrated to Madinah.

    ٱللَّهُ لَاۤ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلۡحَیُّ ٱلۡقَیُّومُۚ لَا تَأۡخُذُهُۥ سِنَةࣱ وَلَا نَوۡمࣱۚ لَّهُۥ مَا فِی ٱلسَّمَـٰوَ ٰ⁠تِ وَمَا فِی ٱلۡأَرۡضِۗ مَن ذَا ٱلَّذِی یَشۡفَعُ عِندَهُۥۤ إِلَّا بِإِذۡنِهِۦۚ یَعۡلَمُ مَا بَیۡنَ أَیۡدِیهِمۡ وَمَا خَلۡفَهُمۡۖ وَلَا یُحِیطُونَ بِشَیۡءࣲ مِّنۡ عِلۡمِهِۦۤ إِلَّا بِمَا شَاۤءَۚ وَسِعَ كُرۡسِیُّهُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَ ٰ⁠تِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَۖ وَلَا یَـُٔودُهُۥ حِفۡظُهُمَاۚ وَهُوَ ٱلۡعَلِیُّ ٱلۡعَظِیمُ


    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    98

    Who said that Zakat is treasure of Islam?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Muhammad
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The saying “Zakat is the treasure of Islam” is attributed to Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
    • Zakat purifies wealth and supports poor and needy people.
    • It strengthens social justice in society.
    • It is the 3rd pillar of Islam.
    • Zakat system is clearly mentioned in the Qur’an, especially in Surah At-Tawbah.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    99

    Who gave the idea of 'Divine affirmation of God's Uniqueness' (Tauheed-i-Illah)?

    Correct Answer: Akbar
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Founder → Akbar
    • Year → 1582 AD
    • Name → Din-i-Ilahi / Tauheed-i-Illahi
    • Nature → Syncretic (mixed religious system)

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    100

    "Perfect Man" (al-insanal-Kamil) is written by?

    Correct Answer: Ibn Arabi
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The concept of “Al-Insan al-Kamil” (Perfect Man) is presented by Ibn Arabi.
    • He was a famous Sufi scholar and philosopher.
    • He is known as “Sheikh-e-Akbar” (The Greatest Sheikh).
    • His writings deeply influenced Islamic mysticism (Tasawwuf).

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    101

    The famous "Dome of the Rock" at Jerusalem was built by?

    Correct Answer: Abdul Malik
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Dome of the Rock

    • Location: Temple Mount, Old City of Jerusalem
    • Completed: 691–692 CE by Umayyad Caliph Abd al-Malik
    • Built on: Site of the Second Jewish Temple
    • Historical Fact: Original temple destroyed in 70 CE by Romans
    • Significance: Important Islamic shrine and landmark

    Last verified on 15-01-2026

    102

    Apostasy movement took place in the khilafat of Hazrat _____?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The Apostasy (Ridda) Movement took place during the Khilafat of Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A).
    • After the death of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH), some tribes refused to pay Zakat and left Islam.
    • Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A) launched campaigns to bring unity in the Muslim Ummah.
    • He successfully suppressed all apostate tribes.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026
    103

    The year of Elephant (Amulfeel) is?

    Correct Answer: 570
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Year → 570 AD
    • Event → Attack on Makkah by Abraha
    • Army → Included elephants
    • Result → Army destroyed by Ababil birds
    • Significance → Birth year of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    104

    Grand Mosque at Damascus was built by?

    Correct Answer: Walid bin Abdul Malik
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The Great Mosque of Damascus is also known as the Umayyad Mosque.
    • It was built by the Umayyad Caliph al-Walīd I between 705 and 715 CE.
    • It is located in the old city of Damascus.
    • It is the capital of Syria.
    • It is one of the largest and oldest mosques in the world.
    • The mosque opened in 715 AD.

    Last verified 25-04-2026

    105

    Who was the first Muslim to interpret Qur'an in English in 1905?

    Correct Answer: Dr. Khalifa Abdul Hakeem
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The first Muslim to publish an English translation/interpretation of the Qur'an in 1905 was Dr. Muhammad Abdul Hakim Khan.

    Additional information:

    • Urdu (First translation): Shah Abdul Qadir & Shah Rafiuddin (Mojaz-ul-Quran)
    • Punjabi (First translation): Tauqir Amaan Khan
    • Sindhi (First translation): Akhund Azizullah
    • English (First translation): George Sale
    • First translation in India: Sindhi
    • First translation in the world: Persian

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    106

    At least how many persons constitute a congregation?

    Correct Answer: 2
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • A congregation (Jama’ah) in Islam can be formed with at least 2 persons.
    • One person acts as Imam and the other as follower (Muqtadi).
    • This applies to Jama’ah prayer (Salah in congregation).

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    107

    In which Ghazwa did the Holy Prophet (P.B.U.H) give the good news of the conquest of Persia and Rome?

    Correct Answer: Khandaq
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The good news of the conquest of Persia and Rome was given during the Battle of the Trench (Khandaq).
    • During digging the trench, Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) struck a rock and made this prophecy.
    • The Battle of Khandaq is also called Ghazwa-e-Ahzab.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    108

    Fast literally means?

    Correct Answer: To prevent
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The word “Fast” (Saum / Sawm) literally means “to prevent” or “to abstain.”
    • In Islam, it refers to preventing oneself from food, drink, and sinful acts.
    • It is an important act of worship in Islam.
    • It in Islam is observed during the month of Ramadan.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    109

    Name the mother of Hazrat Yousaf (AS)?

    Correct Answer: Raheel
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Prophet Yusuf (عليه السلام) was one of the twelve sons of Prophet Yaqub (AS).
    • His mother name was Raheel, (Rachel).


    Last verified on 25-04-2026
    110

    Kharijite, Qadarite, Jabriate, Mu’tazilite, and Ash’arite movements are known as?

    Correct Answer: Religious sects
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • These groups emerged in early Islamic history due to differences in beliefs (Aqeedah).
    • They are classified as religious sects.
    • Each group had its own interpretation of faith, free will, and divine power.
    • Kharijites – strict views on leadership and faith
    • Qadarites – believed in free will
    • Jabriates – believed in predestination (no free will)
    • Mu’tazilites – emphasized logic and reasoning
    • Ash’arites – balanced revelation and rationality

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    111

    The meaning of the word Al-Hadid is?

    Correct Answer: The Iron
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Al-Hadid is an Arabic word meaning “Iron.”
    • It is also the name of the 57th Surah of the Quran.
    • Iron is mentioned as a strong and beneficial metal for mankind.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    112

    The famous book “The Three Great Prophets of the World” is written by?

    Correct Answer: Lord Headley
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • “The Three Great Prophets of the World” was written by Lord Headley.
    • Published in 1923, it discusses the lives of Hazrat Musa (AS), Hazrat Isa (AS), and Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
    • Birth: 1855
    • Death: 1935
    • Accepted Islam: 1913

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    113

    The book Bidayat-al-Mujtahid is written by?

    Correct Answer: Ibn Rushd
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Author: Ibn Rushd
    • Field: Fiqh (Islamic Law)
    • Style: Comparative jurisprudence
    • Region: Al-Andalus (Spain)
    • Known as: Averroes in the West

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    114

    The day when Hazrat Israfil shall blow the trumpet and all human beings and living souls shall die, is called?

    Correct Answer: Youm-e-Qayamt
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Hazrat Israfil will blow the trumpet (Sur).
    • This event marks the beginning of Yawm al-Qiyamah.
    • All living beings will die at the first blowing of the trumpet.
    • It is a major sign of the end of the world in Islamic belief.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    115

    The language of Jews of Madina was?

    Correct Answer: Hebrew
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The Jews of Madina mainly used Hebrew language in religious texts and identity.
    • At the time of Prophet Muhammad, Hebrew was the primary written and cultural language of the Jewish tribes.
    • Madina had multiple tribes including Jewish communities like Banu Qaynuqa, Banu Nadir, and Banu Qurayza.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    116

    Amir Mawiyyia made his capital Damascus.

    Correct Answer: True
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Muawiyah ibn Abi Sufyan established Damascus (Dimashq) as the capital of the Umayyad Caliphate.
    • This happened after the period of the Rightly Guided Caliphs.
    • Damascus became the political center of the Islamic empire during his rule.
    • He ruled from 661–680 AD.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    117

    Hazrat Shoaib (A.S) was the father-in law of Hazrat Musa (A.S).

    Correct Answer: True
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Hazrat Shoaib (Jethro) is traditionally believed to be connected to Hazrat Musa (Moses).
    • In Islamic tradition, Hazrat Musa (A.S) married the daughter of Hazrat Shoaib (A.S) while staying in Madyan.
    • Therefore, Hazrat Shoaib (A.S) is commonly described as Musa (A.S)’s father-in-law (sustaan).
    • This event is mentioned in the story of Musa (A.S) in the Qur’an.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    118

    Which of the following is the second source of Islamic laws?

    Correct Answer: Hadith (Sunnah)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In Islamic jurisprudence, the sources of Islamic law are:

    1. Quran (the holy book of Islam)
    2. Hadith (the sayings and actions of the Prophet Muhammad)
    3. Ijma (the consensus of the Muslim community)
    4. Qiyas (analogy or reasoning)

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    119

    Name the term which can also be called illegitimate?

    Correct Answer: Makrub-i-Tanzihi
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • In Islamic jurisprudence, actions that are not sinful but disliked are called Makruh (Makrub-i-Tanzihi).
    • These acts are better avoided, but no punishment is given if done.
    • It falls under Fiqh rulings between halal and haram.
    • It is considered discouraged but not forbidden.
    • Makruh is of two types: Makruh Tanzihi (mild dislike) and Makruh Tahrimi (strong dislike).

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    120

    Who was the conqueror of Spain?

    Correct Answer: Tariq bin Ziad
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The conqueror of Spain was Tariq ibn Ziyad.
    • He led the Muslim army in 711 AD and began the conquest of the Iberian Peninsula.
    • He defeated the Visigothic ruler King Roderic at the Battle of Guadalete.
    • His landing place is famously known as Jabal al-Tariq (Gibraltar).

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    121

    Tragedy of Karbala took place in the year _____?

    Correct Answer: 61 Hijri
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The Battle of Karbala took place on Muharram 10.
    • In the year 61 A.H of the Islamic calendar (October 10, 680).
    • In Karbala, present-day Iraq.
    • It was fought between the army of the second Umayyad Caliph, Yazid I, and a small army led by Hussain ibn Ali (AS).
    • The Umayyad Caliph Muawiyah I had nominated his son Yazid as his successor.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    122

    Order without obligations called?

    Correct Answer: Mustahab
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • In Islamic teachings, actions that are recommended but not compulsory are called Mustahab.
    • These acts bring reward if done but no sin if left.
    • They fall under Fiqh categories of voluntary deeds.
    • They are encouraged to increase spiritual reward and closeness to Allah.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    123

    Hazrat Ali (R.A) transferred his capital from Madina to Kufa.

    Correct Answer: True
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Hazrat Ali ibn Abi Talib shifted the capital of the Islamic state from Madina to Kufa.
    • This happened during his caliphate (656–661 AD).
    • Kufa became the political center due to growing administrative and military needs.
    • Madina remained the spiritual center of Islam.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    124

    The battle of Jamal was fought between Hazrat Aysha and Amir Mawiyyia.

    Correct Answer: False
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Battle of the Camel (Jang-e-Jamal) was fought in 656 AD (36 AH).
    • It was not between Hazrat Aisha (RA) and Muawiyah (RA).

    The main sides were:

    1. Hazrat Ali (RA)
    2. Opposing group including Hazrat Aisha (RA), Talha (RA), and Zubair (RA) (as they sought justice for Hazrat Usman’s (RA) murder)

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    125

    Hazrat Usman Ghani (RA) accepted Islam by the invitation of Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA).

    Correct Answer: True
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Hazrat Usman ibn Affan accepted Islam in the early period of Islam.
    • He embraced Islam through the invitation of Hazrat Abu Bakr.
    • He later became the third Caliph.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    126

    For the first time Muslim women took part in the battle of Khyber.

    Correct Answer: False
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Battle of Khaybar was fought in 7 AH (628 AD).
    • During this battle, Muslim women provided support roles such as water supply, nursing, and assistance.
    • Famous companion Hazrat Safiyyah bint Abdul Muttalib also participated in support.
    • However, women did not participate as frontline fighters in organized combat.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    127

    The prophet Muhammad (S.AW.W) stayed with Hazrat Ayub Ansari (R.A) for seven months.

    Correct Answer: True
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Prophet Muhammad migrated to Madina during Hijrah (622 AD).
    • He stayed seven months at the house of Hazrat Abu Ayyub Al-Ansari.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    128

    The famous battle of Qudisiyfah was fought during the period of Hazrat Usman.

    Correct Answer: False
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Battle of al-Qadisiyyah was fought during the caliphate of Hazrat Umar (RA), not Hazrat Usman (RA).
    • It took place in 14 AH (636 AD) against the Persian Empire.
    • The Muslim army was led by Saad ibn Abi Waqqas (RA).

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    129

    Which Prophet's story' is named as Ahsan-ul-Qasas in the Holy Quran?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Yousuf (AS)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The story of Prophet Yusuf (Joseph) (AS) is described as Ahsan-ul-Qasas
    • Ahsan-ul-Qasas means (the best of stories) in the Holy Quran.
    • Surah Yusuf: 12th chapter of the Qur’an
    • Main theme: Patience and trust in Allah
    • Nation: Bani Israel
    • Lessons: Forgiveness, honesty, patience

    Last verified on 25-04-2026
    130

    How many times Hazrat Jibrael (AS) is mentioned in the holy Quran?

    Correct Answer: 3
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Hazrat Jibrael (Gabriel) is the angel who brought revelation to prophets.
    • His name is explicitly mentioned 3 times in the Qur'an.
    • These references are found in Surah Al-Baqarah and Surah At-Tahrim.
    • He is also known as Rooh-ul-Qudus (The Holy Spirit) and Rooh-ul-Ameen (The Trustworthy Spirit) in Islam.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    131

    The Bait-e-Rizwan is an?

    Correct Answer: Oath
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Bait-e-Rizwan was a historic pledge taken by Muslims at Hudaybiyyah in 6 A.H.
    • It was taken under the leadership of Prophet Muhammad when rumors spread about the killing of Hazrat Usman (RA).
    • Participants: Around 1400 Sahaba
    • Reason: To support Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
    • Result: Treaty of Hudaybiyyah followed

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    132

    After sleeping for 100 years, he again woke up?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Uzair (AS)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Prophet: Uzair (A.S)
    • Nation: Bani Israel
    • Event: Death for 100 years and revival
    • Lesson: Allah has complete power over life and death
    • Purpose: To show a sign for mankind
    • Mentioned in Surah Al-Baqarah (2:259)

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    133

    For how many months Hazrat Hasan (R.A) remained khalifa?

    Correct Answer: 14 Months
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Hazrat Hasan ibn Ali became caliph after the martyrdom of Hazrat Ali (RA) in 661 AD.
    • Duration: 14 months
    • Father: Hazrat Ali ibn Abi Talib
    • Grandfather: Prophet Muhammad
    • Known for: Peace agreement and unity of Muslims

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    134

    Siha-e-Sitta are ______ books of Hadith?

    Correct Answer: Six
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    ؒList of Siha-e-Sita Books

    1. Sahih al Bukhari, collected by Imam Bukhari (d. 870), includes 7275 ahadith

    2. Sahih Muslim, collected by Muslim b. al-Hajjaj (d. 875), includes 9200 ahadith

    3. Sunan Nasai, collected by an-Nasa'i (d. 915) includes 5270 ahadith

    4. Sunan Abu Dawood, collected by Abu Dawood (d. 888)  includes 4800 ahadith

    5. Jami at-Tirmidhi, collected by al-Tirmidhi (d. 892)  includes 3956 ahadith

    6. Sunan ibn Majah, collected by Ibn Majah (d. 887)  includes 4000 ahadith


    Last verified on 26-04-2026

    135

    Which book of narration was written by Abdullah Ibn Amr Ibn Al-As (R.A)?

    Correct Answer: Sahifah-e-Saadiqa
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As was a famous Sahabi known for writing Hadith.
    • He compiled one of the earliest Hadith collections called Sahifah al-Sadiqah.
    • This Sahifah contained narrations he directly wrote from Prophet Muhammad.
    • It is considered one of the earliest written Hadith documents in Islam.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026
    136

    In Torat by which name Prophet Muhammad ﷺ was called?

    Correct Answer: Tayyab
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The names of Prophet Muhammad PBUH are mentioned in earlier divine books.
    • In the Torat, the name “Tayyab” is attributed to him.
    • “Tayyab” means pure, good, and virtuous.
    • In the Zaboor, the name “Farooq” is mentioned.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026
    137

    Shah Wali Ullah wrote the following commentary of the Holy Quran?

    Correct Answer: Translation of the Holy Quran
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Shah Waliullah Dehlawi was a great Islamic scholar of India.
    • He translated the Qur'an into Persian to make it understandable.
    • His famous work is “Fath-ur-Rahman fi Tarjamatil Qur’an” (Persian translation).

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    138

    The first revealed book was ______?

    Correct Answer: Taurat
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    List of Holy Books with Prophet Names:

    1. Taurat (Torah) - Hazrat Musa (Moses) (AS)
    2. Zabur (Psalms) - Hazrat Dawood (David) (AS)
    3. Injil (Gospel) - Hazrat Isa (Jesus) (AS)
    4. Quran - Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    139

    How many Hafiz-e-Quran were shaheed in Jung-e-Yamama?

    Correct Answer: 70
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Year: 11 AH / 632 CE (December) during caliphate of Hazrat Abu Bakr
    • Opponent: Musaylimah the Liar
    • Muslims Martyred: around 1,200 (including many Huffaz)
    • Around 360–70 Huffaz (Quran memorizers) were martyred (varies in reports)
    • Place: Yamama (Arabia)

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    140

    Where is Masjid ul Hanif located where almost 70 prophets are buried?

    Correct Answer: Mina
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Masjid e Hanif is in Mina.
    • It is located in Medina
    • More than 70 prophets are buried there.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026
    141

    How many times Hajj is due in whole life?

    Correct Answer: Once in a life time
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • In Islam, performing Hajj is Fard (obligatory) once in a lifetime for those who are able.
    • It is one of the Five Pillars of Islam.
    • Qur'an clearly commands Muslims to perform Hajj if they have the means.
    • Repeating Hajj is optional and becomes Nafl (voluntary).

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    142

    Name of Hazrat Yousuf (A.S)’s grandfather was?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Ishaq (A.S)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The grandfather of Hazrat Yusuf (Joseph) was Hazrat Ishaq.
    • Hazrat Yusuf (A.S) was the son of Hazrat Yaqub.
    • Hazrat Yaqub (A.S) was the son of Hazrat Ishaq.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    143

    The battle of Uhud was fought in?

    Correct Answer: 625 AD
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Date: 23 March 625 AD (3 AH)
    • Place: Valley of Uhud, near Madina
    • Muslim Army: ~1000
    • Enemy Army: ~3000
    • 50 skilled archers were placed on Mount Uhud by Prophet Muhammad
    • It was between Muslims and the Quraish of Makkah.
    • The leader of the Quraish was Abu Sufyan.
    • The battle is mentioned in the Qur'an (Surah Aal-e-Imran).

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    144

    Imaduddin Zangi son of Qasim-Ud-Dowlas was a great?

    Correct Answer: General
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Imad al-Din Zangi was a famous ruler of the 12th century.
    • He was the founder of the Zengid dynasty and a strong military commander.
    • He played an important role in fighting the Crusaders.
    • He is best known as a great General.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    145

    How many Farz are there in Ghusul?

    Correct Answer: 3
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • In Islamic purification, Ghusl (ritual bath) has 3 Fard (obligatory acts).
    • These are necessary for the bath to be valid according to Fiqh rules.
    • Ghusl is required after major impurity (Janabat, Haiz, Nifas).
    • Without completing these 3, Ghusl is not valid.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    146

    Jannat-ul-Baqi is situated in _____?

    Correct Answer: Madina
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Jannat al-Baqīʿ, located in Medina, Saudi Arabia.
    • It is the oldest and first Islamic cemetery.
    • It situated southeast of the Prophet's Mosque.
    • It contains the graves of some of Prophet Muhammad's family and friends.
    • The first companion buried there was Uthman Ibn Madhoon.
    • Hazrat Khadija (RA) is the only Ummul Momineen not buried in Jannat al-Baqīʿ.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    147

    The Zakat is _____ on livestock.

    Correct Answer: Wajib
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • In Islamic law, Zakat on livestock is classified as Wajib (necessary obligation) in 2 A.H.
    • It becomes obligatory when livestock reaches a minimum threshold (Nisab) and completes one year.
    • It is part of Fiqh rules of financial worship.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    148

    Hazrat Ali (RA) belongs to which tribe?

    Correct Answer: Banu Hashim
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Hazrat Ali ibn Abi Talib was a close companion, cousin, and son-in-law of Prophet Muhammad.
    • He belonged to the Banu Hashim clan of Quraish tribe in Makkah.
    • He was known as Abu Turab and Asadullah (Lion of Allah).
    • He ruled as the 4th Caliph (656–661 AD) and
    • He was martyred in 661 AD.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    149

    Orders according to Islamic law and jurisprudence are called?

    Correct Answer: Ahkam
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • In Islam, orders or rulings derived from law and jurisprudence are called Ahkam.
    • Ahkam are classified into types like Fard, Wajib, Sunnah, Makruh, Haram.
    • These are practical commands based on Shariah and Fiqh.
    • Ahkam guide Muslims in daily life actions like عبادات (worship) and معاملات (dealings).

    Last verified on 25-04-2026
    150

    Who was the first poet of Islam?

    Correct Answer: Hassan Bin Sabit
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The first poet of Islam was Hassan Bin Sabit.
    • An Arabian poet and companion of the Prophet Muhammad SAW.
    • He was renowned for his poetry defending and promoting Islam.
    • He was Born in 563 AD.
    • He was died in 674 AD, aged 110 or 111.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    151

    Who conquered Bait-tul-Maqdas?

    Correct Answer: Salahuddin Ayyubi
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Salahuddin Ayyubi conquered Bait-ul-Maqdas (Jerusalem) in 1187 AD.
    • He defeated the Crusaders in the famous Battle of Hattin.
    • He was the founder of the Ayyubid dynasty and ruled Egypt and Syria.
    • His victory restored Muslim control over Jerusalem after the Crusades.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    152

    In which Nabvi year the Social Boycott of Banu Hashim was started?

    Correct Answer: 7th Nabvi
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The Social Boycott of Banu Hashim started in the 7th year of Prophethood (Nabvi).
    • It was imposed by the Quraysh of Makkah against Banu Hashim and early Muslims.
    • The boycott continued for 3 years (7th–10th Nabvi).

    Last verified on 03-04-2026

    153

    Who builds the first structure of the Holy Kaaba?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Adam AS
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • According to Islamic belief, the first structure of the Kaaba was built by Hazrat Adam (A.S).
    • Later, it was rebuilt by Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) and his son Hazrat Ismail (A.S).
    • The Kaaba is located in Makkah.
    • It is the holiest site in Islam.
    • It is the direction (Qibla) for Muslim prayers.

    Last verified on 25-04-2026

    154

    A Associating partner with Allah is called?

    Correct Answer: Shirk
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Associating partners with Allah is called Shirk in Islam.
    • A person who commits Shirk is called a Mushrik.
    • It is the opposite of Tawheed (Oneness of Allah).
    • Shirk is considered the greatest sin.
    • The Qur'an strongly condemns it.

    Last verified on 23-04-2026

    155

    Which daughter of Hazrat Abu Bakar who become the wife of Hazrat Muhammad (SAW)?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Ayesha
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Hazrat Ayesha (RA) is the 3rd wife of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ.
    • Daughter of Abu Bakar Saddiq
    • Title of Siddiqa, meaning 'the truthful woman, daughter of the truthful man
    • At the time of the marriage, she was 6 years old. Muhammad was  53 years old.
    • He had intercourse with Hazrat Ayesha when she was nine years old.
    • Also known for narrating 2,210 hadiths
    • She remained alive 46 years after the demise of Muhammad (PBUH).


    *** Additional Information***
