    *****

    Last updated by NA on 25-April-2026

    156

    Hazrat Asma (RA), the daughter of Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA), got married to _________?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Zubair Ibn al-Awam(RA)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Asma bint Abi Bakr was married to Az-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam before the Hijra.
    • Nickname Dhat an-Nitaqayn (meaning she with the two belts) 
    • Asma and Al-Zubayr had eight children
    • Elder half-sister of his wife, Ayesha (RA)

    *****

    Last updated by NA on 25-April-2026


    157

    How many obligatory Acts of Hajj are there?

    Correct Answer: Three
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    There are three obligatory acts of Hajj, known as the pillars of Hajj: 

    1. Ihram
    2. WUF
    3. Tawaf al-Ifadah

    Additional Information:

    Hajj means "to intend."

    Hajj was made compulsory in 9 A.H.

    Hajj is ordained in Surah Al-Baqarah.

    The Prophet Muhammad performed only one Hajj in 10 A.H.

    There are three types of Hajj.

    One Tawaf of Kaaba is known as a Shoot, which begins from the Shoot.

    There are three Jamarat.

    The mosque in Mina is Kheef.

    At Meekat, Hujjaj assume the state of Ihram.

    Kalima Tauheed is recited during Hajj.

    The ritual of offering sacrifice is performed at Mina.


    *****

    Last updated by NA on 25-April-2026

    158

    How many verses in Surah Al-Baqrah?

    Correct Answer: 286 Ayat
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Al-Baqarah means The Cow or The Heifer
    • Second and longest chapter of the Quran
    • Classified as Medinan.
    • 286 verses.
    •  40 Rukus 

    Additional information

    • The story of the cow (vv. 67-73).
    • Verse 282, the longest verse, is known as 'āyatu d-dayn.
    • It consists of 25,500 letters and is positioned in Juzʼ 1–3.
    • Hazrat Ibrahim (علیہ السلام) is mentioned 13 times.
    • The verse 183 describes the obligation of fasting (soam).


    *****

    Last updated by NA on 25-April-2026

    159

    How many months are there in the Islamic calendar?

    Correct Answer: 12
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    There are 12 months in the Islamic (Hijri) calendar

    1. Muḥarram, محرم الحرام
    2. Ṣafar, صفر المظفر
    3. Rabi al Awwal, ربیع الاول
    4. Rabi al Sani, ربیع الثانی
    5. Jumada al Awwal, جمادی الاول
    6. Jumada al Thani, جمادی الثانی
    7. Rajab, رجب المرجب
    8. Shaban, شعبان المعظم
    9. Ramadan (fasting), رمضان المبارک
    10. Shawwal, شوال المکرم
    11. Dhu al Qadah, ذوالقعدۃ الحرام
    12. Dhu al Hijjah, ذوالحجۃ الحرام

    Last updated by NA on 25-April-2026

    160

    Who write down human actions (good and bed deeds)?

    Correct Answer: Kiramen Katibeen
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Kiraman Katibin (Noble Recorders) are:
    • Two angels are assigned to every person
    • One records good deeds
    • The other records bad deeds


    Last updated by NA on 23-April-2026

    161

    Who made Soap for the first time?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Saleh (AS)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Hazrat Saleh invented Soap.
    • He was a prophet of the nation Samood.
    • He is buried in Lebonan.

    Last verified on 23-04-2026

    162

    Where is the grave of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S)?

    Correct Answer: Palestine
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The grave of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) is located in the city of Hebron.
    • This place is historically known as Al-Khalil.
    • It lies in present-day Palestine (West Bank).
    • The site is inside the famous Ibrahimi Mosque (Cave of the Patriarchs).

    Last verified on 23-04-2026

    163

    'Battle of Mutah' was fought between?

    Correct Answer: Muslims and Romans
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Fought in 629 AD (8 AH)
    • Location: Mu’tah (Jordan)
    • Opponents: Muslims vs Byzantine Empire (Romans)
    • Commanders martyred: Zaid bin Harithah, Jafar ibn Abi Talib, Abdullah bin Rawaha
    • Leadership later taken by Khalid ibn al-Walid

    Last verified on 23-04-2026

    164

    Zakat is how much percentage on wealth?

    Correct Answer: 2.5%
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Zakat is a compulsory 2.5%  (or 1⁄40)   levy on net qualifying wealth



    Last updated by NA on 22-April-2026

    165

    Surah Saba is a ______?

    Correct Answer: Makki Surah
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Surah Saba (سورہ سبا) is the 34th chapter of the Qur’an.
    • It consists of 54 ayats.
    • It was revealed in Makki period.
    • It addressing belief, accountability, and the stories of previous nations.
    • Focuses on the consequences of denying Allah’s signs and lessons from Prophet Dawud (A.S.) and previous communities.

    Last verified on 01-04-2026

    166

    Who wrote the book "Tareekh-ul-uman wal mulook"?

    Correct Answer: Ibn-e-Jareer Tabari
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • “Tareekh-ul-Umam wal-Mulook” is a famous book of Islamic history.
    • It covers detailed events of nations, kings, and early Islamic history.
    • The book was written by the great historian Imam Muhammad ibn Jarir al-Tabari.
    • He is one of the most reliable early Islamic historians.
    • The book is also commonly known as “Tarikh al-Tabari”.

    Last verified on 21-04-2026

    167

    Batha Valley is situated in _____?

    Correct Answer: Makkah
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Batha is associated with Riyadh (capital of Saudi Arabia) region.
    • It is part of the central Arabian Peninsula (Najd area).
    • Riyadh is one of the most important modern cities of Saudi Arabia.
    • Makkah was conquered in 8th Hijri (630 AD) by Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.
    • Madinah is located in the Hijaz region, west of Saudi Arabia.

    Last verified on 21-04-2026
    168

    What was the name of Al-Waqidi?

    Correct Answer: Muhammad
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Full name: Muhammad ibn Umar al-Waqidi
    • Famous book: Kitab al-Maghazi
    • Field: Islamic history (especially battles of Prophet SAW)
    • Era: Abbasid Caliphate period (2nd–3rd century AH).
    • He was also a Qadi (judge) in Baghdad.

    Last verified on 21-04-2026

    169

    Name of the woman for whom the beginning of the 28 Para was revealed (Surah Al-Mujadlah)?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Khowlah R.A
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The beginning of Surah Al-Mujadilah was revealed about Khawla bint Tha'labah.
    • She complained to the Prophet (PBUH) about her husband’s unjust practice (Zihar).
    • Allah responded directly to her plea in the Quran.
    • This incident is mentioned in Surah Al-Mujadilah, verse 1.

    Last verified on 21-04-2026

    170

    Arbeen is the book of Hadith in which there are _____ Hadith.

    Correct Answer: 40 Hadith
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Al-Arba'in al-Nawawiyyah is a famous Hadith book.
    • It was compiled by Imam Nawawi.
    • The word “Arbaeen” means forty (40).
    • The book contains 40 selected Hadiths on important Islamic teachings.

    Last verified on 21-04-2026

    171

    Masjid-e-Qiblatain is located in ____?

    Correct Answer: Madina
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Masjid al-Qiblatain is located in Medina.
    • It is where Prophet Muhammad (P.B.U.H) changed the direction of prayer (Qibla) from Masjid al-Aqsa to Masjid al-Haram
    • During the Zuhr prayer.
    • The mosque has two domes and two minarets.
    • It was founded by Sawad ibn Ghanam ibn Ka'ab and first opened in 1987.
    • It is traditionally believed to have been built by Abraham and Ishmael.

    Last verified on 30-03-2026

    172

    In which battle Hazrat Talha and Hazrat Zubair R.A were martyred?

    Correct Answer: Jamal
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Talha ibn Ubaydullah and Zubayr ibn al-Awwam were martyred in the Battle of the Camel.
    • This battle is also known as Battle of Jamal.
    • It took place in 656 AD.

    Additional information:

    • Location → Basra (Iraq)
    • Major Figures → Hazrat Ali (R.A), Hazrat Aisha (R.A)

    Last verified on 21-04-2026

    173

    Which is a religious book of Hinduism? Ramain, Bible, Injeel, None of these

    Correct Answer: Ramain
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Ramayana is a famous epic / religious text of Hinduism.
    • It tells the story of Lord Rama and his life.
    • Language → Originally Sanskrit
    • Major Hindu texts → Ramayana, Mahabharata, Vedas, Upanishads

    Last verified on 21-04-2026

    174

    Zou-Shadatian is title of ____?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Khuzima Bin Sabit (رضہ)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The title Zul-Shahadatayn (ذوالشہادتین) means “the one whose testimony equals two witnesses.”
    • This لقب was given to Khuzaymah ibn Thabit.
    • The Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) accepted his testimony as equal to two witnesses.
    • This was due to his strong faith and truthfulness.

    Last verified on 21-04-2026
    175

    In which surah, the tauba of three sahaba is mentioned who lagged behind in the battle of Tabuk?

    Correct Answer: Al-Tauba
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The incident is mentioned in Surah At-Tawbah.
    • It relates to three companions who stayed behind during the Battle of Tabuk (630 AD).
    • Their sincere repentance was later accepted by Allah.

    Additional information:

    • Surah Number → 9
    • Verse → 9:118
    • Revelation Type → Madani Surah

    Last verified on 21-04-2026

    176

    Hazrat Amar bin Al-Aas R.A was conquer and governor of which one the following states?

    Correct Answer: Egypt
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Amr ibn al-As was the conqueror of Egypt.
    • Born → c. 573, Mecca (Hejaz)
    • Accepted Islam → c. 629
    • Governorship → 640–646, 658–664
    • Died → c. 664 (Age 90–91)
    • Appointed by → Abu Bakr for Syrian campaign

    Last verified on 21-04-2026

    177

    The order of appearance of Surah Al-Kahf in the Holy Quran is________?

    Correct Answer: 18
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Order of Appearance: 18th
    • Total Verses: 110
    • Location: Makkah
    • Placement: 15th and 16th Juz


    Last updated by NS 19 April 2026

    178

    The native homeland of Hazrat Ibrahim was?

    Correct Answer: Ur
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The native homeland of Prophet Ibrahim (AS) was Babylon,
    • The city of Ur is located in Iraq.
    • Title: Khalil Ullah 
    • Mentioned in the Holy Quran 67 times.


    Last updated by NS 19 April 2026

    179

    Who is considered the compiler of the most authentic collection of hadith?

    Correct Answer: Bukhari
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Imam Bukhari (810-870 CE) is widely considered the compiler of the most authentic collection of hadith, known as Sahih al-Bukhari.
    • His collection is regarded as one of the most reliable and authentic sources of hadith.
    • He is considered the most authentic book after the Quran by many Muslims.

    Additional information:

    List of Siha-e-Sita Books:

    1. Sahih al Bukhari, collected by Imam Bukhari (d. 870), includes 7275 ahadith

    2. Sahih Muslim, collected by Muslim b. al-Hajjaj (d. 875), includes 9200 ahadith

    3. Sunan Nasa’i, collected by an-Nasa'i (d. 915)

    4. Sunan Abu Dawood, collected by Abu Dawood (d. 888)

    5. Jami at-Tirmidhi, collected by al-Tirmidhi (d. 892)

    6. Sunan ibn Majah, collected by Ibn Majah (d. 887)


    ؒList of Siha-e-Sita Books:

    1. Sahih al Bukhari, collected by Imam Bukhari (d. 870), includes 7275 ahadith

    2. Sahih Muslim, collected by Muslim b. al-Hajjaj (d. 875), includes 9200 ahadith

    3. Sunan Nasai, collected by an-Nasa'i (d. 915) includes 5270 ahadith

    4. Sunan Abu Dawood, collected by Abu Dawood (d. 888)  includes 4800 ahadith

    5. Jami at-Tirmidhi, collected by al-Tirmidhi (d. 892)  includes 3956 ahadith

    6. Sunan ibn Majah, collected by Ibn Majah (d. 887)  includes 4000 ahadith


    Last verified on 19-04-2026

    180

    Who is known as Rais-ul-Munafiqeen?

    Correct Answer: Abdullah bin Ubay
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Abdullah bin Ubay (Ibn Ubayy ibn Salul) is known as Rais-ul-Munafiqeen (Leader of Hypocrites).
    • He was a prominent figure in Medina during the time of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

    Additional information:

    • Abu Jahl – Opponent of Islam in Makkah
    • Abu Lahab – Uncle of Prophet (PBUH), enemy of Islam
    • Abu Sufyan – Later accepted Islam after conquest of Makkah
    • Musaylima – False prophet
    • Walid bin Mughirah – Quraysh leader and opponent

    Last verified on 19-04-2026

    181

    اسرائیل کے معنی کیا ہیں؟

    Correct Answer: اللہ کا بندہ
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    اسرائیل عبرانی زبان کا لفظ ہے عربی زبان میں اس کا معنی عبداللہ ہے؛ یعنی اللہ کا بندہ۔

    یہ حضرت یعقوب علیہ السلام کا لقب ہے۔

    حضرت یعقوب علیہ السلام کے لقب کے حوالے سے ''بنی اسرائیل'' کا معنی ''عبداللہ کی اولاد'' ہے۔


    Last verified on 19-04-2026

    182

    Hazrat Yousuf was the son of _______?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Yaqoob
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Hazrat Yousuf (AS) was the son of Hazrat Yaqoob (AS) and mother: Raheel.
    • He was one of twelve sons of Hazrat Yaqoob (AS).
    • Known for his ability to interpret dreams and his story is a lesson in patience and faith.

    Additional information:

    • Stayed in the well for three days.
    • Sold as a slave in Egypt; bought by Zulaikha.
    • Real brother: Bin Yameen.
    • Mentioned 27 times in the Holy Quran.
    • Burial place: Palestine.

    Last verified on 09-03-2026

    183

    Which holy book is called ‘Gospel’?

    Correct Answer: Injeel
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Injeel is also called the Gospel.
    • Gospel means truth. 
    • Revealed by Allah to Hazrat Esa (AS).
    • Injeel means good news. 

     Last updated NA 13-04-2026

    184

    Baitul Mamoor is on ______ Heaven.

    Correct Answer: 7th
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    • Baitul Mamoor is on seventh Heaven. 
    • The house located directly above the Kabah on Earth,
    • Where 70,000 angels perform tawaaf (circumambulation) daily and never return. 

    There are seven heavens in Islam.

    1) Dar al-Salam

    2) Dar al-Jalal

    3) Jannatul Mava

    4) Jannatul Khuld

    5) Jannatul Adan

    6) Jannatul Firdous

    7) Jannatul Naeem 

    **

     Last updated NA 13-04-2026

    185

    What is the meaning of the word Ghani?

    Correct Answer: Self-sufficient
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    “Ghani” means self-sufficient or wealthy, often used for Allah.



     Last updated NA 13-04-2026

    186

    Holy Quran was revealed in how many years and months?

    Correct Answer: 22 years and 5 months
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Beginning of revelation: 22 December 609 CE
    • Prophet’s age at start: 40 years
    • Completion of revelation: 632 CE (year of his death)
    • Total years of revelation: ~23 years

      Additional information:

      • The Holy Quran was revealed to Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
      • First word revealed: “Iqra” (Read).
      • First 5 verses of Surah Al-Alaq were revealed in Ghar-e-Hira.
      • Last revealed Surah: Surah An-Nasr.
      • Revelation was oral, through Archangel Gabriel (Jibril).

       Last updated NA 12-04-2026

      187

      What is the relationship between Hazrat Asma (RA) and Hazrat Ayesha (RA)?

      Correct Answer: Half-sisters
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Hazrat Asma (R.A.) and Hazrat Ayesha (R.A.) were half-sisters,
      • Daughters of Hazrat Abu Bakr Siddiq (R.A.)
      • Hazrat Asma was the elder sister,
      • Age difference of 10 to 19 years

       Last updated 12 april 2026

      188

      How many wings did Hazrat Jibrael (AS) have?

      Correct Answer: 600 wings
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Hazrat Jibrael (AS) has 600 wings
      • His title is “Rooh” and “Rooh ul Ameen”.
      • Used to bring down revelations to the Prophet.
      • The Holy Quran mentions by name three times.
      • He is the chief angel.

       Last updated 12 april 2026

      189

      Hazrat Ali (R.A) was martyred in which month of Islamic calendar?

      Correct Answer: Ramazan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      • Hazrat Ali (R.A) was poisoned by a sword. 
      • On 19th Ramazan 40th Hijri
      • Before the time of the FAJAR prayers to the mosque of Kufa.
      • He was buried in Najaf al-Ashraf (Iraq).

      ***



      190

      حضرت جبرائیل کے پر (اردو معنی)

      Correct Answer: طاقت / روحانیت کی علامت
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The wings of Angel Jibreel (Gabriel) symbolize spiritual power and divinity
      • referred to as "Rooh ul Ameen" 
      • The Holy Qurn mentions Hazrat Jibreel (AS) by name only three times.
      • He is the chief angel.
      191

      Which wife of Prophet Muhammad (P.B.U.H) was the daughter of the head of the Banu Nadir tribe?

      Correct Answer: Safiya bint Huyayy
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Huyayy ibn Akhtab was the chieftain or leader of the Banu Nadir Jewish tribe.
      • His  daughter Safiyyah bint Huyayy was the wife of Prophet Muhammad (P.B.U.H) 
      • Tenth wife of Muhammad.
      • Born in Yathrib to the Banu Nadir
      • Resting place: Al-Baqi Cemetery, Medina

      Additional information
      Safiyya's father Huyayy ibn Akhtab and husband Kinana ibn al-Rabi were killed at the Battle of Khaybar in 628,

      Last updated 07-April-2026
      192

      According to Hazrat Umar (R.A), the new caliph should be decided by Shura within?

      Correct Answer: 3 days
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Hazrat Umar instructed the committee to reach a consensus within three days,
      • Before his passing in 23 AH (644 CE), he appointed a six-member committee (Shura) 

      The Members:

      1. Hazrat Ali,
      2. Hazrat Uthman,
      3. Hazrat Talha,
      4. Hazrat Zubair,
      5. Hazrat Abdul Rahman bin Auf, 
      6. Hazrat Saad bin Abi Waqqas

      Last updated 07-O4-2026

      193

      Al-Muhsanāt (Surah An-Noor) refers to?

      Correct Answer: Chaste married women
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • In Surah An-Nur, Al-Muhsanāt refers to chaste women
      • Meaning married women of good character.
      •  24th chapter of the Quran 
      • Position Juz: 18
      • Hizb no. 36
      • No. of verses: 64
      • No. of Rukus: 9
      194

      When was Umar ibn al-Khattab martyred?

      Correct Answer: During Fajr prayer
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Umar ibn al-Khattab, was martyred after Fajr prayer
      • Martyred on 24 A.H 1st Muharram 
      • At the age of 60-61 years.
      • The second Rashidun caliph
      • Ruling from August 634 until his assassination in 644.
      • father-in-law of the Prophet Muhammad SAW.
      • First Muslim to openly pray at the Kaaba.
      • Participated in nearly all battles.
      • Embraced Islam in 616 AD (6th Nabvi) at about 32 years old, after hearing verses from Surah Taha.

      Last updated 07-O4-2026

      195

      Which prominent companion tried to mediate between Uthman ibn Affan and the rebels?

      Correct Answer: Ali ibn Abi Talib
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Ali ibn Abi Talib (R.A) played a key role as a mediator between the rebels and Hazrat Uthman (R.A).
      • Uthman ibn Affan (R.A) was the 3rd Caliph of Islam.
      • Hazrat Ali (R.A) advised both sides to avoid conflict and seek a peaceful resolution.
      • He even sent his sons (Hasan & Husayn) to guard Hazrat Uthman (R.A)’s house.
      196

      During the tenure of Abu Bakr (R.A), the single compilation of the Qur’an is called:

      Correct Answer: Jamʿ-ul-Qur’an
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Jam-ul-Quran  means The Compilation of the Qur’an
      • During the caliphate of Abu Bakr, the single compilation of the Qur’an into one volume
      • Began after the Battle of Yamamah, which is called جمع القرآن (Jam -ul-Quran)


      197

      Who was the first mu'azzin (prayer caller) of Islam?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Bilal
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Bilal called the first Azan of  Fajar prayer

      First Azan was called out in 1 A.H

      The Title of Bilal ibn Rabah: Sayyid al-Muʾaḏḏin

      Born: 580 AD, Mecca, Saudi Arabia

      Died: on March 2, 640 AD, in Damascus, Syria

      Place of burial: Bab el Saghir Cemetery, Damascus, Syria

      Hazrat 'Umar was the first person to suggest the calling of the Azan.

      198

      Hazrat Umar bin Abdul Aziz was died in which year?

      Correct Answer: 720 A.D/101 A.H
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz was a just ruler of the Umayyad Caliphate.
      • He ruled from 717 to 720 A.D.
      • He died in 720 A.D (101 A.H) at the age of 39 years.
      • Known as the 5th Rightly Guided Caliph due to his justice and reforms.

      Last verified on 02-04-2026

      199

      What is the meaning of Hijr-e-Aswad?

      Correct Answer: Black Stone
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Hajr-e-Aswad means “Black Stone.”
      • It is a sacred stone fixed in the Kaaba, Makkah.
      • It was given from Heaven to Prophet Ibrahim (AS).
      • Originally white, Hajr-e-Aswad turned black due to sins of people (according to tradition).

      Last verified on 02-04-2026

      200

      When Second migration took place to Habshah?

      Correct Answer: 616 AD
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      First Migration to Habshah (613 A.D.)
      • Participants: 12 men, 4 women
      • Notable Figures: Uthman ibn Affan (R.A), Ruqayyah (R.A)
      • Reason: Escape persecution in Mecca
      Second Migration to Habshah (616 A.D.)
      • Participants: 83 men, 18–19 women
      • Purpose: Larger group sought safety and freedom to practice Islam
      Both migrations are referenced in Surah Anfal.
      Habshah (Abyssinia) was ruled by a just Christian king (Negus) who granted Muslims protection.
      Last verified on 04-04-2026
      201

      In which country Mount Judi (Cudi) also known as Mount Qardū exits?

      Correct Answer: Turkey
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Country: Turkey
      • Mountain: Mount Judi / Qardū
      • Mentioned in: Quran, Surah Hud 44
      • Historical significance: Landing of Noah’s Ark
      • Region: Southeastern Anatolia

      Last verified on 04-04-2026

      202

      Salat-e-Istasqa is offered on the occasion of _____?

      Correct Answer: Shortage of Rain
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Salat-ul-Istisqa: Prayer to ask Allah for rain during drought or low rainfall.
      • Rakat: 2
      • Specials: No Adhan or Iqamah; Quran recited aloud in both rakats.
      • Meaning: Istisqa = seeking water from Allah.
      Additional information:
      • Namaz-e-Khusuf (نماز خسوف): Offered during a lunar eclipse.
      • Namaz-e-Kusoof (نماز کسوف): Offered during a solar eclipse.

      Last verified on 03-04-2026

      203

      Masjid-e-Khief is located in ________?

      Correct Answer: Minna
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Masjid al-Khayf is located in Mina, near Makkah.
      • The Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) offered prayers here and delivered a sermon during Hajjat al-Wada (10 AH).
      • In this mosque almost 70 Prophets are buried.

      Last verified on 03-04-2026

      204

      Ada Bin Hatim Tai embraced Islam in which Hijri?

      Correct Answer: 9th Hijri
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Adi ibn Hatim accepted Islam in 9th Hijri (630 AD).
      • He was the son of Hatim al-Tai, a famous Arab known for generosity.
      • Before accepting Islam, Adi remained opposed to Islam for about 20 years.

      Last verified on 03-04-2026

      205

      Who was the fifth righteous caliph of Hijaz?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz was a just ruler of the Umayyad Caliphate.
      • He ruled from 717 to 720 A.D.
      • He died in 720 A.D (101 A.H) at the age of 39 years.
      • Known as the 5th Rightly Guided Caliph due to his justice and reforms.

      Last verified on 02-04-2026

      206

      When the first wahi was revealed to the Holy Prophet ﷺ?

      Correct Answer: August 610 A.D
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The first Wahi (revelation) was revealed to the Holy Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in 610 AD
        • Location: Cave of Hira (Ghar Hira) on Jabal al-Noor.
        • Date: Monday, Ramadan (roughly August 610 AD).
        • Age of Prophet ﷺ: 40 lunar years, 6 months, and 12 days.
        • Verses Revealed: The first five verses of Surah Al-`Alaq ("Iqra' bismi rabbik...").


        Last updated by NA on 23-April-2026
      207

      Sahib al-Israr (Keeper of Secrets) was ______?

      Correct Answer: Huzaifa
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Sahib al-Israr means “Keeper of Secrets.”
      • This title was given to Hazrat Huzaifa ibn al-Yaman (RA).
      • He was trusted by Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) with confidential matters.
      • He knew the names of the hypocrites (Munafiqeen) in Madinah.
      • He is known for his wisdom and secrecy.

      Last verified on 02-04-2026

      208

      In which Surah the Holy Quran there is mention of Zulqarnain?

      Correct Answer: Surah Al Kahaf
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Allah mentions Zulqarnain (AS) in Surah Kahf.
      • He had immense wisdom to distinguish between right and wrong.
      • Surah Kahf Meaning is "The Cave".

      It is the 18th chapter of the Quran 

      It was revealed in Makkah.

      • No. of letters: 6425
      • No. of rukus: 12
      • No. of verses: 110
      • No. of words: 1583

      Last verified on 02-04-2026

      209

      How many Surah are in the Holy Quran?

      Correct Answer: 114
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The total number of Surah in the Holy Quran is 114.

      Additional information:

      • The number of Makki Surah is 86 and Madni is 28.
      • The first complete Madani surah of the Quran is Al-Baqarah.
      • There are 11 Sajdah are there in Makkah Surah.
      • There are only 03 Sajdah are there in Medinan Surah.

      Last verified on 01-04-2026

      210

      The ritual of standing at Arafat (Wuquf) takes place on which day of Dhul-Hijjah?

      Correct Answer: 9th
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Wuquf (Standing at Arafat) is a key ritual of Hajj.
      • It takes place on 9th Dhul-Hijjah.
      • It is also called Yawm-e-Arafat.
      • Pilgrims gather on the Plains of Arafat to pray and seek forgiveness.
      • They perform combined Dhuhr and Asr prayers and supplicate (du’a).
      • This ritual is essential for Hajj to be valid.

      Last verified on 02-04-2026

      211

      Prophet Muhammad ﷺ lived in Madina for _____ years.

      Correct Answer: 10 years
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      • Prophet Muhammad ﷺ lived in Madina for 10 years.
      • At the age of 53 Prophet Muhammad ﷺ  migrated to Madina.
      • He stayed in Medinah for 10 years from the year 622 to 632 (1 to 10 Hijri).

      Additional Information:

      • Prophet Muhammad ﷺ lived in Makkah for the first 53 years of his life.

      Last verified on 10-06-2026

      212

      How many Prophets mentioned in Holy Quran?

      Correct Answer: 25
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Holy Quran has 25 Prophets names mentioned:

      1. Prophet Adam (A.S)
      2. Prophet Idris (A.S)
      3. Prophet Noah (A.S)
      4. Prophet Hud (A.S)
      5. Prophet Saleh (A.S)
      6. Prophet Ibrahim (A.S)
      7. Prophet Lut (A.S)
      8. Prophet Ismail (A.S)
      9. Prophet Ishaaq (A.S)
      10. Prophet Yaqoob (A.S)
      11. Prophet Yusuf (A.S)
      12. Prophet Shoaib (A.S)
      13. Prophet Musa (A.S)
      14. Prophet Haroon (A.S)
      15. Prophet Ilyaas (A.S)
      16. Prophet Dawood (A.S)
      17. Prophet Sulaiman (A.S)
      18. Prophet Yunus (A.S)
      19. Prophet Ayyub (A.S)
      20. Prophet Dhul Kifl (A.S)
      21. Prophet Yasaa (A.S)
      22. Prophet Zakariyya (A.S)
      23. Prophet Yahya (A.S)
      24. Prophet Isa (A.S)
      25. Prophet Muhammad (SAW)

      Last verified on 02-04-2026

      213

      The Holy Prophet PBUH was blessed with Prophethood in the age of ____?

      Correct Answer: 40
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Prophet Muhammad PBUH have at the age of 40.
      • He and his wife had six children — two boys (who did not live into adulthood) and four girls.
      • By the time he was 40, he began having religious visions that would change his life.
      • This is Prophet Muhammad's mosque in Medina.
      • At the age of 40 in 11 BH (610 CE), Muhammad is said to have received his first verbal revelation in the cave called Hira, which was the beginning of the descent of the Quran that continued up to the end of his life.

      Last verified on 02-04-2026

      214

      Which Holy Book is called the Old Testament?

      Correct Answer: The Torait (Bible)
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Torait (Tawrat) is known as the Old Testament in the Bible.  
      • It was revealed to Prophet Musa (Moses A.S.). 
      • It contains laws and teachings given to the Israelites.

      Last verified on 02-04-2026

      215

      The fifth pillar of Islam is the symbol of _____?

      Correct Answer: Equality
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The fifth pillar of Islam is Hajj (حج), the pilgrimage to Makkah.
      • It symbolizes equality, unity, and submission to Allah.
      • Every able Muslim must perform Hajj at least once in their lifetime.
      • Pilgrims wear simple white garments (Ihram) to show equality before God.

      Last verified on 01-04-2026

      216

      Imam Shafi took the office of 'Religious Judgement' at the age of ______?

      Correct Answer: 15 years
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Full Name: Abū ʿAbdullāh Muhammad ibn Idrīs al-Shāfiʿī
      • Founder of: Shafi‘i school of Fiqh
      • Age when given Fatwa: 15 years
      • Birth: 767 AD, Gaza
      • Death: January 19, 820 AD, Old Cairo, Egypt
      • Burial Place: قبة الامام الشافعی, Egypt
      • Major Contribution: Principles of Islamic jurisprudence (Usul al-Fiqh)

      Last verified on 01-04-2026

      217

      Surah Hajj consists of _____ Ayats.

      Correct Answer: 78
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Surah Al-Hajj (سورہ حج) is the 22nd chapter of the Qur’an.
      • It consists of 78 ayats and 10 rukus.
      • It was revealed in Madina, but part of it (verses 1–24) relates to the Makki period of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
      • The rest (verses 25–78) was revealed during the early Madni period.

      Last verified on 01-04-2026

      218

      Khalid bin Waleed belonged to the tribe ______?

      Correct Answer: Banu Makhzoom
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Khalid bin Waleed was a famous Muslim military commander.
      • He belonged to the Banu Makhzoom of Quraysh in Makkah.
      • He is known as “The Sword of Allah” for his role in early Islamic battles.
      • He played a key role in the Battle of Uhud, Battle of Mu’tah, and the conquest of Persia and Arabia.

      Last verified on 01-04-2026

      219

      What is the seventh month of Islamic calendar?

      Correct Answer: Rajab
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      12 Islamic Months

      1. Muḥarram (محرم الحرام)
      2. Ṣafar (صفر المظفر)
      3. Rabi al-Awwal (ربیع الاول)
      4. Rabi al-Thani / Rabi al-Sani (ربیع الثانی)
      5. Jumada al-Awwal (جمادی الاول)
      6. Jumada al-Thani (جمادی الثانی)
      7. Rajab (رجب المرجب)
      8. Shaʿban (شعبان المعظم)
      9. Ramadan (رمضان المبارک) – Month of fasting
      10. Shawwal (شوال المکرم)
      11. Dhu al-Qaʿdah (ذوالقعدہ الحرام)
      12. Dhu al-Hijjah (ذوالحجہ الحرام) – Month of Hajj

      Last verified on 14-03-2026

      220

      Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) migrated from Makkah to Madina in the which year?

      Correct Answer: 622 CE
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) migrated from Makkah to Madina in the year 622 CE (13th Year of the Prophethood).
      • This event, known as the Hijra.
      • This marks the beginning of the Islamic calendar.

      Last verified on 14-03-2026

      221

      When did the Holy Prophet (SAW) performed Hajjatul-Wada?

      Correct Answer: 632 AD
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The Holy Prophet (SAW) performed Hajjat-ul-Wida in the 10th Hijri.
      • It was performed on Friday, the 9th of Dhu al-Hijjah (March 6, 632 A.D).
      • This final pilgrimage included his renowned Farewell Sermon.

      Last verified on 01-04-2026

      222

      Surah-e-Al Alaq has how many verses?

      Correct Answer: 19
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Surah Al-‘Alaq is the 96th chapter of the Qur’an.
      • It has 19 verses (āyāt).
      • It is a Makki Surah.
      • The first 5 verses were revealed as the first Wahy (revelation).
      • Revelation took place in Ghar-e-Hira.
      • It is also known as “Surat Iqra” (Read).

      Last verified on 01-04-2026

      223

      Identify the name of the angel who is the Gatekeeper of Heaven mentioned in the Holy Quran?

      Correct Answer: Rizwan
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Angel Rizwan is appointed as the gatekeeper of Heaven (Jannah).
      • He welcomes the righteous believers into Paradise.

      Additional information:

      • Angel Malik is the chief gatekeeper of Hell (Jahannam).
      • He oversees the punishments of the inhabitants of Hell.

      Last verified on 31-03-2026

      224

      Which of following important event took place in the 6th year of Hijra?

      Correct Answer: Sulah Hudaibiya
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The treaty allowed for a temporary truce between Muslims and Quraysh.

      Additional information:

      • Year Signed: 628 AD / 6 Hijri
      • Month: Dhu al-Qi’dah (March 628)
      • Duration: 10 years
      • Clauses: 7
      • Location: Near the tree called Hudaybiah

      Key Signatories:

      • Muslims: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, Ali ibn Abi Talib (scribe)
      • Quraysh Mecca: Suhail bin Amar

      Last verified on 02-02-2026

      225

      What is meant by Allah's attribute Al-Bari?

      Correct Answer: The Maker
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Al-Bari (الباري) means "The Maker" or "The Creator", emphasizing Allah's power and ability to create and shape everything in existence.

      Additional information:

      • Ar-Rahman (الرحمن): The Most Merciful
      • Al-Wadud (الودود): The Loving One
      • Al-Hakeem (الحكيم): The Wise
      • Al-Aleem (العليم): The All-Knowing
      • Al-Qadir (القدير): The Powerful
      • The Generous: Al-Kareem (الكريم)
      • The Glorious: Al-Majid (المجيد)
      • The Aware: Al-Khabir (الخبير)

      Last verified on 31-03-2026

      226

      Which is the longest (largest) surah in the Holy Quran?

      Correct Answer: Baqarah
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Al-Baqarah (The Cow or The Heifer) is the second and longest chapter of the Quran, with 286 verses.
      • It derives its name from the story of the cow (vv. 67-73).
      • Verse 282, the longest verse, is known as 'āyatu d-dayn.
      • It consists of 25,500 letters and is positioned in Juzʼ 1–3.
      • The chapter has 40 Rukus.
      • It is classified as Medinan.

      Additional information:

      • Hazrat Ibrahim (علیہ السلام) is mentioned 13 times.
      • The verse 183 describes the obligation of fasting (soam).
      • Surah Aal-e-Imran is the second longest surah of Holy Quran.
      • Surah Al-Kausar is the shortest surah of Holy Quran.

      Last verified on 30-03-2026

      227

      Cave Hira is in the _______ mountain.

      Correct Answer: Al-Noor
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Cave (Ghar-e-Hira) is located on Jabal Al-Noor mountain.
      • Jabal Al-Noor also Called the “Mountain of Light” or “Hill of Illumination”.
      • It takes around 45 minutes to reach the top.
      • Muhammad first received revelation in a Mount Hira cave.
      • The Holy Qur’an was revealed from 610-632 AD.

      Last verified on 30-03-2026

      228

      The Islamic calendar started in the year?

      Correct Answer: 622 AD
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Islamic Calendar starts from 622 AD, the year of Hijrah (migration) of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) from Mecca to Medina.
      • It is a lunar calendar with 354 or 355 days in a year.
      • Officially established during Hazrat Umar (R.A)’s era.
      • First year of Islamic calendar is 1 AH (Anno Hegirae).

      Last verified on 30-03-2026

      229

      In Surah Fatah ______ is called Fatah Al-Mubeen?

      Correct Answer: Sulah e Hudaibiya
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Sulah e Hudaibiya was signed in the year 628/6 A.H.
      • Sule e Huabaibiya was signed for 10 Years.

      • Al-Hudaibiyah Treaty was scribed by Ali Al-Murtaza (R.A).
      • Hudabia is the name of the tree.
      • It was a pivotal treaty between Muhammadﷺ, representing the state of Medina, and the Qurayshi tribe.

      Last verified on 30-03-2026

      230

      Name the Sahabi who narrated maximum number of Ahadis?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Hurrairah R.A
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Abd Ar-Rahman ibn Sakhr was a companion of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
      • Known by the kunyah: Abu Hurairah.
      • Most prolific narrator of Hadith, credited with 5,374 Hadiths.
      • Born 603 CE in Al Jabour, Arabia (present-day Al Bahah, KSA).
      • Died 681 CE in Medina, resting at Al-Baqi', Medina.

      Last verified on 30-03-226

      231

      Who was the leader of pagans (non Muslims) in the Battle of Badr?

      Correct Answer: Abu Jehal
      Show explanation
      Explanation
    • "Badr" means "full moon" in Arabic.

      • The Battle of Badr, also called Furqan.
      • The Battle of Badr is also known as The Day of the Criterion.
      • Day of the Criterion means کسوٹی کا دن
      • It took place on Tuesday, 13 March 624 CE (17 Ramadan, 2 AH) near the city of Badr.
      • Prophet Muhammad SAW, commanding his Sahaba, defeated the Quraysh army led by Abu Jahl (Amr ibn Hishām).
      • The Kufar forces numbered 1,000.
      • It was the first battle of Islam, with 313-317 Muslims.
      • The Muslim forces included 82-85 Muhajirun and 231 Ansar (61 from Aws and 170 from Khazraj).
      • They had 2 horses and 70 camels.
      • Fourteen Muslims were killed, while 70 Kufar were killed and 70 captured.
      • Surah Al-Anfal mentions that Allah promised to reinforce the Muslims with 1,000 angels.

      Last verified 30-03-2026

      232

      Which Prophet is called as Najiullah?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Noah (AS)
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Prophet Nuh (Noah) lived for 950 years.
      • He preached about the Oneness of Allah to his people for 950 years, but only a few accepted his message.
      • An entire chapter in the Holy Qur’an, Surah Nuh (71st Surah), is dedicated to his struggle and message.

      Titles of Prophet Nuh (AS):

      • Naji Ullah (The Saved by Allah)
      • Shaikh-ul-Ambiya (Elder among the Prophets)
      • Abu-ul-Bashr Sani (Second Father of Mankind)

      He is also called a predecessor due to his early role in guiding mankind after Prophet Adam (AS).

      Last verified on 30-03-2026

      233

      Name the wife of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) who was daughter of Umar Farooq (R.A)?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Hafsah (رضہ)
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Umm al-Mu'minin Hafsah, daughter of Umar (رضي الله عنه).
      • She was the wife of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.
      • She was the first woman known as a "Hafiz e Quran."
      • Her marriage to the Prophet ﷺ took place in 3 A.H.
      • She was born in 605 AD in Mecca.
      • She was passed away in 665 AD in Medina.

      Last verified on 30-03-2026

      234

      Which Prophet was thrown into well by his Brothers?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Yousaf (AS)
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Hazrat Yousaf (AS) was 15 years old when his brothers thrown him into the wall.
      • Hazrat Yousaf (AS) remained in the well for three days.

      Last verified on 30-03-2026

      235

      The first/oldest masjid (Mosque) on the surface of earth is _____?

      Correct Answer: Masjid-ul-Haram (Bait-ul-Allah)
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Masjid al-Haram is the first mosque on Earth.
      • It surrounds the Kaaba, originally built by Prophet Ibrahim (A.S) and Prophet Ismail (A.S).

      Additional information:

      • Masjid Al-Aqsa → Built/rebuilt by Prophet Sulaiman (A.S).
      • First mosque in Islam: Quba Mosque
      • Built after Hijrah: 622 AD (Masjid-e-Quba)

      Last verified on 30-03-2026

      236

      Who is the Current Secretary General of OIC?

      Correct Answer: Hussein Ibrahim Taha
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Hussein Ibrahim Taha was elected as Secretary-General of OIC for a five-year term starting 17 November 2021.
      • He belongs to the Republic of Chad.

      Last verified on 18-03-2026


      237

      How many ayats in the Holy Quran

      Correct Answer: 6236
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      6,236 Ayats ✅ – Most accepted count; Bismillah is not counted separately.
      6,349 Ayats – Includes Bismillah of each Surah (except Surah Tawbah).
      6,666 Ayats ❌       – Popular figure but not based on authentic scholarly counting.

      238

      How many pebbles are needed at a meximum for the ritual of stoning during Hajj?

      Correct Answer: 70
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Maximum pebbles required: 70 pebbles

      Breakdown:

      10th Dhul-Hijjah: 7 pebbles

      11th Dhul-Hijjah: 21 pebbles

      12th Dhul-Hijjah: 21 pebbles

      13th Dhul-Hijjah (if stayed): 21 pebbles

      Total Maximum = 70 pebbles

      کم سے کم 49 چاہے ہوتے

      239

      How many pebbles are needed at a minimum for the ritual of stoning during Hajj?

      Correct Answer: 49
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      اس سوال کو اچھے سے سمجھیں

       کم از کم 49  کنکریاں

      (10 ذوالحجہ = 7

      11 ذوالحجہ = 21

      12 ذوالحجہ = 21)

       

      زیادہ سے زیادہ 70  کنکریاں

      اگر حاجی 13 ذوالحجہ کو بھی رمی کرے (3 دنِ تشریق پورے کرے) تو13 ذوالحجہ کو = 21

      کل = 49 + 21 = 70

       

      240

      When a Muslim prepares for Obligatory prayer, he is like a person ______?

      Correct Answer: Who prepare for Hajj and Umrah
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Preparing for Obligatory prayer (Namaz) is compared to a person preparing for Hajj or Umrah.
      • It involves purification, focus, and intention before approaching Allah.

      Additional information:

      Preparation includes:

      • Performing Wudu (وضو)
      • Wearing clean clothes
      • Facing Qibla
      • Intention (Niyyah) for prayer
      • Mentally focusing on Allah

      Last verified on 22-03-2026

      241

      When someone forgets in Namaz, He has ______?

      Correct Answer: To perform "Sajda-e-Sahw" in the end
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Sajda-e-Sahw (سجدۂ سهو) is performed at the end of prayer to compensate for minor mistakes or forgetfulness during Namaz.
      • It is a simple prostration to correct errors like missing or adding a Rakaah unintentionally.

      Additional information:

      Situations for Sajda-e-Sahw:

      1. Forgetting a part of recitation
      2. Adding an extra Rakaah by mistake
      3. Minor mistakes in posture or actions
      4. Omission of recommended actions (Sunnah)
      5. Done before or after Tasleem, depending on madhhab

      Last verified on 22-03-2026

      242

      There is no charity on a Muslim with regard to his? Iron, Gold, Horse, Silver

      Correct Answer: Horse
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • In Islam, Zakat is not obligatory on horses meant for personal use or transport.

      Additional information:

      • Gold – above Nisab, 2.5%
      • Silver – above Nisab, 2.5%
      • Cash, trade goods, livestock (cattle, camels, sheep) – if above Nisab

      Last verified on 22-03-2026

      243

      What is the ratio / Nisab of Zakat?

      Correct Answer: 2.50%
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Zakat is an obligatory charity in Islam, calculated as 2.5% of wealth above the Nisab threshold.

      Additional information:

      • Gold: 7.5 tola (~87.48 g)
      • Silver: 52.5 tola (~612.36 g)

      Last verified on 22-03-2026

      244

      In which direction is Khyber located from Madinah?

      Correct Answer: North
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Khyber is a historic region known for the Khyber Pass.
      • From Madinah (Saudi Arabia), Khyber lies toward the north.
      • Distance from Madinah: ~60 km north of Madinah
      • Significance: Site of the Battle of Khaybar during Prophet Muhammad ﷺ’s time

      Last verified on 19-03-2026

      245

      How many signs of a hypocrite (Munafiq) did Prophet Muhammad (SAW) mention?

      Correct Answer: Three
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Prophet Muhammad ﷺ mentioned three signs of a hypocrite (Munafiq) in Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim.

      These signs help identify someone whose faith does not match actions.

      1. Lying – speaks falsehood even in ordinary matters
      2. Breaking promises – does not keep commitments
      3. Betraying trust – dishonest in trusts or responsibilities

      Last verified on 19-03-2026

      246

      Where do pilgrims perform "Wuquf" during Hajj?

      Correct Answer: Arafat
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Wuquf (وقف) is the central ritual of Hajj where pilgrims stand in prayer and supplication.
      • It takes place at Mount Arafat.
      • It is considered the most important day of Hajj.
      • Date: 9th of Dhul-Hijjah (Islamic calendar)

      Last verified on 19-03-2026

      247

      What title is given to the great leaders of Jainism?

      Correct Answer: Tirthankar
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Tirthankars are spiritual teachers in Jainism who have achieved liberation (moksha) and guide others.
      • They help followers cross the “river of worldly life” (samsara) toward salvation.

      Additional information:

      Total 24 Tirthankars in the current time cycle.

      • Rishabhanatha – First Tirthankar
      • Mahavira – 24th and last Tirthankar

      Last verified on 19-03-2026

      248

      Who was the mother of Hazrat Usman (RA)?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Arwa (RA)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Usman (RA), also known as Uthman ibn Affan.

      • He was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
      • He was the third caliph of Islam.
      • His mother was Arwa bint Kurayz (Arvi).
      • His father was Affan ibn Abi al-Asad.

      Last verified on 19-03-2026

      249

      Hazrat Usman (R.A) was martyred at the age of _____?

      Correct Answer: 82
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Usman (R.A)

      Belonged

      Quraysh

      Born

      Makkah

      Caliph

      Third caliph

      Caliphate

      23 to 35 Hijjah

      Caliphate

      644 to 656 CE

      Titles

      Zun-Noorain & Jame-al-Quran

      Martyred

      18th Zul-Hijjah 35 A.H

      Martyrdom day

      Friday

      Wives

      Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA)

      Full Name

      Usman bin Affaan

      Death cause

      Martyred by rebels


      Last verified on 18-03-2026

      250

      What is the fundamental principle of Jainism?

      Correct Answer: Ahimsa
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The fundamental principle of Jainism is Ahimsa (non-violence), which means avoiding harm to all living beings.

      Additional information:

      • Founded: ~6th century BCE, India
      • Founder: Lord Mahavira (599–527 BCE)
      • Core principles (Five Vows): Ahimsa, Satya (truth), Asteya (non-stealing), Brahmacharya (chastity), Aparigraha (non-possessiveness)
      • Major followers today: ~4–5 million
      • Main sects: Digambara, Svetambara

      Last verified on 18-03-2026

      251

      How many Muhajireen (emigrants) participated among the martyrs of the Battle of Badr?

      Correct Answer: 6
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Battle of Badr

      • Total Muslim fighters at Badr: ~313
      •  14 Muslims were martyred
      • Muhajireen martyrs: 6
      • Ansar (helpers from Madinah) martyrs: 8
      • Major battle date: 17 Ramadan, 2 AH (13 March 624 AD)
      • Location: Badr, near Madinah

      Last verified on 18-03-2026

      252

      In which Hijri year did Hazrat Khalid bin Walid (RA) pass away?

      Correct Answer: 21 AH
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Khalid Ibn Al-Walid

      • Birth: 592 AD, Mecca, Arabia
      • Death: 21 AH / 642 AD, Homs, Syria
      • Age at death: ~50 years
      • Major battles participated: Battle of Uhud, Battle of Yarmouk, Battle of Mu’tah
      • Title: “Saifullah” (Sword of Allah)

      Last verified on 18-03-2026
      253

      Injeel was revealed on which Prophet by Allah?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Isa
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Injeel is the holy book revealed by Allah to Hazrat Isa (A.S).
      • Meaning: “Good News”; also called Gospel (means Truth)
      • It was revealed in Hebrew.

      Additional information:

      • Tawrat: Revealed to Hazrat Musa (A.S)
      • Zabur: Revealed to Hazrat Dawud (A.S)
      • Quran: Revealed to Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)

      Last verified on 14-03-2026

      254

      Who was the angel that delivered messages to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ from Allah?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Jibrael (علیہ)
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Hazrat Jibrael (AS) delivered the Message to every Prophet from Hazrat Adam to Muhammad (PBUH).

      Additional information:

      • Izrail – The Angel of Death
      • Israfil – The day of resurrection.
      • Mikāal – Responsible for bringing rain and thunder to Earth.
      Last verified on 17-03-2026

      255

      Which is fifth month of Islamic Calendar?

      Correct Answer: Jumada-al-Awwal
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      12 Islamic Months

      1. Muḥarram (محرم الحرام)
      2. Ṣafar (صفر المظفر)
      3. Rabi al-Awwal (ربیع الاول)
      4. Rabi al-Thani / Rabi al-Sani (ربیع الثانی)
      5. Jumada al-Awwal (جمادی الاول)
      6. Jumada al-Thani (جمادی الثانی)
      7. Rajab (رجب المرجب)
      8. Shaʿban (شعبان المعظم)
      9. Ramadan (رمضان المبارک) – Month of fasting
      10. Shawwal (شوال المکرم)
      11. Dhu al-Qaʿdah (ذوالقعدہ الحرام)
      12. Dhu al-Hijjah (ذوالحجہ الحرام) – Month of Hajj

      Last verified on 14-03-2026

      256

      How many soldiers were in the Muslim army during the Battle of Tabuk?

      Correct Answer: 30,000
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The Expedition of Tabuk, also known as the Expedition of Usra.
      • Muslims forces was 30,000.
      • Start date: October 630 AD
      • Date: October 630 CE (Rajab AH 9)
      • Result: Bloodless battle
      • Location: north to Tabuk, near the Gulf of Aqaba, in present-day northwestern Saudi Arabia
      •  Ghazwa means that war in which our PROPHET (PBUH) took part while in which PROPHET (PBUH) did not took part is known as war.
      • The first ghazaw was ghazawa Al abawa.

      Last verified on 16-03-2026

      257

      Which religious/philosophical book, after the Bible, has been translated into the most languages in the world?

      Correct Answer: Laotzi
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Laotzi’s work (Tao Te Ching) is a foundational text of Taoism.
      • It is written by Laotzi in ancient China.
      • It has been translated into hundreds of languages worldwide, second only to the Bible.
      • The book focuses on philosophy, morality, and the way of life (Tao).
      • Its teachings influence ethics, governance, and spiritual practices.

      Last verified on 16-03-2026

      258

      Which of the following is a place of worship in Buddhism? Mosque, Church, Pagoda, None of these

      Correct Answer: Pagoda
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • A Pagoda is a tiered tower with multiple eaves.
      • It is commonly used in Buddhist temples for worship and meditation.
      • Pagodas often house relics of the Buddha or sacred texts.
      • They are found mainly in East and Southeast Asia like China, Japan, Thailand, and Myanmar.
      • Pagodas serve as both a religious and cultural symbol of Buddhism.

      Additional information:

      • Mosque: Place of worship for Muslims.
      • Church: Place of worship for Christians.
      • Stupa: Dome-shaped structure also used in Buddhism for meditation and relics.
      • Monastery (Vihara): Residence for Buddhist monks and nuns.

      Last verified on 16-03-2026

      259

      What do the last five commandments out of the Ten Commandments given to Prophet Musa (A.S) mainly deal with?

      Correct Answer: Social relations
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Ten Commandments were given to Prophet Musa (A.S) on Mount Sinai.
      • They are recorded in the Torah.
      • They are important in Judaism and Christianity.
      • The first five commandments mainly deal with worship of God.

      The last five commandments focus on how humans should treat each other in society.

      Examples of social commandments:

      1. Honor your parents
      2. Do not murder
      3. Do not steal
      4. Do not bear false witness
      5. Do not covet others’ belongings

      Last verified on 16-03-2026

      260

      How many chapters does the Bhagavad Gita have?

      Correct Answer: 18
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The Bhagavad Gita has 18 chapters.
      • It is each chapter called a “Yoga” or spiritual lesson.
      • The Gita is a dialogue between Lord Krishna and Arjuna, addressing duty, righteousness, and devotion.

      Additional information:

      • Total verses (shlokas): 700
      • Language: Sanskrit
      • Part of the Indian epic: Bhishma Parva of Mahabharata
      • Major teachings: Karma Yoga, Bhakti Yoga, Jnana Yoga

      Last verified on 16-03-2026

      261

      Which Prophet is not included in the list of Ulul-Azm Prophets? Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH), Hazrat Ayub (A.S), Hazrat Musa (A.S), Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S)

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Ayub (A.S)
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Hazrat Ayub (A.S) is not among the Ulul-Azm Prophets.
      • Ulul-Azm are the prophets who showed great patience, determination, and received major divine missions or scriptures.

      In Islamic tradition, there are five Ulul-Azm Prophets.

      1. Hazrat Nuh (A.S)
      2. Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S)
      3. Hazrat Musa (A.S)
      4. Hazrat Isa (A.S)
      5. Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)

      Last verified on 16-03-2026

      262

      Which religion is not considered a Semitic religion? Islam, Christianity, Judaism, Hinduism

      Correct Answer: Hinduism
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Hinduism originated in the Indian subcontinent, not in the Semitic cultural region.
      • Semitic religions originated in the Middle East among Semitic-speaking peoples.
      • The main Semitic religions are Judaism, Christianity, and Islam.
      • Semitic religions are also called Abrahamic religions because they trace their spiritual heritage to Prophet Ibrahim (A.S.).

      Last verified on 16-03-2026

      263

      What was the name of the father of Prophet Musa (A.S)?

      Correct Answer: Imran
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Prophet Musa (A.S.) was the great prophet of Bani Israel mentioned 136 times in the Qur’an.
      • Father: Imran
      • Mother: Yokabed (Yukabid)
      • Brother: Prophet Harun (A.S.)
      • Sister: Miriam (Maryam)
      • Follower and successor: Yusha bin Nun

      Last verified on 16-03-2026

      264

      Which religion’s followers consider themselves the most favored people of God?

      Correct Answer: Judaism
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • In Judaism, Jews believe they are the “Chosen People” of God according to their religious scriptures.
      • This belief comes from the covenant between God and Prophet Moses (Musa A.S.) mentioned in the Torah.
      • The concept means Jews were chosen to follow God’s laws and guide humanity.

      Last verified on 16-03-2026

      265

      Gautama Buddha was born in ____?

      Correct Answer: Nepal
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Gautam Budha was born in Lumbini, Nepal in 623 BC.
      • Language of discourse/lectures of Budhha was: Pali 
      • He was the founder of Buddhism.

      Last verified on 16-03-2026

      266

      Which is the first month of Islamic Calendar?

      Correct Answer: Muharram
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      12 Islamic Months

      1. Muḥarram (محرم الحرام)
      2. Ṣafar (صفر المظفر)
      3. Rabi al-Awwal (ربیع الاول)
      4. Rabi al-Thani / Rabi al-Sani (ربیع الثانی)
      5. Jumada al-Awwal (جمادی الاول)
      6. Jumada al-Thani (جمادی الثانی)
      7. Rajab (رجب المرجب)
      8. Shaʿban (شعبان المعظم)
      9. Ramadan (رمضان المبارک) – Month of fasting
      10. Shawwal (شوال المکرم)
      11. Dhu al-Qaʿdah (ذوالقعدہ الحرام)
      12. Dhu al-Hijjah (ذوالحجہ الحرام) – Month of Hajj
