      267

      Which Prophet is known as Imam-ul-Anbiya?

      Correct Answer: Prophet Muhammad (SAW)
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) is known as Imam-ul-Anbiya (Leader of the Prophets).
      • During the event of Al-Isra wal Mi'raj, he led all prophets in prayer at Al-Aqsa Mosque.
      • This leadership is the reason he is given the title Imam-ul-Anbiya.

      Additional information:

      • Final Prophet: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
      • Last Divine Book: Qur’an
      • Title: Khatam-un-Nabiyyin (Last of the Prophets)

      Last verified on 14-03-2026

      268

      Who performed first Hajj Bait-Ullah?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Adam and Hazrat Hawa
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • First Hajj: Hazrat Adam (A.S) and Hazrat Hawwa (A.S)
      • Kaaba rebuilt by: Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) and Hazrat Ismail (A.S)
      • Hajj became obligatory: 9 Hijri
      • Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) performed Hajj: 10 Hijri (632 AD)
      • Hajjat-ul-Wada: The first and last Hajj of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
      • The Prophet (PBUH) performed Umrah four times in his life.

      Last verified on 14-03-2026

      269

      Which was the first mosque built by the Holy Prophet (PBUH)?

      Correct Answer: Masjid-e-Quba
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Masjid-e-Quba was the first mosque built in the history of Islam.
      • Established in 622 CE on the outskirts of Medina (Yathrib).
      • Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) spent 14 days praying Qasr here.
      • The first Friday prayer was led by Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) in this mosque.

      Additional and Most Important information:

      • Masjid-ul-Haram (Kaaba): First mosque on the surface of the earth.
      • Masjid-e-Quba: First mosque built by Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) in Islamic history.
      • Masjid-e-Aqsa: Built much earlier, associated with Hazrat Sulaiman (A.S.).
      • Masjid-e-Nabavi: Built after Prophet’s arrival in Medina, not the first.

      Last verified on 14-03-2026

      270

      The word "Diwali" means ______?

      Correct Answer: Row of Lights
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The word Diwali comes from the Sanskrit term “Deepavali”.
      • It means a row or series of lights (lamps).
      • It refers to the tradition of lighting lamps to symbolize the victory of light over darkness.

      Last verified on 14-03-2026

      271

      How many times Prophet Musa (A.S) is mentioned in the Holy Qur’an?

      Correct Answer: 136 Times
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Prophet Musa (A.S) is mentioned about 136 times in the Holy Qur’an.
      • Prophet Musa (A.S) was given the Torah (Tawrat) as a divine book.
      • He is one of the Ulul-Azm prophets (the prophets of strong determination).

      Additional information:

      • Ibrahim (A.S) – about 69 times
      • Nuh (A.S) – about 43 times
      • Lut (A.S) – about 27 times
      • Isa (A.S) – about 25 times

      Last verified on 14-03-2026

      272

      Who was the first Caliph of Islam?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Bakar (R.A.)
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • First Caliph: Hazrat Abu Bakr (June 8, 632 – August 23, 634)
        • Second Caliph: Hazrat Umar (R.A.)
        • Third Caliph: Hazrat Usman (R.A.)
        • Fourth Caliph: Hazrat Ali (R.A.) (656–661 CE)

        Sahabi who proposed Hazrat Abu Bakr for the Caliphate: Hazrat Umar (R.A.)

        Last verified on 13-03-2026

        273

        Where was Holy Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) born?

        Correct Answer: Mecca
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Holy Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) was born in Makkah (Mecca), Saudi Arabia.
        • His birth took place in 571 AD, known as the Year of the Elephant.
        • He belonged to the Quraysh tribe, specifically the Banu Hashim clan.
        • His father Abdullah died before his birth and his mother was Amina bint Wahb.

        Last verified on 13-03-2026

        274

        Muslims pray towards the Kaabah (Qibla), but what was the Qibla before the Kaabah?

        Correct Answer: Masjid-e-Aqsa
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Before the Kaabah in Makkah, the Qibla for Muslims was Masjid-e-Aqsa in Jerusalem.
        • The Quran mentions this change in Surah Al-Baqarah (2:144), directing Muslims to face the Kaabah.

        Additional information:

        • Direction shift: from Jerusalem to Makkah
        • Change occurred: around 16–17 months after Hijrah (622 AD)
        • Purpose: unity and distinct identity for Muslims

          Last verified on 12-03-2026

          275

          Who followed infant Musa (A) basket when it was going down the river?

          Correct Answer: Musa(A)’ sister
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Prophet Musa (A)’s sister followed the basket to ensure his safety when it was set afloat in the Nile river.
          • She later offered to find a wet nurse for him, which led to their mother breastfeeding Musa (A) in Pharaoh’s palace.
          • This event is mentioned in Surah Al-Qasas, Quran.

          Last verified on 12-03-2026

          276

          What is Islamic Jurisprudence of law?

          Correct Answer: Fiqh
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • The Islamic jurisprudence of law is known as Fiqh.
          • It is the understanding and application of Shariah (Islamic law) in daily life.
          • Fiqh explains how Muslims should act in matters of worship, transactions, ethics, and personal conduct.
          • It is based on the Quran, Hadith, Ijma (consensus), and Qiyas (analogy).

          Last verified on 11-03-2026

          277

          What is the terminological meaning of 'Fiqh' ?

          Correct Answer: Law of Shariah
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • The term “Fiqh” in Islamic studies refers to the understanding of Islamic law (Shariah).
          • It is derived from the Arabic word “Fahm”, meaning deep comprehension or understanding.
          • It provides rules derived from Quran, Sunnah, Ijma (consensus), and Qiyas (analogy).

          Last verified on 11-03-2026

          278

          Who founded Buddhism in India?

          Correct Answer: Gautama Buddha
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Founder: Gautama Buddha (Siddhartha Gautama)

          • Place of Origin: India
          • Time Period: Around 5th–6th century BCE
          • Core Teachings: Four Noble Truths, Eightfold Path
          • Followers: Called Buddhists

          Last verified on 10-03-2026

          279

          Which of the following people did not believe in Nuh (AS)?

          Correct Answer: His son
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Prophet Nuh (AS) lived for 950 years preaching to his people.
          • His son drowned in the Great Flood because he refused to board the Ark.
          • The story is mentioned in the Quran, Surah Hud (11:42–43).
          • He is one of the five Ulu’l Azm prophets in Islam.
          • His Ark carried believers and pairs of animals during the flood.

          Last verified on 09-03-2026

          280

          Name the Creation of Allah who are made of smokeless fire?

          Correct Answer: Jinns
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Jinns are a creation of Allah made from smokeless fire.
            • They are invisible to humans but have free will, unlike angels.
            • Mentioned in the Quran: Surah Al-Rahman (55:15) and Surah Al-Hijr (15:27).

            Last verified on 08-03-2026

            281

            What was the significant event of 27 Rajab of the 10th year of Prophethood?

            Correct Answer: Isra and Miraj
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Miraj event took place on the 27th of Rajab in the 10th (621 A.D) year of Prophethood.

            Prophet Muhammad (SAW) was staying at the house of Umm Hani on the night of Miraj.

            That night, Angel Jibreel came to the Prophet (SAW) and took him on the journey of Israa and Miraj.

            During Miraj, Hazrat Adam and Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) met at the first heaven.

            The event is mentioned in Surah Bani Israel.

            It is also known as 'Isra and Mi'raj' or 'Shab-e-Meraj'. 

            • Isra: Night journey from Makkah to Masjid Al-Aqsa.
            • Mi’raj: Journey from Masjid Al-Aqsa to the heavens.

            Gifts of the 27th of Rajab:

            • The five prescribed daily prayers.
            • The last part of the chapter of Al-Baqara (Amanar-Rasulu).

            Last verified on 07-03-2026

            282

            Where did the Prophet (S.A.W) and Abu Bakr take shelter before proceeding to Al-Madinah?

            Correct Answer: Cave of Soor
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • During the Hijrah in 622 AD (1 AH), Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) hid in the Cave of Thawr to avoid the Quraysh who were pursuing them.
            • They stayed there for three days before continuing their journey to Madinah.

            Last verified on 07-03-2026

            283

            The sayings of the Muhammad (SAW) are called?

            Correct Answer: Hadith-e-Qauli
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Hadith-e-Qauli refers to the sayings or verbal statements of Prophet Muhammad (SAW).
              • These sayings guide Muslims in faith, worship, and daily life.

            Additional information:

            • Hadith-e-Taqriri – Approval of others’ actions by the Prophet
            • Hadith-e-Faeli – Actions performed by the Prophet

            Last verified on 06-03-2026

            284

            After Tofaan-e-Noah Hazrat Noah's (AS) boat stopped at a mountain. What is the name of this mountain?

            Correct Answer: Koh-e-Jodi
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Hazrat Noah's (AS) boat (Ark) stopped at Koh-e-Judi, Turkey.
            • The duration of the Toofan-e-Noah (Flood of Noah) was around 6 months.
            • The event occurred 2,242 years after Hazrat Adam (AS).
            • About 80 people were with him in the boat.

            Additional information:

            • Hazrat Noah (AS) lived for 950 years.
            • He was sent to Iraq.
            • He is called the Predecessor, Naji Ullah, and Shaikh ul Ambiya.
            • His title is Abu ul Bashr Sani.
            • After the flood, the city of Khasran was established.

            The nation of Noah worshiped five idols:

            • Wadh
            • Suwa
            • Yaghuth
            • Yauq
            • Nas

            Last verified on 06-03-2026

            285

            The word “Bunyan Marsus” is mentioned in which Surah of the Qur’an?

            Correct Answer: Surah As-Saff
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Operation Bunyan Um Marsoos was launched by Pakistan on 10 May 2025, after Fajr prayer.
            • It targeted seven key Indian military sites, including Pathankot, Udhampur, Gujarat air bases, Rajasthan air base, and BrahMos storage site.
            • The operation was retaliation for Indian missile strikes inside Pakistan.
            • The name Bunyan Um Marsoos comes from Surah Al-Saff (61:4), meaning “a structure firmly joined together.”
            • Verse reference: “Allah indeed loves those who fight in His Way as though they are a solid wall cemented with molten lead.”


            Last verified on 22-02-2026

            286

            Prophet Muhammad ﷺ belonged to __________ family.

            Correct Answer: Hashmi
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Prophet Muhammad belonged to the Banu Hashim (Hashimite) clan


            Named after his great-grandfather, Hashim ibn Abd Manaf, this clan was part of the Adnani Quraish. 


            Tribe: Quraysh


            Parents: Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib and Aminah bint Wahb

            NOTE:The Banu Hashim held a prestigious position in Mecca, often responsible for providing water to pilgrims at the Kaaba. 


            Last updated 18-02-2026


            287

            Who opposed Banu Hashim at the time when Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) was young?

            Correct Answer: Abu Lahab
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Abu Lahab strongly opposed the Banu Hashim and later opposed the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.


            He was a paternal uncle of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ


            Last updated 18-02-2026

            288

            Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A), the first Caliph of Islam, fought for the establishment and propagation of Islam. What was his primary focus during his leadership?

            Correct Answer: Justice
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A), the first Caliph of Islam


            Hewas known for his unwavering commitment to justice.


            Reigning from 632–634 CE


            Known as Al-Siddiq (The Truthful)


            He passed away in 13 AH (634 CE) at the age of 63


            Buried beside the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) in Masjid Nabawi, Madinah. 


            Last updated question  18-02-2026



            289

            When the battle of Trench was fought?

            Correct Answer: 627 AD
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The battle took place in 627 


            Fought in fifth Hijri


            Aso known as the Battle of Khandaq and the Battle of the Confederates.


             It is a 27-day-long defense.


            Muslims of Yathrib from Arab and Jewish tribes.

            290

            The duration of Hazrat Ali's (RA) Khilafat was?

            Correct Answer: 4 years and 9 months
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Khilafat of Hazrat Ali (R.A) lasted approximately 4 years and 9 months,


            From June 656 AD (Dhul-Hijjah 35 AH) to January 661 AD (Ramadan 40 AH).


            He was the fourth and final of the Rashidun Caliphs.


            Last updated question  18-02-2026

            291

            Jamrats' are the three places where Satan came to misguide ______?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Ibrahim (AS)
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Jamrats are the three stone pillars in Mina, Saudi Arabia.
            • They represent the places where Satan tried to misguide Hazrat Ibrahim (AS) during his obedience to Allah.

            Additional information:

            • Ritual of Rami al-Jamarat is performed on 10–13 Dhu al-Hijjah.
            • Symbolizes resisting evil and obedience to Allah.

            Last verified on 10-02-2026

            292

            The Holy Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) belongs to the tribe ______?

            Correct Answer: Banu Hashim (Quresh)
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) was born in 570 A.D. in Mecca.
            • He belonged to the Banu Hashim clan of the Quraish tribe, the most powerful tribe in Mecca at that time.
            • His great-grandfather was Hashim ibn Abd Manaf, after whom the clan is named.

            Last verified on 09-02-2026

            293

            The meaning of the name ‘Muhammad’ (PBUH) is ______?

            Correct Answer: Praised one
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • The name Muhammad (PBUH) means “the praised one” in Arabic.
            • It reflects the honor and respect associated with the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
            • The name Ahmed (PBUH) means "highly praised".

            Last verified on 09-02-2026

            294

            Which is the old name of Islam?

            Correct Answer: Deen-e-Hanif
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Islam is a monotheistic religion revealed by Allah to Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
            • Its second name is Deen-e-Haneef, meaning the upright or pure religion of Prophet Abraham (PBUH).
            • Originated in Mecca and Medina at the start of the 7th century CE.
            • History includes political, social, economic, and cultural developments of Islamic civilization.
            • Islam emphasizes belief in one God, worship, and moral conduct.

            Last verified on 07-02-2026
            295

            This is the only book, reading its every word is a worship?

            Correct Answer: Holy Quran
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Holy Quran is the word of Allah revealed to Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
            • Reading every word is considered an act of worship (Ibadah).
            • Written in Arabic, it guides faith, morality, and law.

            Additional information:

            • Memorizing Quran is called Hifz, highly rewarded in Islam.
            • Divided into 114 chapters (Surahs) and 6,666 verses (Ayahs).

            Last verified on 07-02-2026

            296

            The term “Riba” literally means?

            Correct Answer: Any excess
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The term "Riba" literally means "Any excess."

            297

            On which date do banks close for Zakat deduction?

            Correct Answer: 1st of Ramadan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Based on the Zakat and Ushr Ordinance of 1980, banks in Pakistan close for public dealings to deduct Zakat on the 1st of Ramadan. 


            This day is designated as a "Bank Holiday" specifically for the deduction of 2.5% Zakat 

            298

            Complete the Hadith: A Muslim is a ___ to another Muslim.

            Correct Answer: Brother
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Based on the Hadith narrated by 'Abdullah bin 'Umar (Sahih al-Bukhari 2442, Sahih Muslim 2580), the completed phrase is:


            The full text of the Hadith is: "A Muslim is a brother of another Muslim, so he should not oppress him, nor should he hand him over to an oppressor". 

            299

            What does Al Noor mean?

            Correct Answer: The Light
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            Al Noor means The Light (the One who illuminates guidance and truth).

            It is also one of the 99 names of Allah
            300

            What does Silah-e-Rehmi increase?

            Correct Answer: Sustenance (Rizq) and lifespan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Silah-e-Rehmi directly increases both sustenance (Rizq) and lifespan. 


            'Sila e Rehmi' means to look after and/or offer favours to the near relatives



            301

            Which Angel will blow the trumpet (Sur) at the Day of Judgment?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Israfil
            Show explanation
            Explanation


            The Angel commissioned to blow the Trumpet (Sur) on the Day of Judgment is Israfil 


            Munkar and Nakir – The angels responsible for questioning the soul of a person who has died

            302

            The oldest revealed divine (holy) book is ____?

            Correct Answer: Tawrat
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Torah is the compilation of the first five books 


            Tawrat (Torah): Revealed to Prophet Musa (Moses).


            Additional information

            First revealed Holy book: Tawrat

            Second revealed Holy book: Zaboor

            Third revealed Holy book: Injeel

            Last revealed holy book: Quran

            303

            Dhat an-Nataqain is title of?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Asma
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             She received from Muhammad the title Dhat an-Nitaqayn


            Meaning "She of the Two Belts".


            She was married to Zubayr ibn al-Awwam shortly before the Hijra.

            304

            There are _____ types of Hajj?

            Correct Answer: Three
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            There are three different types of Hajj namely:
            Hajj-ul-Ifrad.
            Hajj-ul-Qiran.
            Hajj-ul-Tamattu.
            305

            What is the difference between Hadith and Sunnah?

            Correct Answer: Hadith refers to sayings/actions of Prophet (PBUH), Sunnah refers to practices
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Hadith refers to sayings/actions of Prophet (PBUH), Sunnah refers to practices.

            Additional information:

            • Hadith: Recorded sayings of Prophet ﷺ
            • Hadith: Also includes actions and approvals
            • Hadith books: Bukhari, Muslim, Tirmidhi, Abu Dawood, Nasai
            • Sunnah: Practical way of life of Prophet ﷺ
            • Sunnah: Followed as a model by Muslims
            • Source: Sunnah is known through Hadith
            • Sunnah is the second source of Islamic law after the Quran

            Last verified on 02-02-2026

            306

            Sulah Hudaybiyyah was signed between Muslim and _______?

            Correct Answer: Quraish
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • The treaty allowed for a temporary truce between Muslims and Quraysh.

            Additional information:

            • Year Signed: 628 AD / 6 Hijri
            • Month: Dhu al-Qi’dah (March 628)
            • Duration: 10 years
            • Clauses: 7
            • Location: Near the tree called Hudaybiah

            Key Signatories:

            • Muslims: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, Ali ibn Abi Talib (scribe)
            • Quraysh Mecca: Suhail bin Amar

            Last verified on 02-02-2026

            307

            Whom did Hazrat Ali (RA) kill in the Battle of Badr?

            Correct Answer: Walid ibn Utba
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • The Battle of Badr took place in 624 AD (2 AH) between Muslims and Quraysh.
            • Hazrat Ali (RA) showed great bravery in this battle.
            • He killed Walid ibn Utba, a strong enemy of Islam.

            Additional information:

            • Muslim army: ~313
            • Quraysh army: ~1000
            • First major Muslim victory

            Last verified on 02-02-2026

            308

            Who is known as Hawari-e-Rasool (Helper/Disciple of the Prophet ﷺ)?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Zubair bin Al-Awwam (RA)
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • The title Hawari-e-Rasool (Helper/Disciple of the Prophet ﷺ) was given to Hazrat Zubair bin Al-Awwam (RA).

            Additional information:

            • The Prophet Muhammad ﷺ honored him with this title, stating: "Every Prophet has a Hawari (disciple), and my Hawari is Az-Zubair". 
            • He was one of the closest companions and a brave warrior of Islam.
            • He is also among the Ashra Mubashra (ten companions promised Jannah).

            Last verified on 02-02-2026

            309

            Which cities is/are included in Hijaaz? Makkah, Medina, Taef, All of these

            Correct Answer: All of these
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Hijaaz is a historical region in western Arabia along the Red Sea.
            • Major cities in Hijaaz include Makkah, Medina, and Ta‘if.
            • It is known for its mountainous terrain and desert climate.

            Last verified on 01-02-2026

            310

            Which servant of Hazrat Khadija was alongside Hazrat Muhammad SAW during a business trip?

            Correct Answer: Maisra
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • During Prophet Muhammad ﷺ’s early business trips for Hazrat Khadija (RA), he was often accompanied by trusted servants.
              • Maisra was the servant who traveled with him and helped manage trade and goods safely.
              • These trips helped build Prophet ﷺ’s reputation for honesty and trustworthiness.

              Last verified on 01-02-2026

              311

              Which type of knowledge is in a Mutawatir Hadith?

              Correct Answer: Certain knowledge
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • A Mutawatir Hadith is reported by a large number of reliable narrators at each stage of the chain of transmission.
              • It provides knowledge that is certain (qat’i), leaving no room for doubt.

              Additional information:

              • Mutawatir Hadith ensures authenticity and forms the basis for definitive Islamic rulings.

              Last verified on 28-01-2026

              312

              Who was chosen by Hazrat Umar (R.A) to lead the Muslim Army in the battle of Qadsiya?

              Correct Answer: Hazrat Saad bin Abi Waqas (R.A)
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Sad ibn Abi Waqqas, also known as Saʿd ibn Malik.
              • Hazrat Saad bin Abi Waqas (R.A) was chosen by Hazrat Umar (R.A) to lead the Muslim army at the Battle of Qadsiya (636 AD) against the Sassanid Empire.
              • He was a companion of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

              Additional information:

              • He was seventh person to embrace Islam, at age 17.

              Last verified on 28-01-2026
              313

              Muawiya launched the first navy in which sea?

              Correct Answer: Mediterranean Sea
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The first Muslim navy was established during the Caliphate of Uthman ibn Affan under Muawiya (Governor of Syria).
              • The navy was launched in the Mediterranean Sea to counter Byzantine naval dominance and protect the Syrian coastline.

              Additional information:

              • First major conquest: Cyprus, 649 AD
              • Historic naval battle: Battle of the Masts (Dhat al-Sawari), 655 AD, against Byzantines
              • Purpose: Protect Muslim territories and secure trade routes

              Last verified on 28-01-2026

              314

              Hazrat Salma (RA) gave advice to the Holy Prophet (PBUH) on which event?

              Correct Answer: Treaty of Hudaybiyyah
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Hazrat Salma (RA) advised Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) regarding the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah (628 AD) with Quraysh.
              • Her advice helped the Prophet ﷺ take wise and peaceful steps during the negotiations.

              Last verified on 28-01-2026

              315

              After the downfall of Banu Umayyad, their family members established a kingdom in _____?

              Correct Answer: Andalus
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Downfall: Banu Umayyad overthrown by Abbasids in 750 CE
              • Escape: Abd al-Rahman I (Abd al-Rahman ibn Mu'awiya) fled to Andalus (Muslim Spain)
              • Independent Emirate: Established Emirate of Córdoba in 756 CE
              • Caliphate: Later became Caliphate of Córdoba in 929 CE under Abd al-Rahman III
              • Significance: Continued Umayyad rule outside the Middle East, influencing culture, architecture, and governance in Spain

              Last verified on 28-01-2026

              316

              The Largest Muslim country in the world by landmass/area is ____?

              Correct Answer: Kazakhstan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Kazakhstan is the largest muslim country by area.

              Indonesia is the largest country by population.


              Kazakhstan spans an area of approximately 2.7 million square kilometres.

              Kazakhstan is the ninth (9th) largest country in the world.

              Kazakhstan is the largest landlocked country in the World.

              317

              After the death of Hazrat Umar (RA) a copy of Quran was passed to_________?

              Correct Answer: Hazrat Hafsa (R.A)
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              After the death of Hazrat Umar (RA), the copy was kept with Hazrat Hafza bint Umar.

              She was the Hazrat Umar (RA )'s daughter and the wife of the Prophet (PBUH)


              Additonal information

              • After the compilation of the Quran, the copy was given to the caliph, Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A).
              • After the death of Hazrat Abu Bakr, the copy was transferred to the next caliph, Hazrat Umar (R.A).
              • This copy is known as Mushaf-e-Hafza
              318

              The Rasool Allah ﷺ consulted his wife on important matters such as during the occasion of Treaty of advice from:

              Correct Answer: Hazrat Umm-e-Salma

              Full Name:                                          Hind bint Abi Umayya

              Appellation:                                      Umm-e-Salamah (Mother of Salamah)

              First marriage:                                 Hazrat Abdullah bin Abdil Asad (also known as Abu Salamah)

              Marriage to the Prophets:               Shawwal 4 AH

              319

              حدیث قدسی کے کہتے ہیں؟

              Correct Answer: جس میں رسول اللہ علی السلام فرمائیں کہ یہ اللہ کا فرمان ہے

              'حدیث قدسی' رسول اللہﷺسے منسوب اس روایت کو کہتے ہیں جس میں رسول اللہﷺروایت کو اللہ تعالیٰ سے منسوب کرتے ہیں،

              320

              In Holy Quran which one of the following fruits have been especially mentioned?

              Correct Answer: All the above
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The Holy Quran mentions several fruits as blessings from Allah.
              • These include dates, grapes, olives, figs, pomegranates, and bananas.
              • The fig is highlighted in Surah At-Tin (Chapter 95).
              • The date palm is mentioned over 20 times, e.g., in Surah Maryam (19:25-26).

                Additional information:

                • Olives: Mentioned as a blessed tree (Surah An-Nur 24:35)
                • Pomegranates & Bananas: Symbols of heavenly fruits (Surah Al-Rahman 55:68)

                Last verified on 25-01-2026

                321

                Which of the following approaches says that ethical issues should be judged on basis of some universal code?

                Correct Answer: Deontological
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Deontological ethics focuses on duty, rules, and moral obligations.


                It judges actions based on whether they adhere to a universal moral code, not just outcomes.


                Key philosopher: Immanuel Kant.

                322

                Which country has been name as the World's Happiest Country in World Happiness Index 2022?

                Correct Answer: Finland
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                1. Finland
                2. Denmark
                3. Iceland
                4. Israel
                5. Netherlands

                Pakistan ranked in 2022: 121 out of 146
