                323

                When Abbasid defeated Ummayad in__________?

                Correct Answer: 750 AD
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Abbasid overthrew the Umayyad dynasty in 750 CE, supporting the Mawali, or non-Arab Muslims, by moving the capital to Baghdad in 762 CE. 

                *********

                Remember Tips

                یاد کرنے کے لیے

                1. Rashidin -30 years
                2. Umayyad -100 years
                3. Abbasid - 500 years
                4. Ottoman Empire - 600 years


                324

                When was Zakat made compulsory?

                Correct Answer: 2 AH
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • Zakat was made compulsory in 2. A.H. 7-1/2 is the nasab of gold and 52-half tolas for silver.
                • Injunction of utilization of zakat is in Surah-al Tauba.
                • Zakāt or "alms giving", one of the five Pillars of Islam, is the giving 2.5% of one's possessions  to charity, normally to the terrible and needy.
                325

                How many times command of the Namaz is given in the Quran?

                Correct Answer: 700 Times
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Namaz commanded in Quran for 700 times.

                Salat made compulsory in 10th Hijri.

                Salat Juma became Farz in Madina.

                32 times the word Zakat occurs in the Holy Quran.

                22 times word Zakat is used in Makki Surahs.

                326

                How many Muslims/Sahaba (RA) Martyred in Battle of Badr?

                Correct Answer: 14
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                14 Muslims were killed in the Battle of Badr.


                It was fought on 624 CE (17 Ramadan, 2 AH), near the city of Badr.


                It was the first battle of Islam 


                All important questions and information related to battle of Badr

                • Badr means in Arabic: Full moon
                • Furqan, the second name of the battle of Badr
                • Abu Jehal was the leader of the kufars in the Battle of Badar.
                • 70 infidels were killed in Ghazwa Badr
                • It is also referred to as The Day of the Criterion.
                • No of the Kufars:  1000
                • Mentioned in Surat Al-Anfal 
                • 1000 angels participated in the Battle of Badr

                Total: Muslim 313-317

                Muhajirun: 82-85

                Ansar: 231

                Aws: 61

                Khazraj: 170

                2 horses

                70 camels

                **

                ND23-2-2023

                327

                Treaty of Sevres was signed in ________?

                Correct Answer: 1920
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Treaty of Sèvres was signed in August 10, 1920.

                It was an agreement between the Allied Powers and the Ottoman Empire after World War I.

                The treaty aimed to divide Ottoman territories and limit its power.


                Additional Details / Facts

                It was never fully implemented due to the Turkish War of Independence.

                Later replaced by the Treaty of Lausanne (1923).

                Key loss for the Ottoman Empire included lands in the Middle East and Anatolia.

                328

                Who wrote the book ‘Hujjat Allah Al Baligha’?

                Correct Answer: Shah Wali Ullah
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Conclusive Argument From God (Hujjat Allah al Baligha)

                The Conclusive Argument of God is the masterwork of Shah Wallah of Delhi (1762)

                Originally published on: 1979

                Author of Hujjat Allah al Baligha: Shah Waliullah Dehlawi

                Editor of Hujjat Allah al Baligha : Marcia Hermansen

                ****

                nd9-2-2023



                329

                The shortest Surah of the Holy Quran is _____?

                Correct Answer: Kausar
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Surah Al Kawthar is the shortest surah of Holy Quran

                (3 Verses).


                The Surah Baqarah is longest                 surah of Holy Quran

                (286 Verses).

                *********

                330

                What is the distance between the Earth and the first heaven according to Hadith?

                Correct Answer: 500 years of travel
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                According to Hadith, the distance to the first heaven is described metaphorically as 500 years of travel, indicating its vastness.

                This description appears in authentic narrations to convey the immense separation between Earth and the heavens.

                331

                Which Prophet’s nation was destroyed by a powerful sound (Sayhah)?

                Correct Answer: Prophet Saleh (AS)
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The nation of Thamud, to whom Prophet Saleh (AS) was sent, was destroyed by a powerful sound (Ṣayḥah).

                This punishment is mentioned in the Qur’an as a divine consequence of their disobedience.

                332

                What did Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) say is the fastest way to enter Paradise?

                Correct Answer: Being kind to parents
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) emphasized that kindness and obedience to parents is a direct means to attain Paradise.

                It is considered one of the greatest acts of worship and moral duty in Islam.

                333

                What is the punishment for theft in Islam (under strict conditions)?

                Correct Answer: Cutting of the hand
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Under Shariah law, theft is punishable by amputation of the hand if strict conditions are met.

                These conditions include minimum value of stolen property, security of the item, and absence of necessity or coercion.

                334

                Which companion of the Holy Prophet (SAW) were included in the scribes of revelation?

                Correct Answer: Hazrat Zaid bin Sabit (RA)
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Hazrat Zaid bin Sabit (RA) was one of the closest companions of the Holy Prophet (SAW) and was included among the scribes of revelation.

                He was responsible for writing down the divine revelations received by the Prophet (SAW).

                He was known for his excellent memory, literacy, and dedication to the Prophet's missio

                n.

                335

                The "Farewell Sermon" was delivered during which Islamic month?

                Correct Answer: Dhul-Hijjah
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The Prophet (PBUH) delivered his final sermon during the Hajj on Mount Arafat.
                • It is also known as Farewell Sermon.
                • The Holy Prophet (SAW) performed Hajjat-ul-Wida in the 10th Hijri.
                • It was performed on Friday, the 9th of Dhu al-Hijjah (March 6, 632 A.D).
                • This final pilgrimage included his renowned Farewell Sermon.
                336

                Who was the first child born after the migration (hijrah)?

                Correct Answer: Abdul bin Zubair (R.A)
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Abd Allah ibn al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam born May 624

                Abd Allah ibn al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam was the leader of a caliphate based in Mecca that rivaled the Umayyads from 683 until his death.

                Burial Jannat al-Mu'alla, Mecca

                ND14-7-2023

                337

                Which companion was appointed as the first chief justice (Qadi) of Islam?

                Correct Answer: Umar ibn al-Khattab
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                During Caliph Abu Bakr’s (RA) time, judicial matters were mostly handled personally by the Caliph.

                Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA) was later appointed to establish the formal office of Qadi, founding the Islamic judicial system.

                338

                What is the name of month after Ramadan in Islamic calendar?

                Correct Answer: Shawwal
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Shawwal is the 10th month of the Islamic lunar calendar, immediately after Ramadan.

                Eid-ul-Fitr is celebrated on the 1st of Shawwal.

                List of Islamic Months

                1. Muḥarram, محرم الحرام
                2. Ṣafar, صفر المظفر
                3. Rabi al Awwal ربیع الاول
                4. Rabi al Sani ربیع الثانی
                5. Jumada al Awwal جمادی الاول
                6. Jumada al Thani جمادی الثانی
                7. Rajab, رجب المرجب
                8. Shabanشعبان المعظم
                9. Ramadan(fasting), رمضان المبارک
                10. Shawwal شوال المکرم
                11. Dhu al Qadah ذوالقعدۃ الحرام
                12. Dhu al Hijjah ذوالحجۃ الحرام:

                **** 


                339

                Which Hadith collection is uniquely arranged by legal themes (not chapters)?

                Correct Answer: Muwatta Malik
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Muwatta Malik is arranged by legal (Fiqh) topics, not by chronological order.

                It focuses on practical application of Hadith in law and daily life.

                340

                Who was the commander who rebuilt the Kaaba before the Prophet's (PBUH) mission?

                Correct Answer: Abdul Muttalib
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Abdul Muttalib, the grandfather of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH), rebuilt the Kaaba before the Prophet’s mission.

                He ensured the proper placement of the Black Stone during reconstruction.

                341

                Who led Muslims in the Battle of Yarmouk?

                Correct Answer: Khalid bin al-Waleed (RA)
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • Battle of Yarmouk: Fought between the Byzantine Empire and Muslim forces under Khalid bin Waleed.
                  • Dates: August 15 to August 20, 636 AD.
                  • Location: Near the Yarmouk River.
                  • Caliphate: During the reign of Hazrat Umar (RA).
                  • Result: Decisive victory for the Muslims, marking the end of Byzantine rule in Syria.
                  342

                  The term 'Fard Kifayah' (Communal Obligation) is best exemplified by which of the following acts?

                  Correct Answer: Participating in a funeral prayer (Salat al-Janaza)
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Fard Kifayah (Communal Obligation) is a duty that is obligatory on the Muslim community as a whole.

                  The obligation is fulfilled if a sufficient number of individuals within that community carry it out.

                  If enough people perform the act, the rest of the community is absolved of the duty.

                  A prime example is the Salat al-Janaza (funeral prayer).

                  Once a group of Muslims performs the funeral prayer for the deceased, the obligation is met for the entire community.

                  343

                  The first Islamic capital established outside the Arabian Peninsula was Kufa, founded during the early Caliphate in which modern-day country?

                  Correct Answer: Iraq
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Kufa (Al-Kufah) was one of several amsar (garrison cities) founded by the Rashidun Caliphate outside the Arabian Peninsula.

                  It was established in Iraq in 638 AD by order of Caliph Umar Ibn al-Khattab.

                  344

                  The Battle of Al-Qadisiyyah (636 AD) was the decisive Muslim victory that led to the collapse of which major empire?

                  Correct Answer: Sasanian (Persian) Empire
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Battle of Al-Qadisiyyah (636 AD) was a pivotal confrontation between the forces of the Rashidun Caliphate and the Sasanian Empire.

                  Led by General Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, the Muslim army secured a decisive victory over the Persians, who were commanded by the legendary general Rostam Farrokhzad. 

                  345

                  A land tax imposed on the non-Muslims cultivators and landlords is known as ______?

                  Correct Answer: Kharaj
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • A land Tax imposed on the non-Muslim cultivator and landlords was known as Kharag.
                  • ***
                  • Kharāj (Arabic: خراج) is a type of individual Islamic tax on agricultural land and its products, developed under Islamic law. 
                  • Kharaj was originally a property tax paid only by non-Muslims, but Muslims had to pay a "tithe".
                  • But finally, the term  Kharaj refers to all taxes paid to Islamic authorities.
                  • *********
                  • The list of taxes imposed under Firoz Shah Tughlaq is as follows: Kharaj: land tax which was equal to one-tenth of the produce of the land.
                  • ***
                  • Jizyah, historically, a tax paid by certain non-Muslim minorities to rulers in Muslim states.
                  • ***

                  • VU FIA UDC CONSTABLE PAST PAPER 31-12-2021
                  346

                  The specific term for the part of a Hadith that contains the actual saying or action of the Prophet (PBUH) is ______?

                  Correct Answer: Matn (Text/Content)
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  A Hadith consists of two primary components.

                  The Matn is the actual body of the text, describing the specific words, actions, or silent approvals of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

                  It is the core message that conveys the religious or legal guidance.

                  347

                  The philosophical concept of "Ijaz al-Qur'an" refers to the Qur'an's _____?

                  Correct Answer: Inimitability (miraculous nature)
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  I'jaz al-Qur'an (Arabic: إعجاز القرآن) is an Islamic philosophical and theological concept that refers to the inimitability of the Quran.

                  It asserts that the Quran's content, style, linguistic beauty, structure, and depth are so unique and perfect that no human or group of humans can ever produce a text like it.

                  This quality is considered a form of divine miracle, serving as proof of its divine origin and the authenticity of the prophethood of Muhammad.

                  348

                  The Islamic financial partnership known as 'Musharakah' involves profit and loss sharing, where capital is contributed by ____?

                  Correct Answer: Both partners
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Musharakah (Arabic: مشاركة, meaning "sharing") is an essential concept in Islamic finance that represents a joint partnership or a joint venture.

                  It is a structure where all partners contribute capital, and they agree to share both the profits and any potential losses of the enterprise.

                  The key principles of Musharakah are based on equity and justice:

                  Profit Sharing: Profits are distributed among partners based on a pre-agreed ratio (which does not necessarily have to match the capital contribution ratio).

                  Loss Sharing: Losses, however, must be shared strictly in proportion to each partner's capital contribution.

                  349

                  Which famous Muslim explorer and traveler covered nearly the entire Islamic world and parts of China and India in the 14th century?

                  Correct Answer: Ibn Battuta
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Ibn Battuta (1304–1368) was a Moroccan scholar and explorer who is widely recognized as one of the greatest travelers in history.

                  Over a period of nearly 30 years starting in 1325.

                  He traveled approximately 75,000 miles (117,000 km).

                  He covering almost the entire Islamic world (Dar al-Islam) and beyond.

                  His journeys took him through North Africa, West Africa, Southern and Eastern Europe, the Middle East, India, Southeast Asia, and China.

                  His accounts were later compiled into a famous travelogue titled the Rihla.

                  350

                  The famous Muslim historian and sociologist, known for his work Al-Muqaddimah, which laid the groundwork for modern historiography, was ______?

                  Correct Answer: Ibn Khaldun
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Ibn Khaldun (1332–1406) was a pioneering North African scholar often referred to as the father of modern sociology, historiography, and economics.

                  His most famous work, the Al-Muqaddimah (The Introduction).

                  It was written in 1377 as the preface to his planned history of the world.

                  351

                  Who was the only Caliph to rule during the entire period of the Rashidun Caliphate (632-661 AD) without facing any internal civil wars?

                  Correct Answer: Umar Ibn al-Khattab
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Umar Ibn al-Khattab (634–644 AD) ruled during the Rashidun Caliphate without any internal civil wars.

                  His period is known for strong administration, justice, and stability within the Muslim state.

                  Major internal conflicts like the Ridda Wars occurred during Abu Bakr’s rule, and the First Fitna began in Uthman’s time—not during Umar’s caliphate.

                  352

                  The legal principle of 'istihsan' (Juristic Preference), used when Qiyas (analogy) leads to an unfair result, is most closely associated with the methodology of which legal school?

                  Correct Answer: Hanafi
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Istihsan (Juristic Preference) is a key principle of the Hanafi school, used to avoid hardship or unfair outcomes.

                  It allows departure from strict Qiyas (analogy) when justice and public interest require it.

                  Imam Abu Hanifa emphasized istihsan to ensure flexibility and practicality in Islamic law.

                  353

                  Which of the following is the Islamic philosophical term for the 'oneness of creation' or the unity of all existence?

                  Correct Answer: Wahdat al-Wujud
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Wahdat al-Wujud (Arabic: وحدة الوجود) literally means "Unity of Existence" or "Unity of Being".

                  This is a central philosophical concept in Islamic mysticism (Sufism) which posits a non-dualistic view where all existence is fundamentally one and a manifestation or expression of the Divine, meaning there is no true separation between the Creator (God) and the creation.

                  The perceived multiplicity of things is seen as different aspects or reflections of the singular ultimate reality, which is God.

                  354

                  After the Battle of Hunayn, the Muslims established supremacy over which region?

                  Correct Answer: Western Arabia
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The battle itself took place in the Hunayn valley, located in Western Arabia between Mecca and Taif. 

                  The Battle of Hunayn was fought against the Bedouin tribe of in 630 CE (8 AH)

                  It was fought between the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) and his companions against the Hawazin and Thaqif tribes.

                  It took place shortly after the conquest of Makkah.

                  355

                  The standardized Quranic text compiled under the caliphate of Uthman ibn Affan to resolve disputes is called?

                  Correct Answer: Mushaf-e-Uthmani
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The standardized Quranic text compiled under the caliphate of Uthman ibn Affan is known as the Uthmanic codex/ Mushaf Uthmani

                  The Uthmanic codex is the only edition of the Quran compiled by the third Rashidun caliph Uthman ibn Affan.

                  He ordered it to be copied and sent to Islamic countries after the death of Muhammad.

                  The Quran was collected in a single book by order of the first caliph Abu Bakr al-Siddiq.

                  356

                  When does the time for Fajr prayer begin?

                  Correct Answer: At dawn (Subh Sadiq)
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Fajr prayer time begins at Subh Sadiq (true dawn), when light first appears on the horizon, and ends at sunrise.

                   Indicators of Fajr Time:

                  True Dawn (Al-Fajr Al-Sadiq):

                  • A horizontal, widespread glow on the horizon that grows brighter.

                  False Dawn (Al-Fajr Al-Kadhib):

                  • A vertical, fleeting light that appears before true dawn and is followed by darkness, which is not the start of Fajr time. 
                  357

                  Maozotain means:

                  Correct Answer: Surah Falaq And Surah Naas
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Maozotain means:  Surah Falaq And Surah Naas

                  ***

                  The last two Qur’an surahs are called surah Mauzatain (al-Muʽawwidhatan), meaning the two [surahs] seeking refuge. 

                  The benefit of Surah Nass the reciter will be safe for a whole night from every evil.

                  The benefits of Surah Falaq is asking Allah to protect us. 

                  Sh4-9-2023


                  358

                  Which region was conquered by Saad bin Abi Waqqas (RA)?

                  Correct Answer: Persia
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Saad ibn Abi Waqqas (RA) wasresponsible for the conquest of Persia (Iran) and Iraq.

                  He was the supreme commander of the Rashidun army

                  He was the founder of Kufa and served as its governor under Umar ibn al-Khattab. 

                  He was the seventh free adult man to embrace Islam, which he did at the age of seventeen.

                  His most famous military engagement was the Battle of al-Qadisiyyah (636 CE),

                  359

                  Which Ayam are called Ayam Madoodat?

                  Correct Answer: Days of Tashreeq
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation


                  Ayam Madoodat refers to the three days after Eid-ul-Adha during which Tashreeq Takbeer is recited.


                  Uqbah bin Amir (R) said: The Messenger of Allahﷺsaid:

                  The Day of Arafah, the Day of Nahr, and the Days of Tashriq (11th, 12th, and 13th of Dhul-Hijjah) are Eid for us. The people of Islam, and they are days of eating and drinking,

                  Jami at-Tirmidhi 773, Sunan Ibn Majah Vol 1: 1720 and Sunan an Nasai 3007

                  360

                  Hazrat Saad Bin Abi Waqqas was made the governor of Kufa during the Khilafat of?

                  Correct Answer: Umar Farooq (R.A)
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  Saad Ibn Abi Waqqas was the one who built the city of Kufa in Iraq
                  During the rule of Umar ibn al-Khattab.
                  Saad also fought under ʿUmar's command against the Sassanid army at the Battle of al-Qādisiyyah and the Battle of Nahavand.
                  He was later appointed governor of Kufa and Nejd during the caliphate of Umar.
                  361

                  Who was the last prophet of Bani Israel?

                  Correct Answer: Hazrat Isa
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  حضرت عیسیٰ (علیہ السلام) بنی اسرائیل کے آخری نبی سمجھے جاتے ہیں۔

                  ان کے بعد کوئی نبی بنی اسرائیل کی طرف نہیں بھیجا گیا۔

                  Hazrat Isa (As) is known Rohullah

                  The holy book Injeel was revealed on Hazrat Esa As

                  362

                  What is the Islamic way of giving wealth to the poor?

                  Correct Answer: Zakat
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Zakat is obligatory charity in Islam.

                  It is fixed at 2.5% on eligible wealth held for one lunar year.

                  It is distributed among 8 categories of beneficiaries mentioned in Surah At-Taubah (9:60).

                  It is mainly the poor and needy.

                  363

                  The parts of each of the Hadis are Sanad and________?

                  Correct Answer: Matan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • The parts of each of the Hadis are 
                    • Sanad/Isnad: The chain of narrators documenting the report's transmission.
                    • Matn: The actual text or message of the Hadith


                  Last updated question 2 May 2026 by NS

                   

                  364

                  Siddique and______the title given to Abu bakkar Siddique?

                  Correct Answer: Ateeque
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Saddique and Ateeque the title given to Abu Bakkar Siddique:
                  • The first Caliph of Islam.
                  • Al-Siddiq means "the truthful."
                  • Atiq means "the one set free" or "noble". 
                  • Abu Bakr Burial in Al-Masjid an-Nabawi, Medina
                  • A title conferred by Prophet Muhammad after Abu Bakr, who immediately believed in the story of Isra and Miraj.

                  Last updated by NA on 26 April 2026

                  365

                  When Zakat become obligatory?

                  Correct Answer: 2 AH
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  A related question about Zakat

                  Zakat is the 3rd fundamental pillar of Islam.

                  Zakat was made compulsory in 2 A.H but its complete system was imposed in the year of 8th hijrah.

                  The word Zakat occurs 32 times in the Holy Quran.


                  **

                  How many camels render are to par Zakat? 5

                  How many goats render are to pay zakat? 40

                  How many cows render are to pay Zakat? 30-40

                  How many bulls render to pay Zakat? 30-40

                  How many buffaloes render to pay zakat? 30-40

                  **

                  Zakat is payable on gold of_________7.5 tolas

                  Zakat is payable on silver of_______52.5 tolas


                  How many times word Zakat is used in Makki Surahs? 22 times

                  Who is not entitled to get Zakat? Parents, Husband, wife, and children

                  **

                  ***

                  ND28-1-2023

                  366

                  Hijri calendar was started in :

                  Correct Answer: 622AD
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Hijri calendar was started in 622AD

                  Islamic Calendar starts from Hijri

                  Islamic calendar officially established by: Hazrat Umar (R.A) ibn al-Khattab.

                  The Prophet's migration or hijra in 622AD marks the beginning of the Hijri year calendar and gives the calendar its name.

                  The Islamic calendar is based on 12 lunar months 


                  یہ سوال ضرور پڑھیں

                  Current Hijri Year is ______?
                  367

                  Who purchased the well of Ruma and declared it as a trust?

                  Correct Answer: Hazrat Usman
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Uthman bin Affan bought the well and gifted it to Muslims and declared it as a Waqf.


                  368

                  وہ لفظ جسے زور لگا کر دو بار پڑھا جاتا ہے کیا کہلاتا ہے؟

                  Correct Answer: شَدّ
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  شَدّ میں حرف کو زور دے کر دو مرتبہ پڑھا جاتا ہے۔

                  مثال: مدّ، حقّ، شدّت

                  369

                  Which Surah provides guidance about the distribution of Mal-e-Ghanimat (war booty)?

                  Correct Answer: Surah Al-Anfal
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The guidance about the distribution of Mal-e-Ghanimat (war booty) is provided in Surah Al-Anfal, Ayat 41

                  The very name Al-Anfal means "The Spoils of War,"

                  370

                  مسیلمہ کذاب کے خلاف کون سی جنگ لڑی گئی؟

                  Correct Answer: جنگِ یمامہ
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  جنگِ یمامہ کی تفصیل

                  مسلمانوں کی قیادت حضرت خالد بن ولیدؓ نے کی۔

                  یہ جنگ بہت سخت اور خونی تھی۔

                  اس جنگ میں

                  بارہ سو کے قریب صحابہ کرامؓ شہید ہوئے۔

                  ان میں سے بڑی تعداد قرّاء (قرآن حافظ) کی تھی۔

                  اسی نقصان کی وجہ سے حضرت عمرؓ نے حضرت ابوبکرؓ کو قرآن جمع کرنے پر زور دیا۔

                  371

                  Stones for Ramy-e-Jamarat are picked up from?

                  Correct Answer: Muzdalifah
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  • Sayee is commenced from Safa and ends at Marwa.

                  • After performing Say’ee Hujjaj go to Mina.

                  • Muzdalfa is a plain.

                  • Muzdalfa is located b/w Mina & Arafat.

                  • Muzdalfa is located six miles from Makkah.

                  • From Mina Muzdalfa is three miles away.

                  • Muzdalfa is called Sacred Monument in Quran.

                  • At Muzdalfa Maghrib & Isha prayers are offered together.

                  • Pebbles are collected from Muzdalfa.

                  • Jamarat which is nearest to Makkah is called Jamarat-ul-Uqba.

                  • Smallest Jamarat is Jamarat-al-Sughra.

                  • Rami is held at Mina.

                  • Talbiah is stopped after Rami.

                  372

                  What was the main reason Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) remained monogamous with Hazrat Khadija (R.A)?

                  Correct Answer: Love & Loyalty
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Prophet (SAW) had deep love and respect for Hazrat Khadija (R.A).

                  His monogamy reflected loyalty and commitment throughout her lifetime.

                  373

                  Talah bin Ubaida became the shelter of Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) in which Ghazwa?

                  Correct Answer: Ghazwa-e-Uhad
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Talah bin Ubaida protected the Prophet (SAW) during the Battle of Uhud.

                  His bravery ensured the safety of the Prophet in a critical moment of the battle.

                  374

                  Egypt was conquered in which era?

                  Correct Answer: Hazrat Umar (RA)
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Egypt was conquered during the caliphate of Hazrat Umar (RA) in 640 CE.

                  This conquest expanded the Islamic empire into North Africa.

                  375

                  Where are the Zohr and Asr prayers performed together?

                  Correct Answer: Arafat
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Pilgrims perform the Zuhr and Asr two prayers together at noon time at Masjid al-Namirah on the Day of Arafah. on the 9th of Dhul Hijjah.

                  This is known as "standing before God" (wuquf) and is one of the most important rites of Hajj.

                  376

                  In which direction of the Kaaba is the Ruku-e-Yamani located?

                  Correct Answer: South
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Rukan Yamani is in the south-western corner of the Holy Kabah, located in Makkah,

                  It can be found on the right side of Hajr-e-Aswad

                  377

                  How many obligatory in umrah?

                  Correct Answer: 2
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  TIn Umrah, there are 2 main obligations (Farz/Wajib):

                  1. Ihram (intention and state of ritual purity) – Entering the state of Ihram from the Miqat (designated boundary).

                  2. Tawaf (circumambulation of Kaaba) – Performing 7 circuits around the Kaaba.


                  378

                  From which month do flights start for Hajj pilgrims?

                  Correct Answer: Zulqadah
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Hajj flights typically begin in the Islamic month of Dhul-Qi'dah (Dhu al-Qidah), usually starting around the beginning of that month (1st Dhul-Qi'dah) and running through the first few days of Dhul-Hijjah

                  379

                  What is ruling of perfume while in state of Ihram?

                  Correct Answer: Prohibited
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  While in Ihram for Hajj or Umrah, using perfume, scented soaps, lotions, or any fragrant cosmetics is prohibited for both men and women as it distracts from spiritual focus



                  380

                  What are the days of Tawaf al-Ifadah?

                  Correct Answer: 10-13 Zulhajjah
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Tawaf al-Ifadah (the mandatory Hajj Tawaf) is best on the 10th (Eid day) but can be done from the 10th to the 13th (Days of Tashreeq). 

                  381

                  How many days did Holy Prophet's PBUH spend at Abu Ansari (A.S) house?

                  Correct Answer: Seven month
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Prophet (peace be upon him) stayed at Abu Ayub's house for about seven months

                  until the Prophet's Mosque was built on the open space where his camel had stopped.

                  The camel stopped at the house of Abu Ayyub al-Ansari, a member of the Banu Najjar, who was regarded as the best tribe of Medina.

                  Abu Ayyub was born in Medina, Hejaz as Khalid bin Zayd to a Najjar family of the Banu Khazraj.

                  ND-21-5-2023

                  382

                  Who is Hazrat Sobia (RA)?

                  Correct Answer: Mother of Hazrat Hamza (RA)
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  •  Hazrat Sobia (RA) was the Mother of Hazrat Hamza (RA)
                  • ****
                  •  Sobia was the maidservant of Prophet (PBUH) uncle Abu Lahab, who was the first one to have the honor of feeding the Prophet (PBUH).
                  • The Prophet liked her so much and was grateful to her and her children till she died in the seventh year A.H (after Hijra)
                  383

                  The Alhambra palace is located in ______?

                  Correct Answer: Spain
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  On a hill overlooking the Spanish city of Granada stands the Alhambra, a palace built by princes belonging to the Muslim Nasrid dynasty (1238–1492) in the 14th century.

                  384

                  Masjid-e-Nimra is situated in _____?

                  Correct Answer: Plains of Arafat
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Masjid-e-Nimra is situated in the Plains of Arafat, Makkah.
                  • Pilgrims gather at Masjid-e-Nimra on the 9th day of Dhu al-Hijjah.
                  • It is also known as the "Musalla".
                  • The Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) delivered his Farewell Sermon at this location.
                  • Pilgrims perform Dhuhr and Asr prayers combined at Masjid-e-Nimra during Hajj.
                  385

                  How far is Mina and from Arafat ( As given in Rehnuma e Hajj booklet (Hanifia/Jafria))?

                  Correct Answer: 13 km
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The distance between Mina and the Plain of Arafat is approximately 13 kilometers.



                  386

                  Which place is NOT the part of Hajj Move? Mina, Arafat, Muzdalifah, Taif

                  Correct Answer: Taif
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Taif is NOT a core site for the main Hajj rituals

                  Taif:

                  A beautiful city in the mountains near Mecca, often a summer retreat, but not a mandatory Hajj site for core rites.

                  Mina:

                  Where pilgrims stay, perform stoning of the devil (Ramy al-Jamarat).

                  Arafat:

                  The peak of Hajj, where pilgrims spend the afternoon in prayer (Wuquf).

                  Muzdalifah:

                  Where pilgrims gather pebbles after Arafat.

                  387

                  From which point does Tawaf in Masjid al-Haram start?

                  Correct Answer: Hajr-e-Aswad
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Tawaf starts from Hajr-e-Aswad (the Black Stone)

                  ANSWER ON 

                  Hajj-2025_Training_Guide slide no 114 (Umrah stages)


                  388

                  From where do Hajj rituals officially start?

                  Correct Answer: Miqat
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  حج کے مراسم باضابطہ طور پر حاجیوں کے مخصوص حدودی مقامات  پر احرام کی حالت میں داخل ہونے سے شروع ہوتے ہیں، جو مکہ کے باہر واقع ہوتے ہیں۔

                  Miqat (Ihram):

                  The first official act is entering the state of Ihram (sacred purity) at the Miqat, a boundary around Mecca, signifying the spiritual start.

                  Mecca (Tawaf):

                  Pilgrims then proceed to Mecca to perform the Tawaf al-Qudum (arrival circumambulation) around the Kaaba.

                  Mina (Day of Tarwiyah)

                  The primary Hajj sequence begins on the 8th of Dhul-Hijjah when pilgrims travel from Mecca to the tent city of Mina, spending the night in prayer and preparation for the Day of Arafat, which is considered the most crucial part of Hajj. 

                  389

                  Who is buried alongside Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in Roza-e-Rasool?

                  Correct Answer: Both Hazrat Abu Bakr and Hazrat Umar رضي الله عنه
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) is buried in the Sacred Chamber alongside the first two Rashidun Caliphs, Abu Bakr al-Siddiq (RA) and Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA), in the Prophet's Mosque (Masjid an-Nabawi) in Medina.

                  390

                  Who is typically the competent authority for approving bills related to Hajj policy?

                  Correct Answer: Ministry of Religious Affairs
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Ministry of Religious Affairs and Interfaith Harmony (MoRA) is the competent authority for approving bills related to Hajj policy in Pakistan.

                  391

                  How many days short hajj scheme consist of?

                  Correct Answer: 20-25 days
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The short package under the Government Hajj Scheme is designed to last for 20-25 days.

                  392

                  On which day of Dhul Hijjah is Nahr (sacrificial slaughter) performed?

                  Correct Answer: 10th
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Nahr is performed on the 10th of Dhul Hijjah (Eid al-Adha)

                  It is known as Yawm al-Nahr (the Day of Sacrifice). This day marks the beginning of the Eid al-Adha festival

                  11th, 12th, and 13th of Dhul Hijjah (Ayyam al-Tashreeq): 

                  393

                  How many days does the Hajj Pilgrimage last?

                  Correct Answer: Five days
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                • Hajj lasts for 5 days, from the 8th to the 12th of Dhul-Hijjah in the Islamic calendar.
                • The pilgrimage includes rituals like Tawaf, Sa'i, Arafat, Muzdalifah, and stoning at Mina.
                  • 394

                  Hajar-e-Aswad’s direction is toward:

                  Correct Answer: Eastern
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The picture above shows the Hajar al-Aswad (The Black Stone), which is set in the eastern corner of the Ka'bah.

                  حج کے متعلق ان سولات کو ضرور پڑھیں

                  Click here

                  395

                  The Khutba of Hajj (Hajj sermon) is delivered in which mosque?

                  Correct Answer: Masid-e-Nimra
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Hajj sermon, also known as the Khutbat al-Wada or Farewell Sermon,

                  It is delivered at Masjid an-Namirah on Mount Arafat during the day of Arafah.

                  396

                  Which app is used for Hajj info?

                  Correct Answer: Pak Hajj App
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                • Pak Hajj App provides information and guidelines for Pakistani pilgrims performing Hajj.
                • It offers features like tracking, complaints, route maps, and guidelines.
                  • 397

                  Which type of vaccine is required for Hajj pilgrims?

                  Correct Answer: Meningitis vaccine
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Quadrivalent meningococcal conjugate (ACYW135) vaccine is required for Hajj pilgrims.

                  The vaccine must be administered no more than five years before arrival in Saudi Arabia

                  398

                  What is Arafat?

                  Correct Answer: Plain
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Arafat refers to the plain of Arafat.
                  • It is a flat and open area located near Mecca, where pilgrims gather on the 9th day of the Islamic month of Dhu al-Hijjah.
                  • Standing at Arafat is a crucial ritual during Hajj, symbolizing unity and equality in Islam.
                  399

                  How many Rukus are there in the Holy Quran?

                  Correct Answer: 558
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • The total number of Rukus in the Holy Quran is 558/540.
                    • The number of verses in the Quran is 6,666/6,236.
                    • The total number of Surahs in the Holy Quran is 114.
                    • The Medinan Surahs typically have more and longer Ayat.
                    • The Makki Surahs typically have shorter Ayat.
                    • The number of Madni Surahs is 28.
                    • Madani Surahs were revealed in 10 years.
                    • The number of Makki Surahs is 86.
                    • Makki Surahs were revealed in 13 years.
                    • There is Juz' 30 in the Quran.
                    • There are 14 Sajda Tilawat in the Quran.
                    400

                    Which Prophet is known as Jadd-ul-Anbiya?

                    Correct Answer: Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S)
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Hazarat Ibrahim A.s is the Abu ul Ambiya ( Father of Prophets

                    Hazrat Ibraheem is known as Jadd-ul-Anbiya.

                    The grave of Hazrat Ibraheem (علیہ) in Israel.

                    Hazrat Ibrahim (AH) name is mentioned in the Holy Quran 67 times.

                    Hazrat Ibraheem As had two wives Hazrat Hajra and Hazrat Sarah.

                    Hazrat Ibraheem As Had Two Sons Hazrat Ismail As And Hazrat Ishaq (As)

                    Hazrat Ibraheem (A.S) met with Holy Prophet ﷺ on the 7th heaven.

                    Hazrat Ibraheem (AS) was sent to Jorden after leaving Hazrat Hajirah and Hazrat Ismaeel in But’ha valley.


                    AGM 02.05.2023


                    401

                    Which of the following Prophets (AS) is known as Safiullah?

                    Correct Answer: Hazrat Adam (AS)
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    Hazrat Adam (AS) is known as Safiullah, meaning "the chosen one of Allah."
                    He is considered the first prophet in Islam and the father of mankind.
                    402

                    Where was Hazrat Shoaib (A.S) born?

                    Correct Answer: Midian
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Know about Hazrat Shoaib (AS)

                    Born: Midian 

                    Grave Location: Jordan

                    Father-in-law of Hazrat Moosa (AS)

                    Title: Speaker of Prophets 


                    403

                    Hazart Shoaib (AS) was the father-in-law of prophet:

                    Correct Answer: Hazrat Musa (AS)
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Know about Hazrat Shoaib (AS)

                    Born: Midian 

                    Grave Location: Jordan

                    Father-in-law of Hazrat Moosa (AS)

                    Title: Speaker of Prophets 


                    404

                    Fasting was made obligatory in ____?

                    Correct Answer: 10th Shaban, 2 A.H
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Fasting during the month of Ramadan was made obligatory (wajib) during the month of Sha'ban (8th Month)

                    Fasting was the 4th fundamental pillar of Islam

                    Fasting (Soam)  is declared as armor

                    Fasting was made obligatory in 10th Shaban,2 A.H

                    The function of fasting is To Make the Sacrifice of Body and Soul

                    Saum (fasting) resembles Hadith with a Shield

                    5 days are prohibited ممنوع for fasting throughout the Year

                    ND08-3-2023


                    405

                    Where is the grave of Hazrat Yahya (A.S)?

                    Correct Answer: Syria
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Hazrat Yahya buried ant The Umayyad Mosque, Damascus, Syria

                    MUG 10-12-2022
                    406

                    Who was a good Player of flute?

                    Correct Answer: Hazart Dawood(AS)
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                     Prophet Dawood (AS) is known for being a good player of flute 

                    Najibullah  is the title of Hazrat Dawood (AS) 

                    The holy book Zaboor revealed to Hazrat Dawood (AS)

                    Hazrat Dawood (AS) is buried in Israel.

                    Hazrat Dawood is called caliph of Allah.

                    Hazrat Dawood (AS) is mentioned in the Holy Quran for 16 times.

                    Bani Israel unanimously acknowledged Hazrat Dawood A.S as their king


                    407

                    How many surahs of the Holy Quran start with the word Sab-Baha?

                    Correct Answer: 3
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Surah start with Sab-Baha (سَبَّحَ) namely

                    1.      Al-Hadid,

                    2.      Al-Hashr and

                    3.      As-Saff

                    408

                    What is the real name of Hazrat Abdur Rehman(RA)?

                    Correct Answer: Abd Amr
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Hazrat Abdur Rahman bin Auf's (RA) original name before Islam was Abd Amr (or Abd al-Kabah), meaning "servant of Amr/Kabah,"

                    409

                    Hazrat Dawood (AS) is called?

                    Correct Answer: Caliph of Allah
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Najibullah  is the title of Hazrat Dawood (AS) 

                    The holy book Zaboor revealed to Hazrat Dawood (AS)

                    Hazrat Dawood (AS) is buried in Israel.

                    Hazrat Dawood is called caliph of Allah.

                    Hazrat Dawood (AS) is mentioned in the Holy Quran for 16 times.

                    Bani Israel unanimously acknowledged Hazrat Dawood A.S as their king



                    410

                    Which False Prophet Was Called The Liar By The Holy Prophet?

                    Correct Answer: Musailama al Kazzab
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Musaylimah fought and was killed in the Battle of Yamama by Wahshi ibn Harb,

                    the same man (Wahshi ibn Harb) who had killed Muhammad's uncle, Hamza, in the battle of Uhud before his conversion to Islam
                    411

                    The Umayyad Dynasty was put to an end in the year ________.

                    Correct Answer: 750 AD
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Umayyad Dynasty was overthrown and its rule in the Islamic Caliphate ended in 750 CE.

                    When the Abbasid Revolution successfully ousted them;

                    Umayyad rule continued in Spain (as the Emirate/Caliphate of Córdoba) until 1031 CE. 

                    412

                    How do the Nazim staff assist in the coordination of services with Saudi authorities during Hajj 2025?

                    Correct Answer: By ensuring compliance with local laws and regulations for the smooth operation of Hajj
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    By ensuring compliance with local laws and regulations for the smooth operation of Hajj
                    Nazim staff ensure that Pakistani pilgrims follow Saudi rules and regulations for a smooth and organized Hajj experience.

                    413

                    In case any Hujjaj is missing, to whom must Nazim convey the information?

                    Correct Answer: Sector Office, MCO, and OPAP
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    In case any Hujjaj is missing then convey to Sector Office, MCO and OPAP.

                    414

                    ______ was called month of migration.

                    Correct Answer: Rabiul Awal
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Rabiul Awal is called the month of migration.

                    This is the month when Prophet Muhammad (s.a.w.w) and his followers migrated from Mecca to Medina in June 622 CE.

                    Rabiul Awal is the 3rd Islamic month.

                    415

                    Which Caliph is credited with standardizing compilation of the Quran?

                    Correct Answer: Usman (RA)
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Caliph Usman (RA) was known for ordering the official compilation of the standardized version of the Quran, known as Uthman's Quran, 

                    416

                    Which Quranic Surah is name after a prophet and also contains stories of previous prophets?

                    Correct Answer: Ibrahim
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Surah Ibrahim is named after the Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham)

                    It includes stories of previous prophets,


                    - Surah Yousaf is named after Prophet Yusuf and mainly narrates his story.

                    - Surah Nuh is named after Prophet Nuh (Noah) and focuses on his story.

                    - Surah Musa is named after Prophet Musa (Moses) but primarily focuses on his story, not multiple prophets.


                    417

                    Which school of thought emphasizes strict adherence to Hadith in Islamic Jurisprudence?

                    Correct Answer: Hanbali
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                     The Hanbali school, founded by Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal,

                    It is known for its strict reliance on Hadith and minimal use of personal opinion (ray) or analogy (qiyas).

                    - It gives priority to authentic Hadiths, even over analogical reasoning.


                    418

                    Which term describes consensus of Muslim scholars on legal issues?

                    Correct Answer: Ijma
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Ijma al-Fuqaha refers to the consensus of scholars (fuqaha) on a particular legal issue

                    This is distinct from individual opinions or consensus within the larger ummah (community).

                    Additional information:

                    It can be operated where: The Quran and Hadith have not clarified a certain aspect of the Law

                    Ijma Literally, 'consensus' or 'agreement'.

                    419

                    Which surah is called The Constitution of the Muslim State?

                    Correct Answer: Maidah
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                     Surah Al-Maidah is often referred to as 'The Constitution of the Muslim State.'

                    Due to the comprehensive nature of its legal and ethical injunctions

                     Al-Maidah means "Meal" or "Banquet".

                    Hizb no: 11 to 13

                    No. of letters: 12206

                    No. of Rukus: 16

                    No. of verses: 120

                    No. of words: 2837

                    Other names: The Feast

                    420

                    Which principle allows a jurist to issue a legal opinion on reasoning and public welfare??

                    Correct Answer: Ijtihad
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Ijtihad is the process of making a legal decision by independent interpretation of the Quran and Sunnah

                    It allows scholars to apply reasoning for the public good (maslahah).

                    421

                    Which treaty allowed Muslims in Madina to coexist with non-Muslims?

                    Correct Answer: Madina
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The treaty that established coexistence between Muslims and non-Muslims in Medina was the Constitution of Medina


                    Constitution of Medina/Charter of Madina میثاق مدینہ 

                    Signed: First Hijri/622 AD 

                    Between: Muslims, Jews & other non-muslims 

                    Clauses: 47

                    Note: The Medina Charter consists of 47 clauses. 23 clauses governed the relationship between Muslims, between Ansar and Muhajirin, while the remaining 24 clauses governed the relations of Muslims with non-Muslims, the Jews, and others.

                    Misaq-e-Madina constituted a formal agreement between Prophet Muhammad(peace be upon himI and all the tribes and families of Yathrib (the old name for Medina) including

                    Muslims, Jews, Christians, and pagans.

                    422

                    Which caliph of the Muslims ordered the compilation of Ahadith and send to the conquered territories?

                    Correct Answer: Hazrat Umar bin abdul Aziz
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz, was the eighth Umayyad caliph.

                    He made various significant contributions and reforms to the society, and he has been described as and was called the first Mujaddid and sixth righteous caliph of Islam after Hasan Bin Ali according to some Muslim Scholars.

                    423

                    Which classical Islamic scholar is known for the work Al-Muwatta?

                    Correct Answer: Imam Malik
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Malik ibn Anas (Imam Malik ibn Anas)

                    Notable work(s) Al-Muwatta

                    Title: Shaykh al-Islam

                    Era: Late Umayyad – early Abbasid (Tabi al-Tabiin)

                    Died: 795 CE (179 AH; aged 83–84) Medina, Hejaz, Abbasid Caliphate 

                    Resting place: Al-Baqi Cemetery, Medina, Hejaz, Saudi Arabia

                    424

                    Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) gave the responsibility of compilation of the Holy Quran to?

                    Correct Answer: Hazrat Zid bin Thabit RA
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Hazrat Abu Bakr was a sahabi, one of the companions of Muhammad and the first Caliph of Islam.

                    He was also Muhammad's father-in-law through Aisha.

                    His real name was Abdullah or Abul-Kaaba and Abu Bakr was his kunya.

                    Hazrat Usman gave the idea to Hazrat Abu Bakar to collect the Quran in the form of a book

                    At the suggestion of Hazrat Umar (RA), Hazrat Abu Bakr ( R.A) entrusted the task of compilation of the holy Quran to Zaid bin Sabit (RA).

                    Holy Quran Collect in the reign of Hazrat Abu Bakr RA was done by Hazrat Zaid RA between 11 to 14 Hijri (632-34).

                    425

                    Which ministry is responsible for making Hajj arrangements according to the Government, Rules of Business 1973?

                    Correct Answer: Ministry of Religious Affairs & Interfaith Harmony
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The MORA&IH is responsible for organizing Hajj arrangements in Pakistan

                    According to the Government of Pakistan's Rules of Business, 1973

                    426

                    Correct Answer: 03
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    There are 11 Sajdah are there in Makkah Surah

                    There are only 03 Sajdah are there in Medinan Surah

                    427

                    In which month did Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) die?

                    Correct Answer: Rabi al-Awwal
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) passed away on 12th Rabi' al-Awwal, 11 AH.

                    This date corresponds to June 8, 632 CE in the Gregorian calendar.

                    Rabi' al-Awwal is the third month of the Islamic calendar.

                    428

                    Which prophet slept for 100 years?

                    Correct Answer: Hazrat Uzair (A.S)
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Uzair is introduced as one of the prophets of God sent to the Children of Israel.

                    The Quran makes mention of his name only once in surah Tawbah verse 30

                    The Holy Quran tells the story of Prophet Uzair عَلَيْهِ ٱلسَّلَامُ as a Prophet who died for 100 years and live again

                    ***

                    ND29-1-2023


                    429

                    Who proposed Azan for the first time?

                    Correct Answer: Hazrat Umer
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Hazrat 'Umar was the first person to suggest the calling of the Azan. Later, through a revelation, Allah gave permission to the Prophet (PBUH) to call out the Azan.
                    430

                    Abdur Rehman al Nasir assumed the title of Ameer ul Mominin in_____ ?

                    Correct Answer: 317
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Amir al-Mu'minin is an Arabic title that is usually translated "Commander of the Believers" or "Leader of the Faithful". It is also translated as "Prince of the Believers" since amir or emir is also used as a princely title in states ruled by the royalty or monarchies.

                    431

                    Hazrat Amna died at the way of-----?

                    Correct Answer: none
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Hazrat Amna is the Mother of the Prophet 

                    Born in Makkah

                    Died at Abwa when Holly Prophet PBUH was 6 years old.

                    She was a member of the Banu Zuhrah clan in the tribe of Quraysh 

                    ****

                    Al-Abwā' (Arabic: ٱَلْأَبْوَاء) is a Hejazi village between Mecca and Medina 

                    432

                    "Tarjuman-ul-Quran" was written by which of the following?

                    Correct Answer: Abul Kalam Azad
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    "Tarjuman-ul-Quran" is a translation and commentary of the Quran.

                    It from Arabic to Urdu by Abul Kalam Azad.

                    It is a notable work in Islamic scholarship.

                    433

                    The battle of Qudasiya was fought during the reign of _______?

                    Correct Answer: Umar Farooq (R.A)
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The battle of Qudasiya was fought during the reign of Umar Farooq (R.A)
                    • ***
                    • The battle of Qudasiya was fought between Umar (R.A) and Iran.
                    • This Battle was fought in Quads,
                    • Therefore its name is Battle of Qudasiya.

                    How many sons did Hazrat Adam (A.S) have?

                    Correct Answer: 3
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Hazrat Adam A.S had 3 sons and 2 daughters.

                    The youngest son of Hazrat Adam A.S was Prophet Shees.

                    **********


                    Adam was the first Muslim, as the Quran, states that all the Prophets preached the same faith of Islam.

                    Known for: First human being

                    Spouse(s): Hawwā (حواء)

                    The height of Hazrat Adam (AS) was 90 Feet

                    Hazrat Adam (AS) was 130 years of age when Shees alaihissalam was born

                    The language is spoken by Adam in the Garden of Eden.

                    What was the Religion of Hazrat Adam?

                    Islam✅

                    Hazrat Adam's (AS) total age ?

                    930 years✅

                    Hazrat Adam (A.S) met with Holy Prophet ﷺ on the ______ heaven?

                    1st✅

                    How many sons did Hazrat Adam (A.S) have?

                    3✅

                    Where is the grave of Hazrat Adam (علیہ)?
