                    ND5.3.2023



                    435

                    Who added the words Assalat-u-Khairum- Menun-Naum in the Fajr Azan?

                    Correct Answer: Hazrat Umar (RA)
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Assalatu Khairum Minan Naum means:

                    الصلاة خير من النوم [Assalatu Khairum Minan Naum] Prayer is better than sleep.

                    "assalatu khairum minan naum" (الصلوۃ خیر من النوم) is an exclusive part of call for morning prayer in Islam.
                    A formal call to prayer in Islam is called Azaan (اذان).
                    The morning prayer is called Fajr (فجر)

                    • Assalatu Khairum Minan Naum                       is used only for the Fajr prayer because it was included by                       Umar Ibn Al-Khattab in the Azan at Fajr.

                    Hazart Umar (RA): 

                     Umar was a leading companion and adviser to the Islamic prophet Muhammad, and became the second Muslim ruler after Muhammad's death and ruled for 10 years.

                    Converting to Islam in the 6th year after Muhammad's first revelation, he spent 18 years in companionship of Muhammad.

                    436

                    The Battle of Badr was fought in ____?

                    Correct Answer: 13 March 624 CE
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • "Badr" means "full moon" in Arabic.
                    • The Battle of Badr, also called Furqan.
                    • The Battle of Badr is also known as The Day of the Criterion.
                    • Day of the Criterion means کسوٹی کا دن
                    • It took place on Tuesday, 13 March 624 CE (17 Ramadan, 2 AH) near the city of Badr.
                    • Prophet Muhammad SAW, commanding his Sahaba, defeated the Quraysh army led by Abu Jahl (Amr ibn Hishām).
                    • The Kufar forces numbered 1,000.
                    • It was the first battle of Islam, with 313-317 Muslims.
                    • The Muslim forces included 82-85 Muhajirun and 231 Ansar (61 from Aws and 170 from Khazraj).
                    • They had 2 horses and 70 camels.
                    • Fourteen Muslims were killed, while 70 Kufar were killed and 70 captured.
                    • Surah Al-Anfal mentions that Allah promised to reinforce the Muslims with 1,000 angels.

                    437

                    Hazrat Abu Bkar (RA) died in:

                    Correct Answer: 13th Hijrah
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A) Born           27 October 573

                    Abu Bakr  was a sahabi, one of the companions of Muham

                    Abu Bakr  was a sahabi, one of the companions of Muhammad and the first Caliph of Islam.

                    He was also Muhammad's father-in-law through Aisha.

                    His real name was Abdullah or Abul-Kaaba and Abu Bakr was his kunya.

                    mad and the first Caliph of Islam.

                    He was also Muhammad's father-in-law through Aisha.

                    His real name was Abdullah or Abul-Kaaba and Abu Bakr was his kunya.


                    Hazrat AbuBakar (R.A) was the first while Hazrat Umer (R.A) was the second caliph of Islam

                    Abu Bakr Abdullah ibn Uthman was a companion and, through his daughter Aisha, a father-in-law of the Islamic prophet Muhammad, as well as the first of the Rashidun Caliphs.

                    The first and second Rashidun Caliphs, Abu Bakr (R.A) and Umar (R.A) are buried next to Muhammadﷺ.


                    438

                    Which wife of the holy prophet (PBUH) was alive at the time of Karbala tragedy?

                    Correct Answer: Hazrat Umm-e-Salma RA
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The last of Muhammad's wives, Umm Salama lived to hear about the tragedy of Karbala in 680, dying the same year.

                    Umm, Salama was one of the most influential wives of Hazrat Muhammad.

                    ***
                    The Battle of                     Karbala took place on Muharram 10, in the year 61 of the Islamic calendar.

                    439

                    Who Urged Hazrat Adam ( A.S ) to taste Prohibited Tree?

                    Correct Answer: Iblees
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Iblees, also known as Satan, was a jinn who had risen to a high rank and was given a place in the heaven. Also called Azazeel, he was made of smokeless fire. When God made Adam from clay, he asked all the angels to bow down before Adam.
                    440

                    Battle in which Holy Prophet ﷺ not participated is known as ______?

                    Correct Answer: Sariyya
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Sariyya  is the kind of battles in which the Prophet (s) sent a group led by one of the companions but he (s) did not participate in them. Such battles are also called as Baʿth.

                    ******
                    441

                    How many types of Tawaf are there____________?

                    Correct Answer: seven
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    There are seven types of Tawaf;

                    1.      Tawaf-ul-Qudoom:

                    It is performed when a person arrived at Makkah whether for Hajj, Umrah, Business or a visit.

                    2.      Tawaf-ul-Ziarah:

                    It is also called Tawaf-ul-Rukn or Tawaf-ul-Hajj. It is performed by all pilgrims who are undertaking the Hajj. This Tawaf is mandatory

                    3.      Tawaf-ul-Wada (Tawaf-e-Sadr):

                    It is a farewell Tawaf and performed as a final ritual of Hajj when a pilgrim leaving Makkah.

                    4.      Tawaf-ul-Nafl:

                    Tawaf-ul-Nafl is an optional tawaf but it is better than performing a nafl salaat. It can be performed at any time and as desired.

                    5.      Tawaf-ul-Umrah:

                    Tawaf-ul-Umrah is a part of Umrah and performed as a ritual of Umrah. It is obligatory for the one who performs Umrah.

                     

                    6.      Tawaf-ul-Nazr:

                    If a person pledged that he will perform Tawaf if his desire is fulfilled by Allah. Then, he must have to perform this Tawaf if Allah fulfills that desire.

                     

                    7.      Tawaf-ul-Tahiyaah:

                    After Tawaf, the person has to offer two units behind Maqam-e-Ibrahim (if possible, otherwise they may offer that two units prayer anywhere in the mosque).
                    442

                    What is the name of birth year of Hazarat Muhammad S.A.W?

                    Correct Answer: Year of Elephant
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Hazarat  Muhammad S.A.W was born in 571 A.D.

                    Last Prophet of Allah.

                    Mother: Hazarat Amina

                    Father : Hazrat Abdullah

                    *****

                    عام الفيل‎, Year of the Elephant, is the name in Islamic history for the year approximately equating to 570–571 CE. According to Islamic resources, it was in this year that Muhammad was born.
                    443

                    Which Prophet invented Soap?

                    Correct Answer: Prophet Saleh (A.S)
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    • Hazrat Saleh invented Soap.
                    • Hazrat Saleh was a prophet of the nation Samood.
                    • Hazrat Saleh is buried in Lebonan.
                    444

                    Hazrat ___________ added 2nd Azan for Friday prayers.

                    Correct Answer: Hazrat Usman (R.A)
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Hazrat Usman RA was the Companion of the Holy Prophet SAW who added/started the second Azan in Namaz-e-Jumma

                    Hazrat Usman bin 'Affan introduced the second Adhan on Fridays when the number of people in the mosque increased.

                    Previously the Adhan on Fridays used to be pronounced only after the Imam had taken his seat

                    The second Friday congregation prayer was offered at Jawatha Mosque, after Masjid an-Nabaw

                    the first Jummah prayer offered in Madinah

                    while

                    A minimum number of people is required for Friday prayers 2.

                    There are 14 Rakats in Jumma Namaz

                    ND2023-2-12

                    445

                    What was the length of the trench dug during the Battle of the Trench (Ghazwa-e-Ahzab)?

                    Correct Answer: 3 1/2 mile
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The trench dug by the Muslims during Ghazwa-e-Ahzab (Battle of the Trench) was approximately 3 1/2 miles long.

                    This trench was dug under the guidance of Hazrat Salman Farsi (RA) to protect the city of Madinah from the confederate army.

                    446

                    غزوہ خندق میں خندق کی کھدائی میں مجاہدین کی تعداد کتنی تھی؟

                    Correct Answer: 3000
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Battle of Khandaq (Ahzab) was fought on 5 A. H (627).

                    Ghazwa e Ahzab and Battle of the Trench is the second name of Ghazwa-e-Khandak 

                    Muslims 3000

                    Kufar 10000

                    10000 Munafiqeen were in Ghazwa-e-Khandaq

                    The length of trench was 5 kilometers.

                    Additional information

                    it was the 30-day-long fight of Yathrib by Arabian and Jewish tribes

                    Hazrat Salman Al-Farsi suggests digging the trench at the time of the Battle of Khandaq

                    ***

                    447

                    Where is the grave of Hazrat Yousaf (علیہ)?

                    Correct Answer: Palestine
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    According to Islamic tradition, the biblical Joseph is buried in Hebron, next to the Cave of the Patriarchs where a medieval structure known as Yussuf-Kalah, the "Castle of Joseph", is located
                    448

                    Hazrat Ali (R.A) was martyred in __________ Hijrah?

                    Correct Answer: 40
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Hazrat Ali (R.A) was attacked with a poisoned sword On 19th Ramazan 40th Hijri,

                    before the time of FAJAR prayers to the mosque of Kufa.

                    He died on the 21st of Ramadan 40 A.H or  28 January 661 CE.

                    He was buried in Najaf al-Ashraf (Iraq).

                    ***



                    449

                    What does Jasr (جسر) mean in Urdu?

                    Correct Answer: Bridges
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    جسر

                    bridge, embankment, viaduct, pier

                    450

                    Hajj is mandatory once in a lifetime for Muslims who are?

                    Correct Answer: Sahib-e-Istetaat
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Hajj is a sacred pilgrimage that is required of every Muslim at least once in their lifetimes

                    Hajj is a pilgrimage made to the Kaaba, the "House of Allah"

                    It is one of the Five Pillars of Islam

                    Verbal meaning of Hajj is The will to visit

                    There are 3 Farz, 10 Sunnat and 6 Wajibat in Hajj

                      Farz in Hajj are Three (3)

                      Farz in Umrah are Four (4)

                      There are three different types of Hajj namely:
                      Hajj-ul-Ifrad.
                      Hajj-ul-Qiran.
                      Hajj-ul-Tamattu.

                      451

                      Ministry of Religious Affairs has introduced _______ App for Hajj 2026.

                      Correct Answer: Pak Hajj App
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                       The Ministry of Religious Affairs of Pakistan introduced the "Pak Hajj 2026" app to help pilgrims prepare for the annual Islamic pilgrimage.

                      452

                      What does the Muhafiz Scheme provide compensation for?

                      Correct Answer: Death or Major/Minor Injury
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The scheme provides compensation for the following losses:

                      Compensation Details:

                      • 1. Death during Hajj in KSA: (2 Million)

                      • 2. Major/Minor Injury: As per details in the Hajj Policy Guidelines

                      • 3. Emergency evacuation without performing Hajj due to illness: Rs. 250,000

                      453

                      Who was the first wife of Prophet Hazrat Ibrahim?

                      Correct Answer: Hazrat Saarah
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Hazrat Saarah was the First wife of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S).

                      She was the mother of Hazrat Ishaq (A.S)

                      ****

                      Hazrat Hajirah was the second wife of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S).

                      She was the mother of Hazrat Ismail (A.S)


                      454

                      Which is the biggest Islamic country with respect to population?

                      Correct Answer: Indonesia
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Indonesia is the largest while Pakistan is the second largest Islamic country in the world with regard to population.

                      Overall, China is the largest, India second, USA third, Indonesia fourth & Pakistan is the fifth largest country in terms of population.

                      455

                      What does the term "Hypocrite" refer to?

                      Correct Answer: Double-faced
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      A hypocrite is someone who presents a false appearance of being virtuous or loyal.

                      But actually harbors different intentions or beliefs.

                      456

                      Namaz is the word of ______ language.

                      Correct Answer: Persian
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The word "namaz" is of Persian origin.

                      The Arabic word for prayer is "Salah". 

                      457

                      Haywaan (River of Life) is on which Heaven?

                      Correct Answer: 4th Heaven
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Haywaan (River of Life) is on the 4th heaven. 

                      Angel Jibreel (Gabriel) dips in this river, and from each drop that falls from his wings, a new angel is created. 

                      458

                      Hazrat Adam (A.S) met with Holy Prophet ﷺ on the ______ heaven?

                      Correct Answer: 1st
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Who met with Holy Prophet PBUH on the Haven?

                      1st Heaven met Adam (AS)

                      2nd Heaven met Isa (AS) and Yahya (AS)

                      3rd Heaven met Yusuf (AS)

                      4th Heaven met Idris (AS)

                      5th Heaven met Haroon (AS)

                      6th Heaven met Musa (AS)

                      7th Heaven met Ibrahim (AS)

                      ND5.3.2023

                      459

                      Who met with Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) on the 2nd heaven during Shab-e-Miraj?

                      Correct Answer: Both B and C
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Who met with Holy Prophet PBUH on the Haven?

                      1st Heaven met Adam (AS)

                      2nd Heaven met Isa (AS) and Yahya (AS)

                      3rd Heaven met Yusuf (AS)

                      4th Heaven met Idris (AS)

                      5th Heaven met Haroon (AS)

                      6th Heaven met Musa (AS)

                      7th Heaven met Ibrahim (AS)

                      460

                      Hazrat Haroon (A.S) met with Holy Prophet ﷺ on the ______ heaven?

                      Correct Answer: 5th
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Who met with Holy Prophet PBUH on the Haven?

                      1st Heaven met Adam (AS)

                      2nd Heaven met Isa (AS) and Yahya (AS)

                      3rd Heaven met Yusuf (AS)

                      4th Heaven met Idris (AS)

                      5th Heaven met Haroon (AS)

                      6th Heaven met Musa (AS)

                      7th Heaven met Ibrahim (AS)


                      • Hazrat Musa (Kaleemullah) and Hazrat Haroon (A.S) were brothers and prophets of God.
                        • Hazrat Musa (A.S) is buried in Ohud, KSA.
                        • Hazrat Haroon (A.S) is buried in Jordan.
                        • Hazrat Haroon (A.S) met the Holy Prophet ﷺ on the 5th heaven.
                        • Zubaida, wife of Haroon ur Rasheed.
                        • She was the mother of Al-Ameen and Al-Haroon.

                        461

                        Hazrat Ibraheem (A.S) met with Holy Prophet ﷺ on the ________ heaven?

                        Correct Answer: 7th
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Who met with Holy Prophet PBUH on the Haven?

                        1st Heaven met Adam (AS)

                        2nd Heaven met Isa (AS) and Yahya (AS)

                        3rd Heaven met Yusuf (AS)

                        4th Heaven met Idris (AS)

                        5th Heaven met Haroon (AS)

                        6th Heaven met Musa (AS)

                        7th Heaven met Ibrahim (AS)

                        ND14-7-2023

                        462

                        In Hajj, stoning the Devil, is called ____ ?

                        Correct Answer: Ramee
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                          • In Hajj, the ritual of stoning the devil is called Ramy al-Jamarat
                          • It involves throwing pebbles at three pillars (or walls) in Mina
                          • During the last stages of Hajj (10th-12th/13th of Dhu al-Hijjah).
                          • Requiring at least 49 pebbles.


                           Last updated NA 13-04-2026

                          463

                          In which timings the Namaz is prohibited?

                          Correct Answer: All of these
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          In the following timings, the Namaz is prohibited.

                          1. Sun rising
                          2. Sun setting
                          3. Sun at the mid

                          **

                          Namaz commanded in Quran for 700 times.

                          Salat made compulsory in 10th Hijri.

                          Salat Juma became Farz in Madina.

                          464

                          Muzdalfa is situated ___?

                          Correct Answer: Betwee Mina and Arafat
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                           It lies southeast of Mina, on the route between Mina and the Plain of Arafat.

                          At Muzdalifah, pilgrims gathered pebbles for a symbolic stoning of the devil ritual.

                          Seven stones are thrown at each of the three pillars to commemorate Ibrahim's rejection of Satan.

                          Maghrib-Isha two prayers are offered together at Muzdalifa on the 9th Zil-ul-Hajj


                          465

                          Zam zam means _____?

                          Correct Answer: To stop
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Zam Zam means "to stop."

                          The Zamzam Well is located within the Masjid al-Haram in Mecca, Saudi Arabia.

                          It 20 meters (66 feet) east of the Kaaba.

                          Millions of pilgrims visit the well each year during Hajj or Umrah to drink its water.
                          466

                          How many Takbeers are in Namaz-e-Janaza?

                          Correct Answer: Four
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • No Sajdas in Salat-e-Janaza.
                          • Namaz-e-Janazah is performed standing in line.
                          • It includes 4 Takbeers.
                          • Namaz-e-Janaza is Farz-e-Kifaya.
                          • The first Namaz-e-Janazah performed by the Holy Prophet ﷺ was for Asad bin Zaraara (RA).
                          467

                          First pillar of Islam is ____?

                          Correct Answer: Shahadatain
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          There are five pillars of Islam:

                          The first pillar of Islam is ____?

                            Shahadatain 

                          The second pillar of Islam is------?

                            Namaz

                          the third fundamental pillar of Islam?

                            Zakat

                          The fourth pillar of Islam is--------?

                           Fasting (Soam)

                          **

                          5th pillar of Islam is?

                            Hajj

                          ****

                          Which pillar of Islam is declared as armor?

                           Fasting (Soam)

                          Which pillar of Islam washes sin like water?

                            Hajj


                          468

                          Who among the Pious Caliphs (Khulfa-e-Rasheen) died a natural death?

                          Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Pious Caliphs were the first four Caliphs.

                          They were:  Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthman, Ali

                          The first Caliph who helped spread Islam was Muhammed's father-in-law, Abu-Bakr.

                          He served for only 2 years, from 632 to 634

                          Hazrat Abubakar (RA) was the first Pious Caliph of Islam.

                          Hazrat Abubakar (RA) became caliph in 632 AD.

                          Hazrat Abubakar (RA) died in 634 AD.

                          The Pious Caliphs were the first four Caliphs.

                          They were:  Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthman, Ali


                          Name (in Arabic)Born (CE)Reigned from (CE)DiedRelationship with MuhammadHouse
                          Abu Bakr (أبو بكر الصديق)5738 June 63222 August 634Father of Aisha,  Muhammad's wifeBanu Taim
                          Umar (عمر بن الخطاب)58423 August 6343 November 644 (assassinated by Abu Lu'lu'a Firuz)Father of Hafsa,  Muhammad's wifeBanu Adi
                          Uthman (عثمان بن عفان)57911 November 64420 June 656 (assassinated at the end of a siege upon his house; see First Fitna)Husband of Muhammad's daughters, Ruqayya and later Umm Kulthum, and grandson of Muhammad's paternal auntBanu Ummaya
                          Ali (علي بن أبي طالب)60120 June 656 29 January 661    (assassinated while praying in the Mosque of Kufa; see First Fitna)Muhammad's cousin, and husband of Fatimah, Muhammad's daughter and Umamah bint Zainab, Muhammad's granddaughterBanu Hashim

                          ND5-5-2023


                          469

                          Pious-Caliphate lasted for about:__________ Years?

                          Correct Answer: 30 years
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Pious Caliphs were the first four Caliphs.

                          They were:  Abu Bakr  Umar  Uthman  Ali

                          RK20.5.2023

                          Name (in Arabic) Born (CE) Reigned from (CE) Died Relationship with Muhammad House
                          Abu Bakr (أبو بكر الصديق) 573 8 June 632 22 August 634 Father of Aisha,  Muhammad's wife Banu Taim
                          Umar (عمر بن الخطاب) 584 23 August 634 3 November 644 (assassinated by Abu Lu'lu'a Firuz) Father of Hafsa,  Muhammad's wife Banu Adi
                          Uthman (عثمان بن عفان) 579 11 November 644 20 June 656 (assassinated at the end of a siege upon his house; see First Fitna) Husband of Muhammad's daughters, Ruqayya and later Umm Kulthum, and grandson of Muhammad's paternal aunt Banu Ummaya
                          Ali (علي بن أبي طالب) 601 20 June 656  29 January 661    (assassinated while praying in the Mosque of Kufa; see First Fitna) Muhammad's cousin, and husband of Fatimah, Muhammad's daughter and Umamah bint Zainab, Muhammad's granddaughter Banu Hashim
                          470

                          "INFAQ" means ____?

                          Correct Answer: Spending wealth for the cause of Islam
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Infāq s an Arabic word meaning spending or disbursement.

                          but also carries the sense of doing so simply to please God without asking for any favor or hoping for a return.

                          The word ʾinfāq is mentioned once in the Qurʾān in Q17:100

                          The imperative form of the verb (ʾanfiqū) (أَنفِقُوا) appears 18 times in the Qurʾān

                          Infāq is different from zakat, which is obligatory on Muslims, or sadaqah

                          ND28-4-2023



                          471

                          Ghazwa Ahzab is also called _____?

                          Correct Answer: Battle of Trench
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Battle of Khandaq is also known as the Battle of the Trench and the Battle of the Confederates.

                          It was a 27-day-long defense by Muslims of Yasrab (now Medina). 

                          Took place in Shawwal – Dhu al-Qi'dah, 5 A.H (627 C.E).



                          Last updated by NA on 22-April-2026

                          472

                          When was the first battle fought by Muslims?

                          Correct Answer: 2 A.H.
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • "Badr" means "full moon" in Arabic.
                          • The Battle of Badr, also called Furqan.
                          • The Battle of Badr is also known as The Day of the Criterion.
                          • Day of the Criterion means کسوٹی کا دن
                          • It took place on Tuesday, 13 March 624 CE (17 Ramadan, 2 AH) near the city of Badr.
                          • Prophet Muhammad SAW, commanding his Sahaba, defeated the Quraysh army led by Abu Jahl (Amr ibn Hishām).
                          • The Kufar forces numbered 1,000.
                          • It was the first battle of Islam, with 313-317 Muslims.
                          • The Muslim forces included 82-85 Muhajirun and 231 Ansar (61 from Aws and 170 from Khazraj).
                          • They had 2 horses and 70 camels.
                          • Fourteen Muslims were killed, while 70 Kufar were killed and 70 captured.
                          • Surah Al-Anfal mentions that Allah promised to reinforce the Muslims with 1,000 angels.
                          473

                          Treaty of Hudaybiyyah was concluded in which Hijri year?

                          Correct Answer: 6
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Al-Hudaibiyah Treaty was scribed by Ali Al-Murtaza (R.A).

                          The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah was an event that took place during the time of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

                          It was a pivotal treaty between Muhammad, representing the state of Medina, and the Qurayshi tribe of Mecca 

                          Sulah e Hudaibiya is called Fatah Al-Mubeen in Surah Al-Fatah

                          Sulah e Hudaibiya Was Signed In The Year 628/ 6H.

                          Sule e Huabaibiya Was Signed For 10 Years.

                          The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah took place in March 628 Dhu al-Qi’dah

                          7 clauses did the treaty of Hudaybiyyah contain

                          Hudabia is the name of the tree.

                          Hazrat Muhammadﷺ, representing the state of Medina

                          Suhail bin Amar signed the Treaty of Hudaibiya on behalf of the Quraish Mecca.

                          ND-21-5-2023


                          474

                          Which surah is known as the Heart of Holy Quran?

                          Correct Answer: Surah Yaseen
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Surah Yaseen is known as the "Heart of the Holy Quran."

                          It is the 36th chapter of the Quran.

                          It contains 83 verses.

                          It is regarded as an earlier Meccan surah.

                          Additional information:

                          Surah Ar-Rahman is known as "Aroos ul Quran" (the Bride of the Quran).

                          Other notable surahs include:

                          Al-Fatihah (the Opening)

                          Al-Baqarah (the Cow)

                          Aali Imran (the Family of Imran)

                          An-Nisa (The Women)

                          Al-Maidah (the Table)

                          475

                          Ayat-ul-Kursi is verse number in Surah Baqrah is _____?

                          Correct Answer: 255
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Ayatul Kursi is the 255th verse of the 2nd surah of the Quran, Al-Baqarah.

                          It is located in the third para named "تِلْكَ ٱلْرُّسُلُ."

                          It is also known as Sardar ul Ayat or The Throne Verse.

                          Surah Al-Baqarah is the longest Surah of the Qur'an.

                          It revealed after the Prophet ﷺ migrated to Madinah.
                          476

                          Which surah is known to as Uroos ul Quran?

                          Correct Answer: Surah Rehman
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Surah Ar Rehman is called Uroos ul Quran.

                          It's the 55th Surah of the Holy Quran which has 78 verses in 3 Ruku. 

                          'Aroos-ul-Qur'an (the Bride of the Qur'an)

                          Surah Yusuf is called the father of the Quran.

                          Surah Yaseen is known as the heart of Quran.

                          List of Surah with nickname

                          1. Al-Fatihah (The Opening)
                          2. Al-Baqarah (The Cow)
                          3. Al-Imran (The Family of Imran)
                          4. An-Nisa' (The Women)
                          5. Al-Ma'idah (The Table Spread)
                          6. Al-An'am (The Cattle)
                          7. Al-A'raf (The Heights)
                          8. Al-Anfal (The Spoils of War)
                          9. At-Tawbah (The Repentance)
                          10. Yunus (Jonah)
                          11. Hud (Hud)
                          12. Yusuf (Joseph)
                          13. Ar-Ra'd (The Thunder)
                          14. Ibrahim (Abraham)
                          15. Al-Hijr (The Rocky Tract)
                          16. An-Nahl (The Bee)
                          17. Al-Isra' (The Night Journey)
                          18. Al-Kahf (The Cave)
                          19. Maryam (Mary)
                          20. Ta-Ha (Ta-Ha)
                          21. Al-Anbiya' (The Prophets)
                          22. Al-Hajj (The Pilgrimage)
                          23. Al-Mu'minun (The Believers)
                          24. An-Nur (The Light)
                          25. Al-Furqan (The Criterion)
                          26. Ash-Shu'ara' (The Poets)
                          27. An-Naml (The Ant)
                          28. Al-Qasas (The Stories)
                          29. Al-Ankabut (The Spider)
                          30. Ar-Rum (The Romans)
                          31. Luqman (Luqman)
                          32. As-Sajda (The Prostration)
                          33. Al-Ahzab (The Confederates)
                          34. Saba' (Sheba)
                          35. Fatir (The Originator)
                          36. Ya-Sin (Ya-Sin)
                          37. As-Saffat (Those who set the Ranks)
                          38. Sad (Sad)
                          39. Az-Zumar (The Troops)
                          40. Ghafir (The Forgiver)
                          41. Fussilat (Explained in Detail)
                          42. Ash-Shura (The Consultation)
                          43. Az-Zukhruf (The Gold Adornments)
                          44. Ad-Dukhan (The Smoke)
                          45. Al-Jathiyah (The Kneeling)
                          46. Al-Ahqaf (The Sand Dunes)
                          47. Muhammad (Muhammad)
                          48. Al-Fath (The Victory)
                          49. Al-Hujurat (The Rooms)
                          50. Qaf (Qaf)
                          51. Adh-Dhariyat (The Winnowing Winds)
                          52. At-Tur (The Mount)
                          53. An-Najm (The Star)
                          54. Al-Qamar (The Moon)
                          55. Ar-Rahman (The Most Gracious)
                          56. Al-Waqi'ah (The Inevitable)
                          57. Al-Hadid (The Iron)
                          58. Al-Mujadila (The Pleading Woman)
                          59. Al-Hashr (The Exile)
                          60. Al-Mumtahanah (The Woman to be Examined)
                          61. As-Saff (The Ranks)
                          62. Al-Jumu'ah (Friday)
                          63. Al-Munafiqun (The Hypocrites)
                          64. At-Taghabun (The Mutual Disillusion)
                          65. At-Talaq (The Divorce)
                          66. At-Tahrim (The Prohibition)
                          67. Al-Mulk (The Sovereignty)
                          68. Al-Qalam (The Pen)
                          69. Al-Haqqah (The Reality)
                          70. Al-Ma'arij (The Ascending Stairways)
                          71. Nuh (Noah)
                          72. Al-Jinn (The Jinn)
                          73. Al-Muzzammil (The Enshrouded One)
                          74. Al-Muddathir (The Cloaked One)
                          75. Al-Qiyamah (The Resurrection)
                          76. Al-Insan (The Man)
                          77. Al-Mursalat (The Emissaries)
                          78. An-Naba' (The Tidings)
                          79. An-Nazi'at (Those who drag forth)
                          80. Abasa (He frowned)
                          81. At-Takwir (The Overthrowing)
                          82. Al-Infitar (The Cleaving)
                          83. Al-Mutaffifin (Defrauding)
                          84. Al-Inshiqaq (The Splitting Open)
                          85. Al-Buruj (The Mansions of the Stars)
                          86. At-Tariq (The Morning Star)
                          87. Al-A'la (The Most High)
                          88. Al-Ghashiyah (The Overwhelming)
                          89. Al-Fajr (The Dawn)
                          90. Al-Balad (The City)
                          91. Ash-Shams (The Sun)
                          92. Al-Lail (The Night)
                          93. Ad-Duha (The Morning Hours)
                          94. Ash-Sharh (The Relief)
                          95. At-Tin (The Fig)
                          96. Al-'Alaq (The Clot)
                          97. Al-Qadr (The Power)
                          98. Al-Bayyinah (The Clear Proof)
                          99. Az-Zalzalah (The Earthquake)
                          100. Al-'Adiyat (The Chargers)
                          101. Al-Qari'ah (The Striking Hour)
                          102. At-Takathur (The Piling Up)
                          103. Al-'Asr (The Time)
                          104. Al-Humazah (The Slanderer)
                          105. Al-Fil (The Elephant)
                          106. Quraysh (Quraysh)
                          107. Al-Ma'un (The Small Kindnesses)
                          108. Al-Kawthar (The Abundance)
                          109. Al-Kafirun (The Disbelievers)
                          110. An-Nasr (The Help)
                          111. Al-Masad (The Palm Fiber)
                          112. Al-Ikhlas (The Sincerity)
                          113. Al-Falaq (The Daybreak)
                          114. An-Nas (The Mankind)
                          477

                          What is the name of religious book of Muslims?

                          Correct Answer: The Quran
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Quran is the holy book of Muslims.

                          It is believed to be the final revelation delivered to Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

                          The Quran was revealed to Prophet Muhammad SAW.

                          The Injeel (Gospel) was revealed to Prophet Isa (Jesus).

                          The Torait (Tawrat/Torah) was revealed to Prophet Musa (Moses).

                          The Zuboor (Zabur/Psalms) was revealed to Prophet Dawud (David).

                          478

                          Nafl is a/an _______ practice of worship.

                          Correct Answer: Optional
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Nafl refers to voluntary or optional acts of worship in Islam.

                          They are not compulsory, but bring extra reward when performed.

                          479

                          Who is known by the title Umm Al-Arab (ام العرب)?

                          Correct Answer: Hazrat Hajrah (حضرت حاجرہ)
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          ام العرب (عربوں کی ماں) کا خطاب حضرت ہاجرہ علیہا السلام کو دیا جاتا ہے

                          یہ خطاب انہیں حضرت ابراہیم علیہ السلام کی زوجہ اور حضرت اسماعیل علیہ السلام کی والدہ ہونے کی وجہ سے دیا گیا ہے، جو تمام عرب قبائل کے جدِ امجد ہیں

                          اہمیت: اس لقب سے ان کی عرب تاریخ میں مرکزی حیثیت کا اندازہ ہوتا ہے. 

                          Don't be confused about two named 

                          Abu Al-Arab (ابوالعرب) — Prophet Ismail (حضرت اسماعیل)
                          Umm Al-Arab (ام العرب) — Hazrat Hajrah (حضرت حاجرہ) 
                          480

                          Who is known by the title Umm Al-Anbiya (ام الانبیاء)?

                          Correct Answer: Hazrat Sarah (حضرت سارہ)
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          حضرت سارہؑ، حضرت ابراہیمؑ کی اہلیہ اور حضرت اسحاقؑ کی والدہ تھیں،

                          جنہیں اسلامی روایات میں "ام الانبیاء" (پیغمبروں کی ماں) کہا جاتا ہے

                          کیونکہ ان کی اولاد سے ایک پیغمبر یعنی حضرت یعقوبؑ کی نسل سے بہت سے انبیاء مبعوث ہوئے۔ 

                          اہمیت: حضرت سارہؑ کو اسلامی تاریخ میں "ام الانبیاء" کے لقب سے یاد کیا جاتا ہے کیونکہ ان کی نسل سے بہت سے انبیاء مبعوث ہوئے۔ 



                          Don't be confused about two named 

                          ابوالانبیاء (ابوالانبیاء): حضرت ابراہیمؑ

                          ام الانبیاء (ام الانبیاء): حضرت سارہؑ

                          481

                          Who is known by the title Umm Al-Bashar (ام البشر)?

                          Correct Answer: Hazrat Hawwa (حضرت حواء)
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          حضرت حواؑ کو "ام البشر" کہا جاتا ہے کیونکہ وہ تمام انسانوں کی ماں ہیں

                          حضرت آدمؑ کی بیوی ہیں. ان کا نام عربی میں "سرخ مائل" کے معنی دیتا ہے

                          نام کے معنی: "حوا" کا لفظی مطلب "سرخ مائل" ہے، اور ان کے ہونٹوں کی سفیدی کی وجہ سے یہ نام رکھا گیا ہے. 
                          Don't be confused about two named 
                          Abu Al-Bashar (ابوالبشر) — Prophet Adam (حضرت آدم)  
                           Umm Al-Bashar (ام البشر) — Hazrat Hawwa (حضرت حواء)


                          482

                          Which is the best eatable thing mentioned in the Holy Quran.

                          Correct Answer: Honey
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The provided quote is from the Quran, specifically Surah An-Nahl (The Bee), verses 68-69

                          Quran says: "And the Lord inspired the bee, saying: Take your habitations in the mountains and the trees and in what they erect. Then, eat of all fruits and follow the ways of your Lord made easy (for you)'.


                          اور خُداوند نے شہد کی مکھی کو الہام کرتے ہوئے کہا: پہاڑوں اور درختوں میں اور جو وہ کھڑی کرتے ہیں اُن میں اپنی رہائش گاہوں کو لے لو۔ پھر تمام پھلوں میں سے کھاؤ اور اپنے رب کے آسان طریقے پر چلو۔


                          Honey is mentioned two times in the Quran

                          483

                          How many farz Wuzu (ablution) has?

                          Correct Answer: Four
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Wazu (Ablution) was ordered on  5 A.H

                          The acts which break the ablution are called Nawaqiz-e-Wazu

                          There are 6 Sunnah acts of ablution

                          There are 4 obligations for ablution.

                          13 Sunnah are in four obligations of Ablution

                          By Hazrat Jibrail (علیہ), Prophet ﷺ learnt Salat & ablution

                          وضو کے چار فرائض ہیں۔

                          نمبر 1: چہرہ دھونا

                          نمبر 2: دونوں ہاتھوں کو کہینیوں تک دھونا

                          نمبر 3: چوتھائی سر کا مسح کرنا

                          نمبر 4:پاوں کا ٹخنیوں سمیت دھونا

                          ****


                          484

                          Which is the second largest Religion in the World?

                          Correct Answer: Islam
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Islam is the second-largest religion in the World


                          List of World's largest Religions.

                          1.    Christianity (31.2%)

                          2.    Islam (24.1%)

                          3.    No religion (16%)

                          4.    Hinduism (15.1%)

                          5.    Buddhism (6.9%)

                          6.    Folk religions (5.7%)

                          7.    Other religions (0.5%)

                          8.    Sikhism (0.3%)

                          9.     Judaism (0.2%)

                          485

                          ______ is not one of the Five Pillars of Islam. Prayer five times daily, Witnessing of Shahadah, Jihad, Fasting

                          Correct Answer: Jihad
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          There are five pillars of Islam:

                          The first pillar of Islam is ____?

                            Shahadatain 

                          The second pillar of Islam is------?

                            Namaz

                          the third fundamental pillar of Islam?

                            Zakat

                          The fourth pillar of Islam is--------?

                           Fasting (Soam)

                          **

                          5th pillar of Islam is?

                            Hajj

                          ****

                          Which pillar of Islam is declared as armor?

                           Fasting (Soam)

                          Which pillar of Islam washes sin like water?

                            Hajj

                          486

                          The literal meaning of Jihad?

                          Correct Answer: To Strive Hard
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • The literal meaning of jihad is "striving," "struggling," or "exerting utmost effort."
                          • Jihad becomes first in.2AH
                          • 41 times the word jihad appears in the Quran

                           Last updated 12 april 2026

                          487

                          What did the Holy Prophet (SAW) abolish in Khutba Hujjat-ul-Wida?

                          Correct Answer: Blood feuds
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          In the Farewell Sermon, the Prophet (SAW) declared all blood feuds of the Jahiliyyah abolished.

                          This ended the cycle of tribal revenge and promoted peace and justice.

                          488

                          Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) at his last sermon ended which practice?

                          Correct Answer: Blood feuds
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                           Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) abolished all pre-Islamic practices of usury (interest) and blood feuds/revenge killings

                          • The Prophet (PBUH) delivered his final sermon during the Hajj on Mount Arafat.
                          • It is also known as Farewell Sermon.
                          • The Holy Prophet (SAW) performed Hajjat-ul-Wida in the 10th Hijri.
                          • It was performed on Friday, the 9th of Dhu al-Hijjah (March 6, 632 A.D).
                          • This final pilgrimage included his renowned Farewell Sermon.
                          489

                          Which type of Hadith has numerous narrators at each level of the chain, making it impossible to be fabricated?

                          Correct Answer: Mutawatir Hadith
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          A Mutawatir Hadith is narrated by so many people at each stage that it is inconceivable they all agreed to lie.

                          This ensures its authenticity.

                          490

                          A Hadith classified by the sequence of narrators is known as its?

                          Correct Answer: Sanad
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Sanad (chain of narrators) refers to the sequence of people who transmitted the Hadith from the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) to the compiler.

                          The authenticity of a Hadith is verified by examining the reliability and continuity of its Sanad, along with the text (Matn).

                          491

                          What is term for the hadith narrated by a single reliable narrator throughout the chain of transmission?

                          Correct Answer: Gharib
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Gharib (غريب) is a term used in hadith studies to describe a hadith that is narrated by a single reliable narrator throughout the chain of transmission.

                          This means that the hadith is narrated by only one person at each level of the isnad (chain of transmission), making it a unique or solitary narration.

                          492

                          Umm-ul-Masakeen (Mother of Poor) was the title given to one of the wives of the Prophet (SAW)?

                          Correct Answer: Hazrat Zainab benet Khuzima (RA)
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Umm-ul-Masakeen was the title given to one of the wives of the Prophet (SAW): Hazrat Zainab benet Khuzima (RA)

                          Umm-ul-Masakeen means mother of the poor.

                          Hazrat Zainab benet Khuzima (RA) was the fifth wife of Muhammad

                          Zaynab was the first of Muhammad’s wives who were not from the Quraysh tribe.
                           
                          Zaynab's marriage to Muhammad lasted only a few months.

                          She died at the age of about thirty.

                          Muhammad carried her to Jannat al-Baqi

                          493

                          What is the khitab (title) of Hazrat Umar (RA)?

                          Correct Answer: Al-Farooq
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Hazrat Umar (RA) was titled Al-Farooq, meaning the one who distinguishes between right and wrong.

                          This title reflects his justice, strength, and commitment to truth.

                          494

                          The wife of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) who was the mother of his son Ibrahim was ______?

                          Correct Answer: Maria Qibtiyya (RA)
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Maria Qibtiyya (RA) was gifted to the Prophet and gave birth to his son Ibrahim (RA).

                          Ibrahim (RA) passed away in infancy, but his birth is an important part of the Prophet’s life history.

                          495

                          Bait-e- Rizwan was made to take the revenge of

                          Correct Answer: Hazrat Usman (RA)
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Bait-e-Rizwan took place in 6th Hijrah.

                          Bait Rizwan was the pledge taken to get revenge of Hazrat Usman (RA) under the tree known as Rizwan

                          Bait-e-Rizwan is also known as Bait-e-Shajra made under the Keekar tree.

                          ****


                          496

                          What was the leader of the Abbasid Caliphate called?

                          Correct Answer: Caliph
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The first Abbasid caliph, al-Saffāḥ , ordered the elimination of the entire Umayyad clan;

                          The only Umayyad of note who escaped was ʿAbd al-Raḥman, who made his way to Spain and established an Umayyad dynasty that lasted until 1031.

                          __________________________

                          Abbasid caliphate, second of the two great dynasties of the Muslim empire of the caliphate.

                          It overthrew the Umayyad caliphate in 750 ce and reigned as the Abbasid caliphate until it was destroyed by the Mongol invasion in 1258.

                          *********

                          Remember Tips

                          یاد کرنے کے لیے

                          1. Rashidin -30 years
                          2. Umayyad -100 years
                          3. Abbasid - 500 years
                          4. Ottoman Empire - 600 years


                          497

                          What city was the capital city of the Umayyad Caliphate?

                          Correct Answer: Damascus
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Capitals: Damascus, Harran

                          Government: Monarchy

                          Currencies: Dirham, Gold dinar, Fals

                          ______________________________________

                          The Umayyad Caliphate  was the second of the four major caliphates established after the death of Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)

                          498

                          Which was the first person who informed Hazrat Ayesha (RA) about the eveent of Afak?

                          Correct Answer: Hazrat Ummay Mustah bin Asasah (RA)
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The first person who informed Hazrat Ayesha (RA) about the slander (known as the Event of Ifk) was Umm Mistah bint Abi Ruhm.

                          incident occurred during a period when Hazrat Ayesha (RA) was left behind on an expedition and later accused of infidelity.







                          499

                          The Hadith book in which all the Hadith reported by a particular companion are compiled is called:

                          Correct Answer: Mustadrak
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • The Hadith book in which all the Hadith reported by a particular companion are compiled is called Mustadrak
                          • **
                          • Al-Mustadrak 'ala al-Sahihayn كم) is a five-volume hadith collection written by Hakim al-Nishapuri (Nishapur is located in Iran).
                          • He wrote it in the year AH 393 (1002–1003 CE), when he was 72 years old. 
                          • Mustadraks have been authored for various subjects such as Jurisprudence, Rijal, Hadith etc
                          500

                          What was the name of the wife of Pharaoh who believed in Prophet Musa (AS)?

                          Correct Answer: Asiya
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Asiya, the wife of Pharaoh, is honored in Islam for her strong faith in Allah despite her husband's tyranny.

                          She protected baby Musa (AS).

                          She is considered one of the four greatest women in Islam.

                          Nice work getting this far. Consistency beats intensity — come back tomorrow and keep stacking sets.
